articles.name: Salesforce AEP Component App Integrations Overview
articles.title: Salesforce AEP Component App Integrations Overview
articles.body:
This article centralizes all integrations information regarding Salesforce Account Engagement Profile (AEP) Component App, including benefits, FAQs, and setup instructions.

  • Things to Know
  • Timing & Backfills
  • FAQs
  • Prerequisites
  • Installation
  • Permissions & Page Layouts
  • ON24 Connector Settings

Things to Know

Note: Salesforce Sales Cloud and ON24 Advanced Analytics is required for this integration.

The ON24 Salesforce App, built on the Lightning framework, brings powerful engagement insights directly into your CRM. With this integration, you can embed the ON24 Account Engagement Profile (AEP) as a Lightning component on Salesforce Account records—giving your sales and marketing teams a unified view of how each account interacts with your ON24 experiences.

Domains from ON24 engagement data automatically map to Salesforce Accounts via the Website field, eliminating manual work and streamlining workflows. The AEP delivers rich account-level insights—such as attendee activity, content consumption, and lead behavior—right where your teams need them most.

By pulling ON24 engagement data, matching it to the correct company in Salesforce, and displaying it directly on the Account record, this integration empowers your team to act faster, personalize outreach, and convert engagement into revenue.

 

Timing & Backfills

Implementation Timing

To install the Salesforce app and perform a backfill to populate the AEP component will take 30 min to an hour. However, the timing on that will vary according to your resources’ availability.

 

Backfills

You can run a backfill in the Salesforce AEP Component App by following the AEP backfill instructions below.

  • Log into Salesforce
  • Press the 9 dot icon
  • Select ON24 Connector
  • Go to Settings.
  • You should see an image similar to the one below.
  • Fill in the Synchronize Historical Data section
  • Backfills can be ran in batches of 3 months, and can go back up to 2 years

If asked to re-enter the Access Token, Secret Token, and Client Id, then obtain that information from your API Dashboard. Your Client id is unique to your account. 

FAQs

Who can use the Salesforce AEP app?

Any customer who uses Salesforce Sales Cloud.

 

Does the Salesforce AEP Component App solution support customers using the ON24 EU data center?

Yes

 

Do we need to create any custom fields for prior to installing the AEP Component App?

No, installing the app will create all components offered for you.

 

Why am I seeing an error within the AEP visualforce page?

  • If you are experiencing errors in the AEP visualforce page, this could mean a number of things.  First check that the API Access Token is still active and the individual's Platform User in ON24 Elite is still active.  The API Access Token is linked to the email address of the Platform User who create the token, so when their user is removed, the API Access Token becomes invalid.
  • If the Access Token is still valid, Contact Support to open a ticket.

Prerequisites

Salesforce Lightning and API Credentials 

The ON24 Connector App was created for use with Salesforce Lightning. The App uses the ON24 REST API and requires ON24 Connect to generate API credentials for the App to request data from ON24. 

ON24 Advanced Analytics is required for this integration.

 

Salesforce Admin Tasks

  1. Install App as a Salesforce admin
  2. Assign permissions to appropriate profiles for:
    a. Configuration of the App Settings for adding ON24 API credentials
    b. View installed reports
    c. View installed dashboard
    d. View new custom fields on the Account Page
  3. Adjust page layouts to see ON24 specific fields for Accounts

Installation

Obtain and Load the Installation URL 

  1. Log in to Salesforce as the appropriate Admin that will own the new App.
  2. Once you are logged in, click on the installation URL.
    • Current version 1.42
    • https://login.salesforce.com/packaging/installPackage.apexp?p0=04tDn000000GEVs  
    • password: connectionON24

 

Choose the Installation Options 

For the question What if existing component names conflict with ones in this package?, select the option Do not install.  Uninstall any previous versions, then continue with the installation. 

Select Install for All Users then click the Install button.

 

 

When you are prompted to approve third-party access, select the option Yes, grant access to these third-party websites then click the Continue button.

You should see the following screen: 

 
You may get the following message. Click Done and wait until the package is installed.  When the package is ready you will receive a notification via email.

Note: Emails may take up to 30 minutes.

 

To view the installed package, go to the apps section and search for ON24 Connector. 

 

Permissions & Page Layouts

App Permissions 

Assign the appropriate app permissions for users. Go to Permission Sets to configure the ON24 permissions. 

  • ON24 Connector Admin - Assign to user(s) who will be configuring the app settings, adding ON24 API credentials, and viewing logs.
  • ON24 Connector User - Assign users for viewing the embedded AEP app component and the ON24 fields on the Account page.

 

 

Updating Contact and Lead Page Layouts 

Viewing the embedded Account Engagement Profile page in the Account page requires updating page layouts for Salesforce users. There is a ON24AEP iFrame Lightning component that can be added to the Details tab, or you can create a new tab called ON24 AEP and add the Lighting component. 

The new custom field is also included with the app and can be added to the Account page:

  • ON24 Account Engagement Profile URL (ON24CN__AccountEngagementProfileURL__c)

 

ON24 Connector Settings

Provision API Credentials 

The connector requires API credentials associated with an ON24 user from your workspace. Please see https://apidoc.on24.com/obtain-api-access-tokens for instructions on how to generate API tokens.
 

Configuring Connector 

To enable the service, configure the following: 

  • Add API credentials:
  • Access Token
  • Secret Token
  • Client Id 

Select the location for your ON24 Workspace Data Center.  Choose if you want the service to create new Lead.

Choose if you want to receive notifications after each run of the service.

Start Recurring Job.  Select the frequency that you want the integration to run and click Start Recurring Job to enable the service.

Once the Recurring Job is started an automatic backfill from the past 30 days will occur.

articles.name: Accessing and leveraging your AI Content
articles.title: Accessing and leveraging your AI Content
articles.body:
ON24’s uses AI to help marketers repurpose digital experiences into various content types like blog posts, ebooks, FAQs, and social media posts. Content can be downloaded, edited, and published across platforms, including ON24’s Engagement Hub and social media. Nurture Pages allow structured display of articles, videos, and podcasts, enabling continuous engagement and lead generation.

 

  • Accessing your AI content
  • Using AI-Generated
  • Nurture Pages

Accessing AI-Generated Content

Where can you find your AI content?
Each AI-generated asset is accessible in your  Media Manager.
Easily filter to find AI generated content by looking for the created by column and the blue lightning bolt icon!

All article content types below can be downloaded as editable Word documents:

  • Blog Post
  • Ebook
  • FAQ
  • Key Takeaway
  • Follow-Up Email
  • Social Media Post
  • Transcript

To download, preview, or edit:

  • Click the three-dot menu next to the content item.
  • Select Download, Preview, and Edit based on your needs.

Video or Audio based content can be downloaded as media files

  • Key Moments Video Clips (technically not AI content but AI created but usable in the same ways!)
  • Podcasts

Publish Options

Some content types can be published directly to ON24’s Engagement Hub or external platforms. :

  • Podcast
    • Publish to Engagement Hub
  • Key Moment Video
    • Publish to Engagement Hub, YouTube, LinkedIn, Facebook, X (formerly Twitter)
      (videos must be published to YouTube before the other publishing options are available)

Using AI-Generated Content Effectively

Here’s how each content type can be used, with practical examples:

Blog Post

Use Case: Extend thought leadership and drive organic traffic.
Example: Turn a webinar on “2025 Marketing Trends” into a blog post summarizing key insights. Share it on your website and LinkedIn to attract new leads.

Ebook

Use Case: Create gated content for lead generation.
Example: Compile insights from multiple webinars into an ebook titled “The Future of Digital Engagement”. Offer it as a downloadable resource in exchange for email sign-ups.

FAQ

Use Case: Address common questions from your audience.
Example: After a product launch webinar, publish an FAQ answering attendee questions. Add it to your help center or include it in follow-up emails.

Key Takeaway

Use Case: Highlight the most impactful insights.
Example: Share a one-pager summarizing the top 5 takeaways from your event. Use it in sales enablement or as a teaser in email campaigns.

Follow-Up Email

Use Case: Re-engage attendees and nurture leads.
Example: Send a personalized email to attendees with a thank-you note, key takeaways, and links to additional resources through ON24 or your own email [latform or CRM.

Social Media Post

Use Case: Promote your content and drive engagement.
Example: Use AI-generated posts to share quotes or stats from your webinar on LinkedIn and X. Include a link to the full recording or blog post.

Transcript

Use Case: Improve accessibility and SEO.
Example: Add the transcript of your webinar to your blog or resource center. Search engines can index the content, boosting visibility.

Podcast

Use Case: Repurpose audio content for a broader audience.
Example: Publish the audio from your webinar as a podcast episode on your Engagement Hub. Embed it on nurture pages or share via email.

Key Moment Video

Use Case: Capture and share the most engaging moments.
Example:  Share highlights from your keynote speaker and publish it to YouTube and LinkedIn. Use it to drive traffic back to the full event.

Using Content on Nurture Pages

ON24 Nurture Pages allow you to showcase AI-generated content in a structured format:

  • Articles appear as basic HTML text pages.
  • Videos are embedded and playable directly.
  • Podcasts are embedded as playable audio files.

Example: Create a nurture page titled “Your Digital Engagement Toolkit” featuring:

  • A blog post on engagement strategies
  • A video highlight from your last event
  • An embedded podcast episode
  • A downloadable ebook

 

ON24’s AI-generated content tools make it easy to repurpose and amplify your digital experiences. By downloading, editing, and publishing strategically, you can create a continuous stream of content that educates, engages, and converts.

articles.name: Embedding an Event in a 3rd Party Webpage
articles.title: Embedding an Event in a 3rd Party Webpage
articles.body:
ON24 offers an option to embed your events into your own site if needed. While we recommend using the full-page Webcast Elite console for the best experience, this article can help you understand your options and reach out if this is the right fit for your ON24 events.

Note:  Embedding an ON24 event into a third-party website is available for those who need it for alignment with existing ecosystems, though the full-page experience remains ON24's recommended approach for optimal performance and audience engagement.  A ticket to Support is needed to discuss/enable this feature.

  • Considerations
  • Embed Setup
  • Embed Type
  • Other Options
  • Why use the ON24 Console?

Considerations

The ON24 Webcast Elite console is purpose-built to deliver a seamless, engaging, and branded experience across all devices and audiences.  Using the Webcast Elite Console, or Embedding our Engagement Hub solution is the suggested for all ON24 customers. These solutions are fully tested, and supported by the ON24 team. ON24 recommends to all customers using the Full-Page Browser Experience designed specifically for events. 

If third party is embedding is required, the preferred embedding method is to use the the ON24 Engagement Hub—which aggregates and curates webinar content—is a scalable, low-maintenance solution:

  • All updates are centralized
  • Content changes propagate automatically
  • You preserve the ON24 experience and simplify your internal operation

However, for those who need an embedding solution outside of the ON24 ecosystem or the Engagement Hub, event embedding options are available.

  • For 3rd party embedding without the Engagement Hub, the suggested method is to embed the full ON24 console via iFrame, maintaining all event capabilities, integrity, and allowing for the most robust support.
  • You can embed a single event, or every event in your workspace.  (The Support team will ask you to detail your use-case on this point to help you determine which is right for you.)

Note: The embedding option is a new and evolving feature. Currently there are known issues to consider before implementing any solution.

  • Size of the embed area can have negative effects on consoles with responsive behavior on. Tools might be too small or hidden when embed area is too small.
  • Returning registrants are not remembered in Mac or iOS devices. The Registration page will be presented every time when someone visits the page.
  • For iOS attendees, End of Event survey responses do not save.
  • The SSO use case has not been tested and is not currently supported.

 

Embed Setup

If embedding is decided upon, reach out to ON24 Support to enable the availability in your account. Once embedding is enabled in your account, you'll need to set up the predetermined domains in the account, and then create the event before you are able to embed the event on your domain.

From your account settings add the domain(s) where this event can be embedded.  Separate multiple domains by commas (without spaces) and use asterisks as a wildcard, to allow all subdomains. Once supported domains are set up the 'Link and Embed' button will become available.

Note: Manage User permissions is required to set or add domains.

 Two embed codes will be available via the 'Link and Embed' button on your events 'Overview' or 'Registration' page.

Copy the code that works best for your specific use case and implement on your site.

Embed URL Options

Within the embed code section you will find two options, "Webinar in Website" and "Webinar in WordPress Site". Select the best option based on your use case.

Webinar in Website

This option allows you to embed your full Registration page and Event Console.

Depending on your sites style and setup you may wish to adjust or add styling for height and width of the iframe.

 

Webinar in WordPress Site

This option allows you to embed your full Registration page and Event Console in your WordPress site via Supported Shortcode

Install and activate the ON24 Webcast Embed Plugin to use this option.

Use the supplied short code in a short code block.

Adjust the width and height as needed to match your WordPress content.

Additional Options

If using Seamless Registration for your embedded event, additional parameters must be added.

  • When data is passed seamlessly, viewers will skip the ON24 registration page but the rest of the workflow will be the same.
  • The event id, key & email are required parameters.
    • If an email is not available, a unique identifier can be passed in place of that.
  • You must use HTTP POST method: https://event.on24.com/utilApp/r for the Seamless URL.
    • HTTP GET https://event.on24.com/interface/registration/autoreg/index.html will fail.
  • You can also use Unified Seamless Registration - https://seamlessreg.on24.com or https://seamlessreg.eu.on24.com
    • Find more information here.

 

 

Why ON24 Recommends Full-Page Browser Experience

ON24 is Designed as a Full-Browser, Immersive Application

  • The ON24 console operates as a robust single-page application optimized for modern browsers and screens.
  • This architecture ensures a consistent, stable user experience with fully synchronized media, layouts, and engagement tools.
  • Altering this setup—such as rendering it in limited containers—undermines layout control and responsiveness.

 

Limited Display Area = Reduced Business Impact

  • Smaller render areas reduce how many engagement tools can be displayed, directly impacting:
    • User interaction levels
    • Behavioral signals captured
    • Engagement scoring and lead qualification
    • Fewer visible tools = less participation = slower conversion.
  • Example: When looking at Webcast vs Engagement Hub launch data for leading fortune 100 companies, the average Engagement Score for full browser direct launch is 3.5/4.0 and embedded was 2.3/4.0 (over the last 30 days).

 

Degraded Cross-Device and Cross-Browser Experience

  • When the console is embedded in constrained environments, layout anomalies like scrollbars, overlapping content, or broken responsiveness often emerge—especially on mobile.
  • These inconsistencies can reflect poorly on both ON24 and the customer's brand, even if ON24 is not the root cause.

 

Brand Risk: Loss of Plausible Deniability

  • When the experience is embedded directly into a customer-managed environment, any issue—whether ON24’s fault or not—can be perceived as the customer's fault.
    • Example: If ON24 experiences latency, it’s the host brand that may take the reputational hit.
  • Using the full ON24 console preserves a clear line of ownership and trust.

 

Operational Complexity: Managing Dynamic Event Changes

  • When webinars are independently published into a third-party experience:
    • Any update to metadata (title, time, speaker, etc.) must be propagated downstream.
    • APIs exist to support this, but managing them requires additional effort and infrastructure on the customer’s side.
  • As the recipient site, the burden of ensuring real-time accuracy becomes yours.

 

Diminished Personalization and AI Automation

  • ON24’s Smart Tips, Smart Questions, Engagement Scoring, and post-event nurture automation rely on context from the complete user experience.
  • Restricting the engagement surface reduces data availability, making personalization and AI less effective.

 

Analytics and Attribution Gaps

  • ON24’s analytics model assumes complete visibility into user behavior across all tools.
  • If the experience is constrained, analytics:
    • May become fragmented or unreliable
    • Miss key engagement milestones
    • Under report signals that drive campaign ROI

articles.name: Salesforce Data Cloud
articles.title: Salesforce Data Cloud
articles.body:
The ON24 to Salesforce Data Cloud integration allows you to create Data Lake Object (DLO) records with ON24 Event data. This self-service setup streamlines the transfer of your event metadata into Salesforce, for use with Agentforce and other Data Cloud tools.

NOTE: Currently this feature supports ON24 metadata only.  Registrant and attendee data will be included in future updates.

 

DLO Creation and Schema Management

To get started, you must use the predefined ON24 YML schema for the ON24 Event DLO.  

 

Step 1 - Create the Ingestion API Connector 

Note: You must be logged in as a System Administrator to complete these steps.

  1. In Data Cloud , click Setup, and then select Data Cloud Setup
  2. Type in Ingestion API in the Quick Find search bar, and select Ingestion API when the options displays below
  3. Click New and enter a name for the API source
  4. Click Save
  5. On the details page for the new connector, click Upload Schema and navigate to the location of the on24event.yml file
  6. Select the on24event.yml file and click Open
  7. Click Save

The connector page reflects the status as Needs Data Stream. The status changes to In Use when a data stream is created and is active with the connector.

Find more information from Salesforce here.

 

Step 2 - Create the Ingestion API Data Stream

Note: Make sure that your admin has set up the Ingestion API connector and that the schema file is uploaded.

  1. In Data Cloud select Data Streams, or in the App Launcher find, and select Data Streams
  2. In recently viewed data streams, click New
  3. Click Ingestion API
  4. If you have more than one Ingestion API configured, select the one that you want in the dropdown
  5. Select the on24event object, and click Next
  6. In the New Data Stream dialog box, configure these items:
    • Select an object’s category among Profile and Other
    • For Primary Key, select eventid
    • For Record Modified Field, select lastmodified
  7. Click Next
  8. On the summary screen, review the data stream that Data Cloud created
  9. From the Data Space dropdown, select the applicable data space or the default data space
  10. Click Deploy

After the data stream is deployed, you can now set up the Salesforce Data Cloud integration in Elite.

Find more information from Salesforce here.

 

Authentication Setup

Provision API Access Tokens in ON24.  Open Integrations from Platform Tools, click on API Tokens and Provision a New API Access Token.

Make note of the Token Key and Token Secret.

From the Integrations tab, scroll down to ON24 > Salesforce Data Cloud.  

Click Configure.

Click on New Authentication, choose your Salesforce Instance type.

Click Create and login to your Salesforce account via OAuth.  

After successful authentication, enter you ON24 Token Key and Token Secret and click Next.

Select the Ingestion API Connector Information, click Next, choose the API Authentication, and click Next. 

Once done they're validated, click Finish to complete the integration.

To deactivate, use the Disconnect button on the Integrations dashboard.

 

Data Mapping and Scheduled Transfers

Once the DLO is active and the integration is enabled, ON24 will automatically map event metadata to your configured Salesforce structure.

Data Transfer Schedule:

  • Initial Backfill - Pulls events that went live in the past 30 days (dateFilterMode=goodafter).
  • Ongoing Sync - Daily scheduled runs pull events modified in the last 24 hours (dateFilterMode=modified).
  • Manual Backfill Option - Supports a one-time backfill for older or future events if needed.

Transfers are fully automated—no manual pushes required after setup.

articles.name: Key Moments and Nurture Page Service Package
articles.title: Key Moments and Nurture Page Service Package
articles.body:
The Key Moments and Nurture Page Services Package is designed to transform your AI-generated Key Moments into polished videos within a custom branded Nurture Page. This one-time service provides up to 8 hours of expert video editing and design support over a 3-week period, tailored specifically to keep nurturing your audience post-event.

 

 

What’s Included

This one-time service provides up to 8 hours of expert editing and design support. Includes one round of work and one round of revisions in the following four areas:

  • Custom Nurture Page Template: We will help you design a Nurture Page template including navigation, HERO background, and styling. This template can be applied to future pages as well.
  • Key Moments Video Editing: Our expert Video Editing team will edit your AI-generated Key Moment clips and include the following items in each video:
    • Custom logo bumper: We’ll create an animated logo bumper to add to the start and end of each video to add interest and brand awareness to your content.
    • Custom title treatment: We’ll animate and stylize Key Moment video titles to match your professional branding.
  • Nurture Page Implementation: After the template is created and the Key Moment videos are edited, we will arrange and optimize your nurture page to highlight your best content.
  • Nurture Page Email: Finally, we will help you craft one post-event nurture email with embedded media to drive traffic to the page.

 

Why Choose the Key Moments and Nurture Page Services Package?

With our professional video editing and platform expertise, you will quickly have an engaging landing page to keep nurturing your audiences post-event, along with templates to use for future nurture pages.

Note: If purchasing one pack, this service must be utilized within 90 days of purchase. If purchasing two or more packs, the service can be used any time during the contract term.

 

How to Order

To order the Key Moments and Nurture Page Services Package, follow the steps below:

  1. From the Overview page of the event you would like to use a service on, click on the Order Services button.
  2. Choose Key Moments and Nurture Page Services Package.
  3. Click Place Order at the bottom of the page to submit the request.

After submitting, you will receive notifications in your email about your service order, including next steps.

For additional questions or to learn more about the Key Moments and Nurture Page Services Package, please reach out to preprod@on24.com.

articles.name: Salesforce Sales Cloud Integrations Overview
articles.title: Salesforce Sales Cloud Integrations Overview
articles.body:
This article centralizes all Connect information regarding Salesforce Sales Cloud, including benefits, FAQs, and setup instructions.

  • Things to Know
  • Set Up
  • Timing
  • FAQs
  • Registration
  • Legacy Set Up

Things to Know

  • The integration does not create Campaigns, but can update them.
  • The integration automatically adds Campaign Member Status values: Registered, Attended, and Attended On-demand.
  • Clients can add custom fields to the Campaign and Campaign Member objects to store additional ON24 activity.
  • The integration does not update existing Contacts or Leads but will create new Leads, if an existing Contact or Lead is not found, as per the defined mapping.
  • With the create new Leads feature, Company is a required registration field.
  • If you have fuzzy match enabled you will need to allow the integration to bypass that feature.  Instructions are available under the Set Up tab.
  • Backfilling is not available
  • You can connect the integration to a Salesforce sandbox for testing before switching to production.  Ensure field mappings are consistent between environments.
  • The Salesforce Campaign ID must be added to the Campaign Code field on the Overview page of each event.
  • Click here to download a self-service integration flow chart if you'd like.

Set Up

The ON24 self-service integration allows clients to configure and enable synchronization between ON24 and Salesforce directly from the ON24 platform.

This setup supports creating new Leads if a contact or unconverted lead cannot be found.  ON24 maps events to contacts, leads, campaign, and campaign member records.

  • Ensure that custom fields are added to the Salesforce Campaign Member object. These fields will capture ON24 engagement data.  Click on this link for the ON24 API Data Salesforce Self-Serve workbook with all the available fields.
  • You will need to create a dedicated user with ON24 login access to authenticate the connection to Salesforce.
  • If you have fuzzy match enabled, you will need to either exclude the dedicated connection user or alter the fuzzy match to make it harder to match.  See details below.

In your ON24 account, open the Integrations dashboard from Platform Tools and click on API Tokens and click Provision a New API Access Token.  Make note of the Token Key and Token Secret.

Once you provisioned a token, click on the Integrations tab, scroll down to ON24 > Salesforce Sales Cloud and click Configure.

Click on New Authentication.  

Enter your authentication name and choose the Salesforce instance type (Production or Sandbox), then click Create.

Login to Salesforce.  You can use the generic Salesforce login URL, or click Use Custom Domain to log in with your dedicated user credentials.

Note:  This requires a dedicated admin user with Marketing User enabled on the profile.

 

Enter the ON24 API Key and Secret to establish the connection.

Define the mapping between ON24 registration data and Salesforce Lead fields.

Fields are separated into Text Fields (including Picklists), Number Fields, Date/Time Fields, and Boolean fields.

ON24 will display the Lead fields in drop-down list applicable for the field type.

Note: Company, Last Name, and Email are required registration field to create new Leads (this is required by Salesforce).

Set up mapping for ON24 engagement data (e.g., polls, content downloads) to Salesforce Campaign Member fields.

Fields are separated into Text Fields (including Picklists), Number Fields, Date/Time Fields, and Boolean fields.

ON24 will display the Campaign Member fields in drop-down lists applicable for the field type.

Set up mapping for the Event analytics to the Campaign fields.

Fields are separated into Text Fields (including Picklists), Number Fields, Date/Time Fields, and Boolean fields.

ON24 will display the Campaign fields in drop-down lists applicable for the field type.

Note: The integration does not support multi-picklist fields.

Click Finish to enable the integration.  Navigate back to the Integrations page to see that the solution is now active.

Once enabled, the integration will display as active. You can update authentication or mapping anytime using the Configure button.

To deactivate, use the Disconnect button.

When you want to connect your Campaign to an ON24 event, you will need to enter the Campaign ID in the Campaign Code field under Details on the Overview page of the event.

Note: When the integration is first configured, it will pull in and push to Salesforce the last 30 days of data.  It is recommended that you enter the campaign ID in the Campaign Code field prior to configuring the integration.

Reach out to ON24 Support for any issues. 

Timing

Scheduled Timing

Scheduled timing is when the data passes from ON24 to your end system.  The integration is scheduled to run every hour, syncing the last 24 hours of activity.  After the first activation, it will push the last 30 days of activity. 

Note: Scheduled timing is different from Availability Timing.

 

Availability Timing

Availability Timing refers to when data is available in the data warehouse for the integration platforms to collect.

Timing may fluctuate on heavy traffic days.

  • Registrant - 15 minutes
  • Attended Live - 30 minutes to 2 hours after the event ended
  • Attended On-demand - 4 to 12 hours

Data is processed and made available in a defined order based on business relevance. 

  • Registrant data is prioritized and becomes available first. Attendee data follows once event activity has fully completed and reporting is finalized.
  • Within attendee data, live event information is prioritized ahead of on-demand activity due to its immediate relevance. On-demand data is processed after live data and appears once all reporting is complete.

This prioritization ensures that the most relevant and time-sensitive information is available first, while maintaining consistent and reliable reporting across all data sets.

Note: Availability timing is different from Scheduled timing.

FAQs

What is the connection type for the Salesforce Sales Cloud Integration?
The Salesforce sales Cloud Integration connection is an OAuth 2.0 flow Enabled Connected App named ON24 Marketplace.  This connection does support Salesforces Security enhancement requirements.
What are the recommended ON24 state guidelines if our Salesforce uses 2 digit state codes to recognize state values?
ON24’s registration page includes a “state” field for USA and Canadian registrants. The state/province is spelled out (i.e., California, Florida, etc.). 
If your Salesforce org is setup to support 2 digits (i.e., CA, FL, etc.), then you will need to log into Webcast Elite, go to the event, add a custom field in the registration page, add a drop-down type field, and manually enter the 2 digits per state in the Choice 1, 2, 3, etc. fields. 
If you would like assistance with this, please contact your Customer Success Manager to explore a Professional Service team consultation engagement.
What are the best practices to ensure there are no country state validation errors?
Please remove the Country State validation in the lead object under validation rules in Salesforce. 
Unfortunately, Scribe has issues putting the State and Country in the correct order, which is supposed to be Country first and then State next. If you are unable to remove the Country State validation, then setup an integration user in Salesforce if one is not provided to bypass the validation rule. 
See the instructions in the next accordion.
How do I set up a user exception to a validation rule?

To allow your integration to process records without validation failure, login to Salesforce and create a new user named User Integration, if one has not already been provided by Salesforce.

Go to your Salesforce integration as this user.  

  • Under the Connections section, click on the Salesforce connection you created. 
  • Press the Authenticate  
  • Press Save

In Salesforce: 

  • Go to Settings and press Setup
  • Under Objects and Fields, go to Object Manager 
  • Click the object that is producing the error
  • Click Validation Rules and click on the validation that the integration error indicates
  • In the Validation Rule Detail section, go to the Error Condition Formula field. Add the following snippet of code listed below. It is recommended to add it to the beginning of the formula, but it can be placed at the end.  

         $Profile.Name <> "Integration User" 

How are duplicate email addresses handled?  For example, multiple people may share the same email address in Salesforce.
The data integration will match on the first record it comes across. According to some tests performed by our customers, the first created email address was the one the data integration came across. 
However, every Salesforce instance is different and we recommend you perform your own tests. 
In addition to duplicates, please be aware of fuzzy matches. "Fuzzy matching will return a match when two fields are alike (similar)." 
For example, Salesforce may believe Lead A and Lead B are the same person because they have similar email addresses and share the same first and last names. ON24 does not have a workaround solution for it, as it's driven by Salesforce. 
Check out Salesforce's article for more information.  
What data syncs when the Campaign Member is created at Registration?
The Salesforce Sales Cloud Integration will sync only Campaign ID, Contact/Lead ID, and Status = Registered.

Registration

External registration is not supported. Use Unified Seamless Registration instead.

Legacy Set Up

Note: ON24 integration with Salesforce Sales Cloud has moved to self-service.  The instructions in this tab are for legacy users who are using TIBCO Scribe.  If you aren't sure about your solution, contact Support.

Before you begin, you'll need to set up an API Token. 

In your ON24 account, open the Integrations dashboard from Platform Tools and click on API Tokens and click Provision a New API Access Token.  Make note of the Token Key and Token Secret.

Step 1 - TIBCO Scribe

ON24 leverages TIBCO Scribe as our iPaaS provider to pass data from ON24 to Salesforce. As a result, your technical resource will receive access to your ON24 TIBCO Scribe account.

After receiving the TIBCO Scribe email invitation your technical resource will need to follow the Tibco Scribe Connector Guide which consists of:

  • Creating a new Scribe user account and Logging into Scribe.
    • The Scribe user logging in needs to have create, read, and update permissions for the contact and lead objects. The Marketing User setting needs to be enabled on the Salesforce user profile, which will grant campaign and campaign member permissions.
  • Adding a Connection specifically  for your Salesforce instance. The connection may be with your sandbox and/or production org, depending on your preference. 
Step 2 - Review API Documentation
  • Download the attached LEGACY ON24 API Data Salesforce Sales Cloud spreadsheet and start with the Instructions worksheet. This document provides the available fields that may be passed from ON24 to Salesforce.
    • Please work with your Salesforce admin team to configure any potential custom fields within Salesforce directly.
    • Once the API Data document is completed, please send this document back to your Integration Specialist
  • Reminder - If our system doesn't see any activity on a specific token it will be disabled after 60 days. The number of days inactive will reset when it sees activity. You can see the Last Activity date and time by going to Platform Tools > Integrations > API Tokens. Warning notices will be sent for tokens as they approach 60 days without seeing activity.
Step 3 - Salesforce Configuration

In Salesforce, navigate to your Setup page to create fields for Campaign and Campaign Members.

 

Search for Object Manager in the Navigation on the left side of the page. 

 

In the Object Manager, add the fields you want information on in your Campaign Object and Campaign Member Object.  They will be located within the Object Manager List.

 

Click on Campaign Object > Fields & Relationships to bring up the Fields & Relationships page.

 

Click on New to create a new field.

Select Data Type.  This will need to match what is in the spreadsheet for that specific field.  For example, choose Number to set up an Event ID field.

 

Type out the field label name.  Click outside of the box to generate the field name.  This field will need to be added back to the ON24 Salesforce API Data Spreadsheet so the Integrations team can use it for mapping.  When you're done, click Next. 

 

Check Marketing User in the Field-Level Security for Profile section.  It should default to checked if the user creating these fields has this in their permissions.  Click Next.

If you have more fields to set up, click Save & New, then repeat the process above.  If you are done setting up fields, click Save.

Repeat these steps for Campaign Member Object (Registrant and Attendee Data).

Additional Recommendations
  • State Field Guidelines - ON24’s registration page includes a “state” field for USA and Canadian registrants. The state/province is spelled out (i.e., California, Florida, etc.). If your Salesforce org is setup to support 2 digits (i.e., CA, FL, etc.), then you will need to log into Webcast Elite, go to the event, add a custom field in the registration page, add a drop-down type field, and manually enter the 2 digits per state in the Choice 1, 2, 3, etc. fields. If you would like assistance with this, please contact your Customer Success Manager to explore a Professional Service team consultation engagement.
  • Country State Field Validation - Remove the Country State validation in the lead object under validation rules in Salesforce. Unfortunately, Scribe has issues putting the State and Country in the correct order, which is supposed to be Country first and then State next. If you are unable to remove the Country State validation, then setup an integration user in Salesforce if one is not provided to bypass the validation rule. Follow the instructions for user exceptions in the FAQs tab.
  • If you plan to use the ON24 registration form and want to create a lead from the registrant, then your Webcast Elite event’s registration form must include the required fields Email, Last Name, and Company. Inform your Integration Specialist if a workaround is needed.
  • If your Email Opt-In field is a text field, provide your Integration Specialist with the API values (i.e., yes/no, true/false, etc.). If you are not familiar with the difference between an API and label value, then please contact your administrator.
  • Let your Integration Specialist know if you want ON24 to create the campaign member status (i.e., registered, attended, attended on-demand).
Step 4 - Send the Spreadsheet to ON24

Once the spreadsheet has been filled out with all the Field Names, this document will need to be sent to the ON24 Integrations team. Note: You will most likely already have a case with this team you will want to reply to them in this case. 

After the spreadsheet has been sent the integrations team will map these fields within your Scribe org. This process can take a few days to complete pending any errors they come across, they will communicate to you if you need to make any changes on your end in order to get these errors fixed. 

Step 5 - Test the Integration

Once the mapping is complete, we will want to test the integration to make sure all the connections are working properly and that the fields match up to what your company is expecting.

In order for the test to be completed, your team will need to create a campaign in Salesforce and during the creation process in make sure to add the event ID the integrations team provides you in your ON24 Event ID field. Note that the commas will be displayed as this is a number field and this is expected behavior and that this Event ID will be one ON24 has created with dummy data specifically used for testing.

Save the campaign then let ON24 know that the Campaign has been created. ON24 will then push the test data over to your Salesforce Campaign, to populate the fields you have created and create campaign members for that campaign. 

Check the data that was pushed over by clicking on the Campaign name.

To see the details of the Campaign and Event data, click on the Details section.  

To see the Campaign Member Details (Registrant and Attendee) you will want to be on the Related Tab and scroll to Campaign Members. Then click View All.  

This will provide a table of your Campaign Members.  

To get further detail for each Registrant or Attendee, click on their name.  This allows you to see details for this Lead/Contact as it pertains to this specific Event, depending on the fields that were created and mapped.

For example on the attendee data if they attended it live or on demand, how long they watched the webinar for (minutes) and if they answered any polls during the webinar.  

After you have checked to make sure everything has gone through with the test data, test the integration with one of your previous webinars to see how your data will be processed and sent over. Note that if you are a new client this step will not be able to be complete as you won’t have any previous events to select. 

  •  You will need to select a previous ON24 event and provide the integrations team with that event ID. You will also need to make sure a campaign has been created with that event ID in your ON24 Event ID field as noted previously in step 4B.
  • Once that has been provided ON24 will change the connections within your Scribe org and then push the data. They will let you know when this has been completed.
  • Once completed you will want to check your records. 
Step 6 - Go Live with the Integration
Once everything is good from the tests we will have one final step in order to make the integration go live and that will be to have it run on a schedule. The options for running your integration can be anywhere from every hour to once a day. This will depend on limits your company may have for API calls and how often you want your data to be updated. Most clients choose to have their integration run every 2 hours. You will need to provide the integrations team with this schedule. After that has been set up your integration will be live.  
In order for the integration to keep running smoothly you will need to make sure you create a campaign for each event that you have within ON24 as this is a 1-to-1 relationship. If a campaign is not created the data will not pass over.

articles.name: Zoho Integrations Overview
articles.title: Zoho Integrations Overview
articles.body:
This article centralizes all integrations information regarding Zoho, including setup instructions and timing information.

 

  • Things to Know
  • Set Up
  • Timing
  • Backfills
  • Registration

Things to Know

This integration was built and is supported by Zoho.

Set Up

  • Follow the instructions in the Zoho knowledge base from here.
  • Install the Zoho ON24 webinar extension here.
  • Since Zoho built this data integration solution, contact Zoho's Support team with further questions.

Timing

Implementation Timing

The completion time to integrate data from the ON24 platform to Zoho will vary. They have created the data integration connector and are responsible for provisioning new integrations.

Contact their Support team or your account representatives at Zoho for questions.

 

Scheduled Timing

Scheduled timing is when the data passes from ON24 to your end system.

According to Zoho's Knowledge Center article, synchronization can occur daily or weekly.

Contact Zoho's Support Team if you have further questions.

Note: Scheduled timing is different from Availability Timing.

 

Availability Timing

Availability Timing refers to when data is available in the data warehouse for the integration platforms to collect.

Timing may fluctuate on heavy traffic days.

  • Registrant - 15 minutes
  • Attended Live - 30 minutes to 2 hours after the event ended
  • Attended On-demand - 4 to 12 hours

Data is processed and made available in a defined order based on business relevance. 

  • Registrant data is prioritized and becomes available first. Attendee data follows once event activity has fully completed and reporting is finalized.
  • Within attendee data, live event information is prioritized ahead of on-demand activity due to its immediate relevance. On-demand data is processed after live data and appears once all reporting is complete.

This prioritization ensures that the most relevant and time-sensitive information is available first, while maintaining consistent and reliable reporting across all data sets.

Note: Availability timing is different from Scheduled timing.

Backfills

We do not believe backfills are available, but reach out to the Zoho support team for clarification.

Registration

External registration is not supported.  Use Unified Seamless Registration instead.

articles.name: WebRTC and TURN Server Use / Configuration
articles.title: WebRTC and TURN Server Use / Configuration
articles.body:
This article provides steps for resolving TURN server issues when connecting to the ON24 platform.

 

WebRTC Protocols - Presenter Webcam and Screenshare Access

The ON24 component your users are making use of is a cloud audio/video bridge (usually referred to as “VPB” for Video Presenter Bridge).  It accepts audio connections and video connections in a variety of flavors.  Audio uses SIP dial, VCU uses H.323 or SIP, Webcam and Screenshare use WebRTC.  The problematic component is the webRTC piece, so let me focus on that.

WebRTC requires a control connection (HTTPS over 443) and separate audio and video stream channels in both directions, with audio and video connections over UDP high ports.  That doesn't work in most enterprise networks because High Ports are blocked. The user (browser on your internal network) sets up a bridge session (using HTTPS, which works); the bridge and browser then looks for the paths into and out of the network. 

To establish the channels through which audio and video are sent, webRTC uses a process called “discovering ICE candidates”.  This identifies the conference nodes in the bridge system and the means available (ports addresses, protocols) to reach the nodes.  This negotiation process is logged to the console section of the browser dev tools, so it can be easily visualized.  The negotiation process first attempts the native protocols (which use UDP High ports 40000-49999 for audio and video transit and do not support NAT).  These normally are blocked inside corporate networks, but they are always tried at each session initiation, event after it’s clear that they don’t work.  The system then attempts to use a relay (TURN) server, which uses TLS over TCP to move the A/V traffic from the user browser inside the enterprise network filters to the TURN server.  The TURN server then handles the relay of the audio and video via the UDP ports and protocols the VPB conference nodes expect inside the ON24 network.

 

TURN Server Activation

TURN server activation (RFC 5766) used to do an unencrypted preamble that would frequently block successful setup of TURN relay.  Now, we’ve converted to secure TURN (TURNS), which avoids the unencrypted preamble and almost always works without intervention.  NOTE:  this is not HTTPS traffic; it will not be recognized as such by the pac file process.  If your proxy is handling via a CONNECT using HTTP1.1, it will not work.

The Proxy/firewall config required to support this depends on the network configuration on the client side.
 

  • Since the TURNS traffic (TCP over TLS) is encrypted, it will go over the proxy path without incident, given that the pac file will recognize the need to pick it up and send to proxy path.  If it does not recognize the TURNS protocol, then the pac file must select by addresses and route them to the proxy.  Addresses are 199.83.47.94 and 199.83.46.94, and that traffic will likely be flowing to the firewall where it’ll be blocked.  We just need a rule in the pac file that’ll pick up the TURNS traffic or the addresses.  The FQDN which is the destination giving the two addresses indicated is TURN.FAV.ON24.COM.  With the protocol reference, the URI is turns:turn.fav.on24.com
  • Quite frequently, a Palo Alto Networks firewall will require that you allow the STUN protocol.  TURNS is a parts of the STUN RFC (5766), so PAN recognizes the STUN application spec.

 

Verifying Success

Google (authors of webRTC) provides a test link to see if the TURN server discovery process is working:

https://webrtc.github.io/samples/src/content/peerconnection/trickle-ice/

in the STUN or TURN URI:  turns:turn.fav.on24.com:443?transport=tcp

In TURN User:   on24user

In TURN password: nev2Eni@

Click add server and remove any other servers that may be there, then hit “gather candidates”.  You have to get a “relay” response to confirm TURNS is working.

 

Diagram of Webcam and Screen Share Transport

articles.name: Capturing Troubleshooting logs from your Web Browser
articles.title: Capturing Troubleshooting logs from your Web Browser
articles.body:
This article provides step-by-step instructions on how to capture a HAR trace and Console log for troubleshooting purposes.

Occasionally when connecting with support console or HAR logs may be requested. A .har file (or HTTP Archive format) is used to log a web browser's interaction with a site and to export detailed performance data about web pages it loads.  A Console log is used to view messages or errors provided by the webpage being visited. These logs can be valuable when troubleshooting a number of different types of issues. This article outlines the process to capture both HAR and Console logs.

 

Chrome  

You can record your HTTP session (for a HAR file) using the Network tab in the Developer Tools in Chrome. 

  • Right-click within the browser window and select "Inspect" or click F12 to open Developer Tools.
  • Click on the Network Tab.
  • Check the box next to Preserve log.

  • Look for a round button at the top left to Record.

    • Make sure it is red. If it is grey, click it once to start recording.

  • Reproduce the issue.
  • Stop the recording.

  • Click the download icon to Export Har .

To capture a Console log to assist in troubleshooting as well.

  • Right-click within the browser window and select "Inspect" or click F12 to open Developer Tools.
  • Click on the Console Tab.
  • Right click on a line in the log and choose 'save as'

 Edge 

You can record your HTTP session (for a HAR file) using the Network tab in the Developer Tools in Edge. 

  • Right-click within the browser window and select "Inspect" or click F12 to open Developer Tools.
  • Select Inspect.
  • Click on the Network Tab.
  • Check the box next to the Preserve log.

  • Look for a round button at the top left to Record.

    • Make sure it is red. If it is grey, click it once to start recording.

  • Reproduce the issue.
  • Stop the recording.
  • Click the download icon to Export HAR (sanitized).

To capture a Console log to assist in troubleshooting as well.

  • Right-click within the browser window and select "Inspect" or click F12 to open Developer Tools.
  • Click on the Console Tab.
  • Right click on a line in the log and choose 'save as'.

Firefox 

Starting with Firefox 41, HAR files can be created without any additional extension.

  • Right-click within the browser window and select "Inspect" or click F12 to open Developer Tools.

  • Reproduce the issue

  • Save the capture by right-clicking on the grid and choosing "Save all as HAR"

  • Export the capture to a HAR fil

To capture a Console log to assist in troubleshooting as well.

  • Right-click within the browser window and select "Inspect" or click F12 to open Developer Tools.
  • Click on the Console Tab.
  • Right click on a line in the log and choose 'save all messages to file'.

  • The screenshot below is from Chrome, which allows Save As.

  • If having trouble saving, capturing a screenshot can be helpful.

articles.name: Capturing the Console Output from Chrome Using Dev Tools
articles.title: Capturing the Console Output from Chrome Using Dev Tools
articles.body:
Console logging is an easy way to inspect what an application does. Follow these simple steps to capture a console log within Chrome.

Open the webpage of interest and press F12 to access the Developer Tools.

Select the Console tab.

You’ll see a page that looks something like the view below, though the page content will be completely different.

Press shift + F5 to refresh the page.

Refreshing the page will ensure all up-to-date information is captured so ON24 Platform Support can assess the full picture of the issue. 
 

Right-click on the console display and select Save as to save the file.

Once the file is saved, send it to ON24 Platform Support.  The console log will allow the support team to see what is being blocked, and what is to be done to address the issue.

articles.name: Broadcast Video Network Requirements
articles.title: Broadcast Video Network Requirements
articles.body:
Review details on network requirements and ON24 ingress IP addresses for Broadcast Video events.

 

Pre-Event - Venue Network Assessment for RTMP Encode Onsite

Venue should provide 5-10 MB upload and download speed, dedicated, hard-wired Internet with ports open for streaming.

Ports that need to be open are:

  • RTMP default
  • RTMP Port 1935
  • RTMP Port 80
  • RTMP Port 443

Destination IP addresses (ON24  Ingress servers). The onsite encoder needs to be able to push a stream to fmseosa.on24.com  and fmseosb.on24.com on port 1935.

  • 199.83.44.226
  • 199.83.44.227
  • 199.83.45.71
  • 199.83.45.72

articles.name: Troubleshooting Virtual Conference Breakout Rooms
articles.title: Troubleshooting Virtual Conference Breakout Rooms
articles.body:
Steps to troubleshoot VC Breakout Rooms

Need to Knows

  1. We recommend designating a separate Breakout moderator from the webinar Presenter/Producer team.   VC Breakouts were designed to facilitate a two-way conversation between the audience and a guest speaker, sales rep, product consultant, or other relevant subject matter expert during the live webinar. 
  2. Initiating screen share is not supported on the Safari web browser. If you are a moderator who needs to screen share, use a different browser (Chrome, Firefox, or Edge). There is no Safari limitation for attendees viewing someone else’s screen share. It is only initiating a screen share on Safari that is not supported. 
  3. Presenters running Chrome Version 99.0.4844.74 on iOS/Mac (Big Sur 11.6 and other versions) may experience an inability to screen share in Breakout rooms (see fix below).   
  4. If joining a VC Breakout Room as a Moderator, you must join from a desktop or a laptop - joining via a mobile device is only supported for those joining a Breakout Room as an attendee.

General Troubleshooting Steps 

Step 1:  Begin by determining whether the problem may be caused by your network environment or the hardware.

You can use the following steps to determine this:

  1. Make sure you are using the latest version of a WebRTC-capable browser. Check here for a list of known major supported browsers. 
  2. Make sure you have the right ports open. You can check this by running a pre-call test. The pre-call test will be at the login screen of the breakout room.  This test should identify where there are any network issues connecting to the servers.  If something is blocked, proceed to Step 2.

Step 2:  Confirm Your Are Connected to the Internet

  1. Are you connected to the Internet?
  2. Web browser security settings (especially for Internet Explorer) may be too restrictive or blocking certain content.
  3. There may be a security restriction enforced on your network. Check out our guide on restrictive networks here. 

Step 3:  Confirm the Browser can Access the Camera/Microphone

If the earlier pre-call test didn't show any issues with the network, but you weren't able to see the camera/microphone functioning correctly:

  1. Please confirm that you are allowing access to your camera and microphone when prompted by the browser. You may have blocked access and saved this preference. Please check your browser settings to confirm this is not the case.
  1. Check if the camera works on a different browser. For instance, if you are using Firefox see if Chrome works instead.
  2. If you have multiple cameras, make sure that the right camera is selected.
  3. Does the camera work in the WebRTC demo?
  4. Does the camera work in another application, such as Microsoft Teams or Photo Booth?
  5. Still no luck?  Contact Support - we'd love to help. 

Known Screen Share Issue with iOS/Mac and Google Chrome Browser

Presenters running Chrome Version 99.0.4844.74 on iOS/Mac (Big Sur 11.6 and other versions) may experience an inability to screen share in Breakout rooms (and during Live Video and Audio with screen share (Video Presenter Bridge) webinars).

To resolve this issue:

  1. Open System Preferences (users may hit CMD + Space and type “pref”)
  2. Open “Security & Privacy”
  3. On the left, scroll down to “Screen Recording” and click it.
  4. Users may need to click the lock on the bottom left to make changes and enter their credentials.
  5. Uncheck Chrome on the right.
  6. On the popup, click “Quit & Reopen”
  7. Re-check the Chrome box again.
  8. Click “Quit & Reopen”

articles.name: Getting Started with Connect Integrations
articles.title: Getting Started with Connect Integrations
articles.body:
Connect integrates data from the ON24 platform into several leading Customer Relationship Management (CRM), Marketing Automation Platform (MAP), and business intelligence tools. Data collected through ON24 events and content experiences is stored in ON24’s data warehouse through the ON24 REST API. 

  • What is Connect Integrations?
  • How Does it Work?
  • What End System to Integrate With?
  • Integration Support Services
  • FAQs

What is Connect Integrations?

Connect is a license that provides connectors, tools and access to get supported data into and out of the ON24 platform into several leading customer relationship management (CRM), marketing automation platforms (MAP), and business intelligence tools.

Note: Any manipulation, logic or formulations need to be done prior to sending data to ON24 or after retrieving from ON24.

  • A kick-off call, if requested
  • Up to two hours of iPaaS mapping of supported data for applicable systems
  • Access to ON24-hosted data that includes event, registrant, and attendee data across the ON24 platform suite for a single workspace

Each unit of Connect you purchase can be applied to a single workspace and allows you to integrate up to two end systems for that workspace.

 

What are the benefits of ON24 Connect?

Keep your sales and marketing teams informed on engagement activities to drive data-driven actions with a prospect.  

ON24 Connect allows all the rich, first-person insights generated by each experience to be integrated into an ecosystem of third-party applications. This can allow you to deliver actionable insights to: 

  • Enrich your marketing with engagement insights 
  • Extend your automation workflow with integrated data 
  • Optimize your personalization and performance 
  • Identify prospects with higher propensity to convert 
  • Build purposeful and coordinated engagement 

The benefits vary by end system. Visit our Supported Integrations article and click on the end system for more information.  

How Does it Work?

Data is Collected

  • A person may provide a first name, last name, email address, company name, and job title while registering for an event or content experience.
  • This person may attend the event and interact, such as answering a poll, clicking on a CTA, or asking a question.
  • ON24 collects this Attendee Engagement data (poll responses, survey responses, attended minutes, etc.)
  • ON24 also collects Attendee Conversion data, such those who actively reached out during the event to schedule a demo, book meetings, etc.   

 

Data is Stored in ON24’s Warehouse

  • All the data mentioned above is stored in ON24’s data warehouse through the ON24 REST API.
  • The REST API is the entrance to get access to the data. This is the method used to communicate with ON24’s data warehouse.
  • You will receive an API key and secret, which gives you permission to access the information you want, such as attendee data.   

 

Connections 

The various ways to access ON24's REST API include: 

  • ON24 Apps/Connectors - These are apps or connectors that were built and supported by ON24. The apps or connectors are available through ON24 Elite Integrations.
  • TIBCO Scribe - ON24 uses TIBCO as our iPaaS partner to pass data from ON24 to some end systems (i.e., Salesforce Marketing Cloud, Dynamics). 
  • 3rd Party Apps/Connectors - These are apps or connectors that were built by a third party, which is typically the end system. You will need to contact the vendor for integration support.
    • Note: Zapier is another iPaaS solution but it must be managed and supported by you. 
  • Client Code – You may choose to have your developers build a custom solution using the ON24 REST API to retrieve data for the following reasons: you want to retrieve more data than what the supported integration offers, you may want to integrate with an end system that isn’t currently supported, or you may want to control the security of your platform by not giving ON24 direct API access. 

 

Customer Platforms 

  • Data is sent to your end system from ON24. Some of the popular integrations are with Salesforce Sales Cloud, HubSpot, and Marketo.

What End System to Integrate With?

General Technical Considerations 

Supported End Systems

  • Here are the end systems that can integrate with ON24. If your end system is not on the list, you will need to build a custom data integration leveraging our REST API.  

 

How It Works

  • Before making a commitment, ensure you understand how the data integration works with your end system and the data you’ll receive.
  • Do you have the right system version (i.e., HubSpot Professional vs. HubSpot Enterprise) to collect the data?
  • Do you have access to all the objects or entities?
  • Check out the Connect Knowledge Center Home Page and click on the end system for more information. If the data integration does not meet your expectations, consider alternative integrations.  

 

Registration 

  • Will you use ON24 for registration?  Or an external form?  Find information here that can help you decide.  

 

Questions to Raise 

  • What data do you really need, why, and how will you be using it?  How can you future proof data you are already collecting in ON24 for your end system?
  • Who needs to see the data at your company? Are there enough licenses available for them to view it?  
  • Do you want your Marketing Automation System to qualify the leads before it enters the CRM?  
  • How does registration impact the way the integration processes the data? 
  • How will you calculate the ON24 ROI? 

Integration Support Services

Services to help you get up and running with the Connect product that provides the integration and access to several leading CRM and marketing automation platforms.

Additional Integration Support

This integration service is supported by the Integrations Support team as a one-time engagement to perform specific tasks related to end system integrations licensed through ON24 Connect (e.g.: Delivering data to end systems such as Marketo, HubSpot, Salesforce, Veeva, etc.).

 

When this service may be needed:

  • When 4 hours of basic support included with ON24 Connect​ is exceeded
  • When live person support is requested for a walk through of the data integration set up instructions ​
  • When an audit or step-by-step review of existing integration is needed/requested. New hire(s) tend to benefit from this to familiarize themselves with the ON24 Connect configurations and capabilities.
  • When moving from one end-system to another or when multiple integrations are in place and one or more needs to be replaced for a new end system.
  • Transitioning from the North America to the European Union data center and need confirmation that the setup of the ON24 integration is done correctly. 

 

Official Product Description:

  • Up to 15 hours of service provided approximately over a 30-day period for a single ON24 Platform Workspace, which may include: ​
    • Meeting(s) with ON24 integration team to review integration connection set up and options ​
    • Training, set-up and mapping
    • Collaborative assistance with setup, testing & troubleshooting​
    • Does not include end system support
Integration Management Services (IMS)

This is supported by the Integration Management Services team and is ideal for customers who are building one or multiple integration points between ON24 and one or many end systems.  This service has a one-year term and includes a dedicated ON24 resource for support. This service requires a scoping process prior to purchase to ensure needs can be met.

 

When this service may be needed:​​

  • You have multiple end-systems and need dedicated support and consultation of best practices.​​
  • You have limited availability of technical resources and wish to be assigned a dedicated technical resource who has historical knowledge of your integration (vs. opening a case and an available resource will be assigned). ​
  • You do not want to use the ON24 supported app or connector and would like to use Scribe to customize the integration for specific business needs
  • You would like to use Zapier but do not have the available internal technical resources. ​
  • You want to build a custom solution using the REST API and needs additional guidance interpreting the data. ​
  • Standard Scribe integration does not support your needs. Custom or non-standard mapping is required (i.e., writing to an entity that the standard template does not support) to meet business needs.

 

Official Product Description:​​

  • Named ON24 resource​
  • Manages troubleshooting and resolution of integration data issues
  • Consults on the use of Platform and Services data to support Client’s objectives
  • Quarterly analysis and report on effectiveness of use of integration and recommended improvements
  • Liaison between Client and ON24 resources, including product management, engineering, services, and platform operations and support
Marketo Launch Services (MLS)

This integration service is supported by a certified Adobe-Marketo partner as a one-time engagement to perform specific tasks related to end system integrations licensed through ON24 Connect to Marketo as well as related setup and configurations within Marketo.

 

When this service may be needed:

  • When 4 hours of basic support included with ON24 Connect​ is exceeded
  • When live person support is requested for the completion of data integration set up in both ON24 as well as the Marketo
  • You have limited access to technical resources and would like a certified expert to help with the integration
  • You would like data optimization best practice recommendations after the integration is complete​

 

Official Product Description:

  • Up to 10 hours of ON24 & Marketo integration support including: ​
  • ON24 & Marketo default integration ​
  • Review of Marketo Smart Campaign setup 
  • Review of Marketo Trigger
  • Effectiveness review & best practices for use of ON24 data in Marketo ​
  • Requires ON24 Connect
  • Service must be used within 90 days of purchase
Salesforce Launch Services (SLS)

This integration service is supported by a certified Salesforce partner as a one-time engagement to perform specific tasks related to end system integrations licensed through ON24 Connect to Salesforce as well as related setup and configurations within Salesforce.

 

When this service may be needed:

  • When 4 hours of basic support included with ON24 Connect is exceeded
  • When live person support is requested for the completion of data integration set up in both ON24 as well as the Salesforce
  • You have limited access to technical resources and would like a certified expert to help with the integration
  • You would like data optimization best practice recommendations after the integration is complete

 

Official Product Description:

  • 5 hours of Salesforce integration support
  • ON24 & Salesforce CRM Default Integration
  • Integration Testing and Deployment
  • 3 60 Min Subject Matter Expert sessions
  • Requires ON24 Connect
  • Service must be used within 90 days of purchase

FAQs

Should I use ON24 or an external registration form?

Find more information on using either type of registration form in this article.

Can I exclude an event from passing through the integration to our end-system?
Yes.  Add the custom tag APIEXCLUDE on the event's Overview page in Webcast Elite.  Using this tag, you can prevent its data from appearing in the API response and avoid passing the data through any ON24 integration service.  
This feature is useful for scenarios like test events and internal events where data should not be sent to a MAP.

Note: In regard to REST API, Event-level endpoints will still return the data. Event-level endpoints require clients to explicitly call out the specific eventid in the request path, so these endpoints will return data even if the custom tag has been added to an event.

Where can I find the Event ID and Audience URL key for events?

For webinars, grab the Event ID and key from the Audience URL of the event.  The highlighted text is the portion you need.

Where can I find registered or attended data in ON24?

You can find data on registrants and attendees in ON24 via the Reports URL for the event.  

Find more information here

articles.name: SSO Setup Steps
articles.title: SSO Setup Steps
articles.body:
Provides the steps to setup SSO

Setting up SSO for ON24 requires several steps. This article reviews what you need, timing, steps, and what you should expect during the setup.

What you will need to start:

1) Speak to your CSM or Account Manger about adding SSO to your contract. The ON24 team can not begin setup until this service is added to your account.
2) It is advised to have one main contact\team for the setup, SSO takes communication and we want to make sure that the right people are involved.
3) Decide what systems or 'SSO type' you will be implementing. The "type" here refers to where the SSO will be used Elite Admin, Audience, Hub, VC, Go Live or a combination.
4) Your team will need to be able to acquire and send SSO Certificates, Metadata, and attributes. This may take a technical resource from your team.

Timing:

1) Allow for several days to allow for SSO fully implemented.
2) If a specific window of time is required ensure that is clearly communicated and that all required answers and documents are provided ahead of time, also allowing time for testing and communication.
3) Implementation requires action from both teams, including testing. Delays in receiving clarification, certificates, or testing confirmation will delay the SSO completion.

Setup Steps

To begin your SSO implementation, submit a request to Platform Support. Need the steps to submit a ticket? Reference the "How to Submit a Support Ticket" section of this article.

  1. ON24 sends links to QA and Prod SSO Certificates, Metadata and Attributes to Client
  2. Client sends QA metadata, certificate and attributes name(s) to ON24
  3. ON24 setup QA
  4. ON24 sends Client updated QA metadata file and test URL
  5. Client swaps out QA metadata file and then tests URL to validate access
  6. Client sends ON24 Prod metadata and certificate
  7. ON24 setup Prod
  8. ON24 sends Client updated Prod metadata file
  9. Client confirms cut-over date for ON24 account
  10. ON24 enables SSO for account

 

Testing

Please send screenshots of any error messages where the address bar is visible, as well as a SAML trace so that we can review the transactions. Use an application like the Chrome browser extension SAML-tracer to capture a JSON file and send this to ON24 to review.
