Note: Beta screen share is available for iOS. This feature will be enabled by request only. Contact Support for details. The ON24 screen share app will need to be downloaded from the app store. screen share is not available on Safari browsers v15 and older.

And if enabled, Presenters can grant permission to Attendees to share their screens.

  • All attendees have screen share initially disabled
  • Attendees that have been given permission will see the screen share icon at the top of their screen.
Pause/Restart/Seek Video Clips

When playing a video clip during a live Forums event, presenters can pause, restart, and seek the video. Hover over the video to access the video controls.

Note: If using a Mac device, this feature is supported with macOS Sequoia (and future operating systems) only.

Starting and Stopping the Event

Once in the event, presenters have the ability to Start the session directly from the Audience Console.

When everyone is a presenter, the event will start automatically.

Settings and Start Recording/End Recording can be accessed from the three dots menu. Settings will display Connection options. 

Note:  If you want an archive of the event, manually Start Recording to create an archive.

End Event and Leave will display once the event has been started.   

When the event has concluded, select End Event and a confirmation message will display.  

Once an event has been ended, it cannot be restarted.  

 

All Present Types

System Requirements

As a general best practice, close all unnecessary applications and clear your cache before logging into each event. This will improve browser performance while ensuring all recent uploads and changes are visible.

Troubleshooting
  • Confirm all presenters are using a supported browser (Chrome or Edge)
    • Note: The Firefox browser is not supported for Forums. To ensure full functionality and platform reliability, please use the latest version of Google Chrome or Microsoft Edge
  • Make sure ON24 has access to your microphone and camera within the browser - review troubleshooting for connecting to a live event.
  • Try connecting with a Virtual Private Network (VPN). If the issue persists after troubleshooting, it is usually related to network configurations such as VPN or firewalls.
  • All presenters should have a stable, high-speed internet connection.
  • Refresh the page by pressing F5 (Command + R for Macs) or try another browser. Refreshing the page will not end the webinar.
  • Clear cache/cookies to optimize browser performance.
  • Close all unused applications and browser tabs. This is especially true of collaboration apps (Zoom, WebEx, Teams) because they will use all of the available Central Processing Unit (CPU) resources.
  • Reboot the computer if time permits.
  • Find more help in the Forums Troubleshooting article.
  • If these tips do not resolve the issue, please contact Support so our team can work with you and your internal IT department to find a resolution.

articles.name: Elite Studio Interface
articles.title: Elite Studio Interface
articles.body:
The Elite Studio interface design emphasizes the efficient use of screen real estate and a customizable presenter interface.

There is flexibility in the display of windows in Elite Studio. Each presenter can customize their view. Only their view will be impacted, and it doesn’t affect other presenters.  

Pro Tip: User settings are saved per login and will remain as they were during the last login session.

Find more information on using the using the tools in Elite Studio.
 

 

Setup

The Setup tool is located in the upper-right corner and is exclusively available to Producer roles who can upload content and set user views. 

Slide Decks

From the Slides tab in the Setup window, upload your PowerPoint or PDF.  Click the arrow icon and select the file from your network or click and drag your file to this area to upload.

Slides will upload first then convert. You can change tabs or close the panel while the file uploads/converts in the background.

To replace the existing file, click the Replace icon copy and select the updated file.  The first slide deck you upload cannot be deleted, it can only be replaced.

You can hide a slide by hovering over it and clicking the eye icon. This will will grey out the slide thumbnail and prevent it from being shown to the audience.

Slide decks and individual slides cannot be deleted, only replaced or hidden.

To download the existing file, click the Download icon.

Find information on slide deck specifications here.

Video Clips

Upload your video clips in the Video tab.  Click the arrow icon and select the file from your network or click and drag your file to this area to upload.

To rename the video, click the name and then type a new/edited name.

Make sure the Audio checkbox is selected, so the audio can be heard by the audience.

To delete the video, click the trash can.

To download the video, click the download icon.

Find information on video clip specifications here.

Polls

Polls are a great way to capture the voice of the audience.  Poll questions can be used for all present types, and are available during on-demand viewing. 

Click on Polls to add and style questions.

Find more information on creating and managing poll questions here. 

URLs

Add links to your presentation using URL. 

Find more information on creating and managing URLs here. 

Virtual Backgrounds

You can upload a custom virtual background that aligns with your brand or console design in the Virtual Backgrounds tab. 

Find more information on using this feature here. 

Lower Thirds

Use lower third banners to highlight speakers or introduce new sections of your presentation. 

Find more information on creating and using lower third banners here. 

Presenter Tools

In Setup under the Assign Tools option, Producers can control which tools are available for each role and choose whether those tools are open or closed by default. This helps ensure your team sees only what they need to see. Use the checkboxes to decide which tools are available for each role. Then choose if the tool is open by default.

Note: When you copy an event the assigned tool settings carry over, ensuring consistency across your events.

Why Assign Tools?
Before sharing the Presenter link with speakers, the Producers will need to decide which tools the Presenters and Q&A Screeners should have access to. Assigning tools by role ensures:

  • Focus for Speakers: One-time or guest speakers aren’t overwhelmed by unnecessary options or distracted from their primary speaking role.
  • Efficiency for Producers: Producers have full control over the event without clutter.
  • Security & Control: Limits access to sensitive tools (e.g., broadcast controls) to authorized roles only.
  • Streamlined Experience: Each role sees only what they need, reducing confusion and mistakes during live events.


Recommended Tools by Role

Producers
By default, Producers have full access to all tools, including:

  • Communication Tools: Q&A, Team Chat, Attendees, Attendee Chat, Presenters List
  • Event Features: Breakout Rooms (if added to the audience console), Preview, Engagement Tools, Pulse Check
  • Visual Elements: Lower Thirds, Virtual Backgrounds
  • Setup: Slides, Video Clips, Polls, URLs, Banners, and Backgrounds

 
Presenters

  • Slides 
  • Slide Notes
  • Polls (open if they will push polls themselves)
  • Video clips (optional, usually closed unless they need control)
  • Q&A (open if they will engage with audience questions)
  • Team Chat (should be included so they can communicate behind the scenes with fellow presenters)

 
Q&A Moderators

  • Q&A (open by default)
  • Team Chat (should be included so they can communicate behind the scenes with presenters)
  • Attendee Chat (optional, unless they manage audience engagement)
  • Everything else closed to avoid distraction

 
Best Practices

  • Use tools strategically: Open only what’s essential for each role.
  • Keep it simple for speakers: Less is more. Avoid giving them tools they won’t use.
  • Test before going live: Confirm each role’s view matches your plan.
  • Keep it consistent: Create an event template to maintain consistency across events, as tool assignments will carry over.

 

Top Toolbar

The Top Toolbar in Elite Studio lets you update your connection, change the virtual background you're using, share your screen, access the Storyboard setup, or change light/dark mode.

You can also quickly mute your camera or microphone by clicking either Video or Audio.

 

 

Main Stage

The Main Stage of Elite Studio is where the main bulk of your view will be. You can resize the Main Stage using the vertical and horizontal handles.

The aspect ratio is locked, but the image can be resized horizontally.

You can choose which tool you would like featured in the Main Stage.

 

Live View - default for Producers and Presenters

  • From here you can open the Storyboard, access Slide Notes, use the draw/whiteboard tools, enable animated reactions, enable keyboard shortcuts, or move through the Storyboard using the Previous/Next arrows.

 

Media Player

  • Corresponds to the Media Player in the Audience Console
  • This is where all presenters connected to Elite Studio will show
  • Choose the webcam layouts from here

 

Q&A - default for Q&A Moderators

  • All questions submitted by attendees in the Ask Question tool will appear here.

Click on the arrow in any of these tools to move them to the Main Stage. Hover over the tool to find additional options, such as answer options in Q&A or the webcam layouts in the Media Player.

 

Icon List and Right Panel

The icons displayed in the icon list are determined by the settings chosen by the producer. While we provide recommendations for these settings, the actual icons shown will vary based on the producer’s selections. The icons in the icon list cannot be arranged.
 
Open/close the icon list using the arrows >>.  
 
To open a tool from the icon list, click the icon.

Once opened, tools will appear in the right panel. You can adjust the height of each window by dragging its bottom border when it is open in the right panel. Windows can also be reordered by clicking and dragging them within the panel.

Minimize the tool by clicking the ▲ triangle. Close the tool by clicking the X.  

The tools themselves will be hidden when closed, but their icons will remain visible in the icon list.

articles.name: Engagement Hub & Target Seamless Registration
articles.title: Engagement Hub & Target Seamless Registration
articles.body:
ON24 Engagement Hub and Target support external registration forms like those you may be using with your marketing automation platform. This enables you to send registrant information from your external registration page to ON24's registration database, using a script.

This article covers the legacy version of seamless registration.  We recommend using Unified Seamless Registration instead.  

Find more information on using Unified Seamless Registration here. 

 

Things to Know

  • When using seamless registration, use the same registration fields in the same order, to ensure the data carries over correctly. Find more information on registration mapping here.
  • Engagement Hub and Target share the same database.
  • See below for more information on which fields can be passed to ON24. 

Looking to use seamless registration with an embedded Engagement Hub?  Find more information in the How to Embed an Engagement Hub article.

 

Setup

It's a two-step process to add an external registration form to your Engagement Hub or Target.

To setup for Engagement Hub, click the Registration tab. For Target experiences, if you are sharing or inheriting registration from the Engagement Hub, these features are automatically carried over. If not sharing, you can find these Seamless Registrations options in the Attributes tab.

Select External and input your external Registration Page URL (where you will collect registration) in the text box. 

Registration can be prompted in one of two ways:

  • When accessing the Engagement Hub or Target page - forces viewers to register before they can view content
  • When launching gated content - the Engagement Hub or Target experience will automatically load for viewers, however, the registration fields will pop up once they try to view a piece of gated content 

To send registrant data, a script can be used with an HTTP GET or HTTP POST.  For both methods, you may use either HTTP or HTTPS.

Important: The unique ID (eventId), Audience Key (key), and Session ID (sessionid) parameters must be included regardless of the method you use to send the Registrant data. 

Contact Platform Support if you need help identifying your Audience Key.

For the submission process on your registration form, use the Seamless Registration URL to pass the user data to ON24, and register the user.

 

Using "HTTP GET" Method

The GET method relies on JavaScript redirection, which requires a web browser; this method will fail if used for server-to-server requests.

Example: Sending First Name, Last Name, and Email 
https://gateway.on24.com/wcc/autoreg?eventId=XXXXXX&sessionid=1&key=XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX&firstName=paul01252&lastName=test2&email=paul01252@paul.com&targetUrl=

 

Using "HTTP POST" Method

You can also send registrant data to the ON24 Platform by using a POST method.

Example: Sending First Name, Last Name, Email and Company
<form name="myform" action="https://gateway.on24.com/wcc/autoreg" method="POST">
  <input type="hidden" name="eventId" value="XXXX">
  <input type="hidden" name="sessionid" value="1">
  <input type="hidden" name="key" value="XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX">
  <input type="text" name="firstName" value="john">
  <input type="text" name="lastName" value="smith">
  <input type="text" name="email" value="john.smith@abc.com">
  <input type="text" name="company" value="ABC, Inc.">
             <input type="hidden" name="targetUrl" value="">
  <input type="submit" value="Submit Form"/>
</form>

 

What fields can be passed to ON24?

Specific fields can be passed to the ON24 registration database. The parameter names are case-sensitive. Please note that you may have changed the Display Label of the field, but the underlying parameter name is retained.  See this link for seamless registration parameters.  Please note that you may have changed the Display Label of the field, but the underlying parameter name is retained.

Be sure to associate the fields with the correct parameter name (i.e. pass the Attendee's email in the "email" field rather than in some other field). If the data you want to store is not in one of the named demographic fields, use std1-std10.

Any values sent which exceed the designated character limit will be truncated.

Records containing the email address of a registrant who is already in the database will be updated with the latest values being sent.

articles.name: API Tokens
articles.title: API Tokens
articles.body:
API tokens and secret keys are essential for using APIs and automating the webinar workflow, allowing users to create, delete, and report on their webinars.

 

What is the Purpose of a Token?

A token key and token secret is the authentication method used to access the ON24 REST API. Through the REST API, you can obtain valuable information, such as event, registration, and attendee data. 

The ON24 REST API allows customers to directly access the ON24 analytic data. Customers can set up their own services to retrieve the data and import activity information into their CRM, Marketing Automation Platform, Business Intelligence Platform, or any custom repository.

An Application Program Interface (API) is a set of routines, protocols, and tools for building software applications and specifies how software components should interact. The ON24 API makes it easier to develop a program by providing all the building blocks. A programmer then puts the blocks together. 

 

How to Provision a Token

First, ensure your ON24 contract includes "Connect."

Ask the ON24 Platform user(s) with Manage Integrations permission to log into the ON24 Platform. They are the only ones who can provision the token. 

At the top menu, click on Platform Tools, then select Integrations

On the left menu bar, click on API Tokens.

Press the purple Provision a New API Access Token button.

For the Login field, select any user. 

For the Access field, select All endpoints. Press the Provision button.

Rename the token to reflect it's purpose. For example, consider naming it "Salesforce" if you're using it for an ON24-to-Salesforce data integration.

Tokens and secret keys are displayed allowing the user to disable an existing token if needed.  Provisioning is only enabled when there are no tokens or keys previously assigned to the account for the specific producer login.


Inactive Tokens

Tokens with no activity will be disabled after 60 days. Warning email notifications will be sent for tokens as they approach 60 days without seeing activity.

To identify the last time the token was used, look at the Last Activity field. 

To prevent a token from being disabled, simply pass data through the token.

After the token has been disabled, it can be re-enable by pressing the Enable This Token button.

The number of days inactive will reset when it sees activity.

articles.name: Confirming Audio and Video Bitrates
articles.title: Confirming Audio and Video Bitrates
articles.body:
This page outlines the process for confirming audio and video bitrates for Live and On Demand streams. ON24 provides streams for webcast events using HTTP Live Streaming (HLS) and MPEG-DASH (MPD). The material provided here describes how a user can determine the bit rates for these streams, which are visible in the Dev Tools component of modern browsers. Both HLS and MPD streams are provided using Adjustable Bit Rate technology.  This means that multiple streams are normally available. 

 

Process Overview

  • Live events Live Video, Sim-2-Live, and Broadcast Video) are streamed via HLS. The player starts the video at 300k, then steps up to 2500k if bandwidth is sufficient. 
  • Pre-recorded events (Simu-live and On Demand) are streamed via MPD.  The player starts the video at 1000 Kbps and uses ABR to lower it to 500 Kbps if the network capacity available cannot support 1000 Kbps streams. Video clips in the slide area will stream at the same rate.
  • Audio and video streams are two separate streams with HLS and MPD, as will be displayed below.
  • Using Hive or Kollective changes the rules outlined here; please contact Platform Support if streaming information is needed for those environments.

The process for both MPD and HLS is the same. The browser fetches the streams directly.  As a consequence, stream components are directly visible.  The components are:

  • Manifests – XML that describes the streams, available bit rates, etc.
  • Stream segments, or “chunks”, which are the actual streams

It is the ability to visualize these elements which are described below.

The process, then, is:

  1. Open Dev Tools in the target browser – Chrome and Edge are described below.
  2. Locate Manifest files or stream chunks
    1. Manifest files show bit rates available, but metadata can be wrong in some situations
    2. Chunk files show the bit rate in the file name where ABR streams are in use.
    3. Interpret Chunk names to find audio vs video and stream speed. (see below)

 

ABR Stream Structure

ABR streams are structured differently in Live and On-Demand (OD) cases.  The “chunk” names outlined here address Live streams.  OD streams, which have “seg” names, are transcoded using different tools; one should focus on the manifest file to see the available rates.
 

Manifest Files

Manifest file structure and XML data elements are well documented on the web.  For example: 

  • https://www.brendanlong.com/the-structure-of-an-mpeg-dash-mpd.html
  • https://headendinfo.com/manifest-file/
  • https://ottverse.com/hls-http-live-streaming-how-does-it-work/

Manifest files for HLS have the '.m3u8' file suffix, and those for MPD end with '.mpd'.</p>

Decoding Live Stream Names

The bit rates of the HLS streams are in the names displayed in developers tools. Since the HLS chunks contain both audio and video, the rates reflect the audio rate plus the video rate:

Decoding On-Demand Stream Names

On-Demand streams are named differently, and the bit rates are not apparent on the face of the stream names:


 

Bit rates can be found by accessing the manifest file



Accessing the Dev Tools

Chrome

1.  Click on the More Options icon

2.  Click on More tools

3.  Click on Developer Tools


 

4.  Click on the Network tab

Edge

1.  Click on Settings and select More

2.  Click on F12 Developer Tools

3.  Click on the Network tab

articles.name: Go Live Participant Guide - Explore the Event
articles.title: Go Live Participant Guide - Explore the Event
articles.body:
Learn how to navigate a Go Live event.

At the top of the page, you’ll find navigation links with one or two (depending on how the event is configured) drop-down menus:

  • Agenda - provides a visual listing (cards) of all sessions with dates and times, and can be filtered by Tracks, Categories and for presenters, by the sessions they are presenting.
  • Event Stages
    • Main Stage - Accessible from the navigation bar on the top right of the screen or click a session tile for a session that’s currently underway. 
    • Side Stage - This is an additional stage option in Go Live that provides flexibility when organizing and delivering your live content.
  • Exhibit Hall - click here to access all of the Sponsor Locations within an event. Participants can also use the drop-down to directly choose and go to a Sponsor Location
  • Lounge - this location allows users to interact with their peers using video-based breakouts and text chat

 


 

To access the Event Stages, click the arrow icon to see links to go to the Main Stage and Side Stage.

articles.name: ON24 Services
articles.title: ON24 Services
articles.body:
ON24 can help you produce exceptional events with the expertise and skills of the ON24 Services Team. From start to finish, ON24 Services offerings include everything needed for a successful event.  This article provides an overview of all the services, management packages, and launch packages offered by ON24.

 

Click on the type of service to learn more about the packages available for each product.

  • Webcasting Services
  • Forums Services
  • Captioning and Localization Services
  • Creative Services
  • Production Services
  • Smart Plays
  • Engagement Hub and Target Services
  • Connect Services
  • Go Live Services
  • Virtual Conference Services

Webcasting Services

Whether you want extra support on the day of the live webinar, help preparing presenters, or need ON24 to manage all aspects of the upcoming event, the ON24 Services team can help support and scale the program.

Webcast Elite Training Options Your Webcast Elite login comes with access to ON24 Training, our customer training portal, designed for licensed users to learn how to use the platform, but you can purchase a Premium Training package to add on a private Q&A session (up to 2-hours) to answer questions and review areas of the platform you'd like to learn in more detail.
Template Modernization  The Template Modernization Service provides up to 8 hours of expert-led support over three weeks to help you evaluate, optimize, and deploy up to 3 fully refreshed ON24 templates. Through a series of three workshops, you'll receive strategic guidance, best practices, and customized training to enhance engagement, scalability, and performance across your ON24 experiences. Must be used within 90 days of purchase.

Webcast Elite Launch Services

 This package aims to quickly set your team up for success using Webcast Elite and focuses on your process and webinar strategy.  Must be used within 90 days of purchase.   
Event Monitoring for Elite This package includes up to 2.5 hours of service for a single live Event ID on the live day and time of the webcast.  Previously called the Express Monitoring package.
Event Management for Elite This package includes up to 6 hours assistance with your webcast including registration page, emails, console setup, presenter orientation, live event monitoring, and archiving.  Previously called the Premium Event Management package

Town Hall Services Package

 This package includes up to 12 hours of assistance for a single Event ID including registration page, emails, console setup, presenter orientation, live event monitoring, and archiving.  Previously called the High Profile Event Management package.
Webinar Speaker Training & Dry Run Need help training your presenters for an upcoming webinar? This article provides details on how to submit a case for webinar speaker training.

Forums Services

If you only have Forums in your account and need assistance managing your event, these packages can help.

Forums Launch Services package

 This package aims to quickly set your team up for success using Forums and is focused on executing your first event.  Must be used within 90 days of purchase.  

Forums Event Management Package

 Includes up to 6 hours assistance with your Forums event, including registration page, emails, console setup, presenter orientation, and live event monitoring.

Forums Event Monitoring Package

Provides up to 2 hours of service for a single live Event ID on the live day and time of your event.

Captioning and Localization Services

Make your webinar more inclusive by adding live closed captioning for the hearing impaired and deaf or include translations in order to broaden your viewing audience.

Captioning Services This page outlines the differences in captioning services provided by ON24, including information on automated subtitles.
Live Captioning and Translated Subtitles This feature provides captions or translated subtitles of live webinars, done by a human hand, and can be translated live into over 100 languages, multiple languages at a time. This option is requested on a webinar-by-webinar basis.
On-Demand Subtitling and Transcription Request .VTT files to add captions and translated subtitles to your previously run or pre-recorded webcasts. This option is requested on a webinar-by-webinar basis and completed by a human hand.
Live Audio Interpretation Live Audio Translations and Live ASL or BSL (American or British Sign Language) are ON24’s Localization team.

Creative Services

Would you like a memorable environment?  The ON24 Creative Team is here to make your very own, unique, branded environment that will enhance your audience experience, bringing it to a higher level.

Creative Services Whether you need a 2D or 3D environment, a webinar, or a virtual show, our team is here to shape your ideas into reality.

Production Services

Recording & Production Management Packages

 Do you need help with recording or editing content? Are you considering an on-location video crew for your Hybrid Event? Our internal Audio, Video, and Production Management teams are ready to make your webinars shine.

Video Editing Packages

 Do you need a promotional video for your upcoming event?  Could your audience benefit from a 15-minute lobby video that helps them understand how the platform works?  If your event went differently than planned and you need to improve it, our video production experts can help.

Smart Plays

If ACE or a package of Smart Plays has been purchased, a toggle will be enabled on the Smart Plays per event. The toggle will remain grayed out if not a purchased option. See the Webcast Elite Overview Page.

AI-Generated Content Enable an option where a short-form blog post and longer form e-book covering key takeaways from the events are created using AI.

Note: Additional fees apply - contact your Account Manager for details.
Netline Promote your content using NetLine’s B2B content syndication network and acquire new leads using NetLine’s proprietary lead form technology.  Note that these services are provided by NetLine through a partnership with ON24. They can be ordered on the Overview page.  Click here for more details.   

Note: Pricing and fees for NetLine services are to be negotiated directly with NetLine.

Engagement Hub and Target Services

The Engagement Hub and Target Services package provides consultation, initial setup, and training/support.

Engagement Hub Launch Service

 This package aims to quickly set up your Engagement Hub and focuses on onboarding the team that will manage it. Must be used within 90 days of purchase.

Target Launch Service

 This package aims to quickly set up ON24 Target and focuses on onboarding the team that will manage it. Must be used within 90 days of purchase.

Enterprise Engagement Hub Launch Service

 Provides support for the implementation of Engagement Hub Custom Domain Service and requires the ON24 Enterprise Engagement Hub subscription. Must be used within 90 days of purchase.

Engagement Hub and Target Management Packages

 These packages are designed to provide consultation, initial setup, and training and support for Engagement Hubs and Target Content Experiences.

Connect Services

Integrations Services Services to help you get up and running with the Connect product that provides the integration and access to several leading CRM and marketing automation platforms.

Go Live Services

Go Live Express Launch Service

 This package aims to quickly set up Go Live and focuses on onboarding the team that will manage it.  Must be used within 90 days of purchase.

Go Live Event Monitoring Service

This package provides ON24 support for the event organizer on the live day.  

Live Day Event Monitoring Services package includes:

  • ON24 event specialist provides event support for event organizer on the live day
  • Live support provided via web conferencing, text chat and/or phone bridge
  • Up to 8 consecutive hours of service
  • Direct sponsor support and audience-facing support are not included

Virtual Conference Services

ON24 Virtual Conference Services ensure an exciting and innovative event experience for attendees all over the world.

Virtual Conference Manager - Contact Support How to contact ON24 Services for creative services, video production services, Webcast services, and other support needs.

articles.name: Webcast Elite Security Options
articles.title: Webcast Elite Security Options
articles.body:
Webcast Elite offers a range of security options for controlling audience access to webcasts, including Manual Approval, Domain Filtering, HTTP Referrer, Password, and limit Registration Capacity, all managed via the Registration tab.   Each security measure can be tailored to individual webcasts, ensuring only qualified audiences access the content. These settings must be configured before sharing the Audience URL and are applicable only to ON24 registration pages.

From the Registration tab, open Settings & Security to toggle on the security measures you want to use, then click on the security option to configure the settings.

 

  • Things to Know
  • Manual Approval of Registrants
  • Domain Filtering
  • HTTP Referrer
  • Password
  • Registration Capacity

Things to Know

  • The security features mentioned on this page only apply to customers using the ON24 registration page
    • If you use seamless registration, integration services, or another third party to host your registration page, security should be set up through your third-party provider.
  • For the security measures to be effective, they need to be set before distributing the Audience URL.

Manual Approval of Registrants

Manual Approval allows you to manually approve or deny each registrant for your event, ensuring only qualified and compliant audiences can access the content.

Notes: 

  • Once the Manual Approval setting is enabled, it cannot be disabled. 
  • Test toggled events will not work with Manual Approval due to the lack of access to 'Manage Registrants' on test events.
  • The option to Upload Registrants is not available when the Manual Approval setting is enabled.

When toggled ON, you will need to review Manage Users under Settings & Security on the Registration tab to approve or deny registrants.  Your choice will carry over when you copy an event. 

All registrants will be held in Pending until reviewed, but you can click Accept or Deny.  Hover over the options to see who made the change and when.  

Those that register for the event will see a message on the registration page, letting them know their registration is under review.

Once they've been accepted or denied, they'll receive an email based on your decision.  You can edit the emails in the Email Notifications section of the event's Overview tab.

  • Once the toggle is ON and registrations have been captured, this option cannot be toggled OFF
  • If registrants exist and the toggle is OFF, you can enable this option - your registrants will be auto-approved
    • If blocked registrants exist, they will remain denied, but will not receive an email.

Note: Reporting is currently not available for denied registrants.

Domain Filtering

Domain filtering allows you to block or allow registrants from a defined set of domains or email addresses.  This setting will persist when creating a copy of a webcast.   

If you want to block users from a specific domain, choose to filter by Block List and enter the domain.  Example: acme.com.

  • Specific emails can be added as exceptions to the blocked domains, allowing attendees to use those email addresses to register and attend the webinar.   
  • The maximum character limit is 3997 characters.

Alternatively, if you want to only allow users from a domain, you would choose to filter by Allow Only List.   You can add multiple domains using a comma to separate them.

Note: The Email field on the Registration tab must be set to Validate Email Address for Domain Filtering to check email domains at registration.

Create a custom message to display to registrants who are blocked by your security settings.  

If a customized message is not needed, a blocked domain will receive the default message when they register - This event is currently full and is not accepting any more registrations.  Thank you for your interest.

To add a custom message, choose the Custom Message radio button.

HTTP Referrer

HTTP Referrer allows you to require all registrants to access your webcast from a link on a webpage residing within the assigned HTTP referrer location.

For example, if you’re hosting an internal-only event and want attendees to access it exclusively through your home page, enter your home page URL in this field. Then, add the Audience link to your home page. Viewers will only be able to join the event from there—anyone attempting to use the Audience URL elsewhere will be blocked.

Password

Enabling a password will require your audience to enter the password to register and log in to the webcast - using this option adds a password field to both the registration and lobby pages.

You can change the label and order of the Password field in the Registration Setup section.

For registration page passwords, there is a maximum of 50 characters.  Alphanumeric and special characters are supported.

Note: Password protection applies to individual webcasts only. 

  • The password functionality will not carry over onto Event Series pages.  If a password is required for the Event Series, it must be set up on the Event Series page.  
  • If a webcast is added to an ON24 Engagement Hub, the event is no longer password protected since registration for an individual webinar is separate from registration for an ON24 Engagement Hub.

 

Registration Capacity

Note: If attendees are accessing the registration page from an ON24 Engagement Hub or ON24 Target page the Registration Capacity setting does not apply and will be bypassed. 

Use Registration Capacity to limit the number of registrants who can register for a webcast on a per-webcast basis.

Enter the desired capacity in the Registration Capacity field.  This setting will persist when creating a copy of a webcast.

If the registration capacity is reached before the live webinar, the registration page will be replaced with a message stating registration capacity has been reached and will not allow the user to register.

The registration capacity can be changed or removed at any time and ONLY applies to live, upcoming webinars.

Toggle on Display number of remaining seats on registration page if you'd like to encourage early registration by displaying the number of remaining seats available before reaching capacity.

Note: When planning and thinking about registration and attendance for your event, there may be limits outlined in your contract. Generally speaking, if more than 5,000 attendees are expected, submit a large event request or ask your Account Manager about your specific contract limits.

 

Event Series Pages and Registration Capacity

When using a Event Series page that includes a webcast with a registration capacity, we recommend toggling on Display number of remaining seats on registration page.  This will show the number of seats remaining on the series registration page, so registrants know if that particular event is full.

articles.name: Introduction to Media Manager
articles.title: Introduction to Media Manager
articles.body:
The Media Manager is a central media storage within the ON24 platform that allows you to upload and organize various pieces of content within the ON24 platform for use in Elite, Forums, and Go Live events, Engagement Hubs and Target content experiences.  Users can leverage the search functionality, sorting capabilities, and robust filtering options to easily find and share content. 

Content, such as externally created videos, images (thumbnails, backgrounds, logos, etc.) and documents can be uploaded to share with other users collaborating on shared campaigns or projects.  You can also find new content, such as single file archives of your webinars, full-length videos, as well as auto-generated post-webinar AI Generated Content (these are populated based on optional packages available in your account).

Access the Media Manager from the top of your account, under Platform Tools.

 

Media Manager Features

The Media Manager allows you to:

  • Add your content
    • Videos may be used as is and combined with other videos and images to create new assets
      • Videos must be a minimum of 3 seconds
    • PDFs are converted into an easy-to-read and interactive flipbook format
    • Images are converted into a three-second video clip for merging with videos or can be used as thumbnails throughout the Engagement Hub/Target pages
    • URLs can include blog posts, HTML white papers, and other web pages suitable to include with other media types in the Engagement Hub/Target page
    • Podcasts can be uploaded as MP3 files.  The MP3 file will be combined with a default thumbnail image and converted to an MP4 file for use in editing /merging functions, as well as publishing to the Engagement Hub/Target page.
  • Articles can be created in the Media Manager
  • AI Generated Content that is automatically created from the archive of your event
  • Update content names, thumbnails, and tags in the Flyout Panel
  • Publish Content to an Engagement Hub, Target pages, and popular social media sites (LinkedIn, YouTube, Facebook, and Twitter). 
    • Note: Before content can be used in these places, it must be uploaded to the Media Manager.
  • Edit and Merge videos and images
  • Add caption files to your videos

Content may be searched using the search feature, or sorted by clicking on the headers of any column.  Filter your Media Manager using the Filter panel on the side. 

 

How can I use Media Manager with Other ON24 Products?

In Webcast Elite and Forums events, Go Live virtual events

Share thumbnails, speaker bio images, pre-roll, demo, or promo videos with producers/event administrators for use in events.

 

With Engagement Hub and Target

Upload, manage (set thumbnails, edit titles, and set custom content tags), and publish your content (videos, documents, images, URLs, podcasts, and articles) into your Engagement Hubs and Target pages. Publish videos to the social media sites YouTube, LinkedIn, Twitter, and Facebook.

 

With AI-Generated Content

A few hours after your webinars end Media Manager will be populated automatically with AI-generated content for these events. These content assets can be published to Engagement Hub to engage your prospects and customers and organized like other Engagement Hub content into categories and playlists.

articles.name: Engagement Hub Subscription Emails
articles.title: Engagement Hub Subscription Emails
articles.body:
Subscribing to an Engagement Hub provides the option to notify viewers when updates and new content are added. Learn here how to build a community and leverage automated emails with the Subscribe feature.

Registered users can subscribe to your Engagement Hub and receive email updates when new content is added.   To ensure your audiences have a consistent experience with your brand and communication, you can customize these subscription email notifications.

To edit the content of the subscription notification email, navigate to the Overview section and scroll to the Configuration Options section to find Enable Subscribe to Engagement Hub.

Toggle the option on, then click the Preview/Edit to review and update the email text.

Note: Emails are sent out at 9:00 a.m. PST on Tuesdays (any content added and deleted before this will not be sent out).   


 

The following fields can be customized.  Click Save to save all changes.

  • From Field - the name of the sender 
  • Reply To Field - used for audience replies (only one email address can be used)
  • To Field - used to send a test email for previewing email content (up to five email addresses can be entered, separated by commas)
  • Subject - customize the subject line to drive more attention from your audience
  • Body - style and edit the messaging as needed
    • Use the token function to auto-populate key details 
      • Token examples are highlighted
      • Find tokens in the [T] icon



Note: If the Engagement Hub is unpublished, it is strongly recommended that the option Enable Subscribe to Engagement Hub be toggled off.  As new content is being added to the Engagement Hub while unpublished, subscribers will continue to receive new content emails.

You can find analytics in the Email Subscription report.  The Email Subscription Report shows the number of weekly subscription emails sent, opened, and clicked thru, and tracks the bounce rate in the system. There is a summary, a data table that includes details for every subscriber, and details of every email that has been sent.  Find more information on the report here.  

How will the email look to Subscribers?

If using the Legacy display options subscription emails will inherit the same banner and content thumbnail image as the Engagement Hub by default. 

If you are using the Hero Layout, a banner will not be automatically pulled into the email.  Instead, the text Engagement Hub Content Digest will appear. This text is not editable.

Engagement Hub Example
 

Subscription Email Example

 

Unsubscribing

Subscribers can unsubscribe from the emails by clicking the link at the bottom of the email, or by clicking the Unsubscribe button on the Engagement Hub.

articles.name: Forums Attendee User Guide
articles.title: Forums Attendee User Guide
articles.body:
This article outlines the attendee experience for Forum events, covering system requirements like supported OS and browsers, joining procedures, camera and mic setup, and troubleshooting. It explains event capacity limits, media player tools, mic permissions, screen sharing restrictions, and the Pass the Mic feature allowing attendees to request speaking rights during live events.

 

Attendee System Requirements

As a general best practice, close all unnecessary applications and clear your cache before logging into each event.  This will improve browser performance while ensuring all recent uploads and changes are visible.

 

Joining a Forum

Attendees will be able to enter the Audience Console 15 minutes before the live start time. When attendees first enter the Audience Console, they will first see a control panel to connect to the Forum prior to entering the event.

Find information on the attendee mobile experience.

Note: Google Chrome and Microsoft Edge are the suggested browsers for presenters and attendees in Forums.  Firefox is not supported. Also, users who do not have Safari 15 browser will need to update to the latest version of Safari, or use another supported browser in order to share their screen on their desktop.


 

Camera

  • If you have more than one webcam connected, choose your webcam source. After specifying a camera source, a preview is shown below.
  • Once your webcam is connected, click on the person icon in the webcam area to choose a virtual background or to blur the background.
  • Note: Backgrounds are only available for those joining via desktop at this time.
  • Use the dropdown to choose your camera, or set it to None if you want the camera off.

Mic

  • If you have more than one webcam connected, choose your microphone source. Click the microphone icon to toggle between being muted or unmuted upon entering the room.
  • Note: Presenters in Elite Studio have full control over when attendees' mics are enabled. If a microphone is currently disabled, the message "You can not unmute until a moderator gives you permission" will show below the microphone icon. Once the microphone has been enabled, attendees can unmute themselves to be heard during the event.

Input Volume

  • After specifying a microphone source, adjust the input volume.

Status  

  • If the status is Connected, you will be able to enter the event.
  • If the status is Disconnected, there is a problem with the connection to the event.

Troubleshooting Tips

  • Access a page of troubleshooting tips if the status is Disconnected.

Display My Name

  • When toggled ON, other participants in the event will see the first and last name the user entered during registration.
  • When toggled OFF, other participants in the event will not see the user’s first and last name.  Instead, they will see an anonymous label like “Participant 1,” “Participant 2,” etc. 
    • The exception to this is that moderators always have access to participants’ full names.  Moderators will see the anonymized label followed by the actual name, such as “Participant 1 (Mary Jones)”.

Join

  • Click the Join button to enter the event.

 

Event Capacity Reached

Forums events can accommodate up to 50 video participants and 200 additional listen-only participants, depending on how the event was set up.

If the maximum number of participants allowed on camera has been reached, attendees will see the message below to join in listen-only mode when joining.

For example, if the maximum of video participants was set to 50, the first 50 to join (any combination of presenters and attendees)​ get video seats. The 51st person will see the message below, and only be able to join in listen-only mode.

If the total capacity for the event has been reached, those trying to join will not be able to enter and will see the message below about capacity.

 

Engaging in the Event

Once the event has started, participants will be shown in a grid layout in the Media Player. The grid layout automatically adjusts as more people join.

If a person starts speaking they will be promoted to the main grid area and take the place of a person who has not spoken recently.

Those with badges next to their names are Presenters or Moderators for the event and logged in through Elite Studio.

Note: Lower Third Banners will not be available unless you turn on the Lock Aspect Ratio toggle in the Attributes.

 

Media Player Tools

The toolbar at the top of the Media Player allows you to:

  • Adjust your Volume 
  • See the list of participants by selecting People 
    • Note: This is the only tool available for those who joined the event in Listen-Only mode.
  • Raise hand
  • Adjust Camera and Microphone Settings
  • Request Mic (if enabled during event setup)
  • Minimize/maximize/full screen/click and drag the Media Player

 

Blocked Microphone

If an attendee has blocked access to their microphone during the event, their name will have a red icon next to it during the event.

Attendees with blocked microphones can hear the event, but not speak.

If the user with the blocked microphone attempts to unmute, they will see a message that informs them their camera and mic access are blocked. They must go into their browser settings to unblock.

 

Sharing Your Screen

If enabled, presenters can grant permission to attendees to share their screens.

All attendees have screen share initially disabled.

Attendees that have been given permission will see the screen share icon at the top of their screen.

Note: Screenshare is not supported on mobile devices or on Safari 15 browsers and older.

 

Pass the Mic Events

If this was enabled during the event setup, attendees can be promoted to use their microphone during the live event. The event will start with Presenters on camera, and attendees will have the opportunity to use their microphones upon request. 

Click on the Request Mic icon to alert presenters you would like to be promoted to use microphone,. Select your microphone and Send Request.

A banner will display to alert you have been successfully added to the queue. You can leave the queue at any time.

Once a presenter has accepted your request, the banner will display that your mic is now live.  You can disconnect your mic at any time.

articles.name: Marketing Cloud Account Engagement to ON24 Registration
articles.title: Marketing Cloud Account Engagement to ON24 Registration
articles.body:
Using Marketing Cloud Account Engagement (formerly known as Pardot) registration forms to post data to ON24.

 

Create a New Layout Template

This is a very specialized form that will need a slightly different layout template from your other forms and landing pages. The modification is minor and will not affect the look and feel of the form or landing page.

  • Navigate to Marketing > Forms > Layout Templates
  • Take your existing form or landing page layout and copy it
  • Note:  If your form resides on a MCAE landing page, make these edits to the landing page layout template. If the form is being iframed onto an external (non-MCAE) page, edit the form's layout template.

Find more information in MCAE's Forwarding data from a Account Engagement Form Post to a third-party article.

 

Edit the Layout Template's Form Tab

In the Form tab of the layout template, copy the following lines from rows 4-8 and move them to lines 1-5.

%%form-if-thank-you%%
    %%form-javascript-focus%%
    %%form-thank-you-content%%
    %%form-thank-you-code%%
%%form-end-if-thank-you%%

This should look like the following:

%%form-if-thank-you%% 
    %%form-javascript-focus%% 
    %%form-thank-you-content%% 
    %%form-thank-you-code%% 
%%form-end-if-thank-you%% 
<form accept-charset="UTF-8" method="post" action="%%form-action-url%%" class="form" id="pardot-form"> 
%%form-opening-general-content%% 
...

Save the layout template.

 

Edit Your Existing Form

Click on Marketing in the left navigation, then click on Forms in the resulting sub-navigation and edit your existing form. 

Go to Step 3: Look and Feel and select your Layout Template created from the previous step.

Note: If you are placing the form on a Pardot landing page, you will need to edit the landing page and select your new layout template from the steps above there instead of at the form level.  

Go to Step 4: Completion Actions and click on the Thank You Content tab, then click the HTML button in the WYSIWYG editor and add the following. The details you enter will vary based on the ON24 product. Click the relevant tab for additional information.

Note: Since the ON24 eventid and key are unique, each MCAE registration form supports only one event. 

  • Webinars
  • Engagement Hub
  • Target

Webinars

The eventid and key are unique per webinar and can be pulled from the Audience Link/URL found on the event's Overview page. 

  • NA Database Customers - use https://seamlessreg.on24.com in the highlighted form action
  • EU Database Customers - use https://seamlessreg.eu.on24.com in the highlighted form action 
If you're using Pardot Merge Language (PML)
<blockquote> 
<form action="https://seamlessreg.on24.com" method="post" name="hidden_form"> 
<input type="hidden" name="eventid" value="XXXXXX" /> 
<input type="hidden" name="key" value="XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX" /> 
<input type="hidden" name="email" value="%%email{html}%%" /> 
<input type="hidden" name="company" value="%%company{html}%%" /> 
<input type="hidden" name="firstname" value="%%first_name{html}%%" /> 
<input type="hidden" name="lastname" value="%%last_name{html}%%" /> 
<input type="hidden" name="jobtitle" value="%%job_title{html}%%" /> 
<input type="hidden" name="workphone" value="%%phone{html}%%" /> 
<input type="hidden" name="address1" value="%%address_one{html}%%" /> 
<input type="hidden" name="city" value="%%city{html}%%" /> 
<input type="hidden" name="state" value="%%state{html}%%" /> 
<input type="hidden" name="zip" value="%%zip{html}%%" /> 
</form> 
</blockquote>
If you're using Handlebars Merge Language (HML)
<blockquote>
<form action="https://seamlessreg.on24.com" method="post" name="hidden_form"> 
<input type="hidden" name="eventid" value="XXXXXX" /> 
<input type="hidden" name="key" value="XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX" /> 
<input type="hidden" name="email" value="{{Recipient.Email}}" /> 
<input type="hidden" name="company" value="{{Recipient.Company}}" /> 
<input type="hidden" name="firstname" value="{{Recipient.FirstName}}" /> 
<input type="hidden" name="lastname" value="{{Recipient.LastName}}" /> 
<input type="hidden" name="jobtitle" value="{{Recipient.Title}}" /> 
<input type="hidden" name="workphone" value="{{Recipient.Phone}}" /> 
<input type="hidden" name="address1" value="{{{Recipient.Street}}}" /> 
<input type="hidden" name="city" value="{{Recipient.City}}" /> 
<input type="hidden" name="state" value="{{Recipient.State}}" /> 
<input type="hidden" name="zip" value="{{Recipient.PostalCode}}" /> 
</form> 
</blockquote>
If you're using an iframe
If not using an iframe, skip this step.
If using the form in an iframe add target="_parent".
<blockquote> 
<form action="https://seamlessreg.on24.com" method="post" name="hidden_form" target="_parent"> 
... 
</form> 
</blockquote>
If you want to redirect to a specific page
If you want users to stay on the ON24 lobby page after registering, skip this step.
If you want to redirect to another page other than the ON24 lobby page after posting data to ON24, add the below input value replacing the highlighted 'URL' with your URL. 
<blockquote> 
<form action="https://seamlessreg.on24.com" method="post" name="hidden_form"> 
... 
<input type="hidden" name="lobby" value="URL" /> 
</form> 
</blockquote>  
Edit the ON24 Registration Form

We recommend you enable Login Only on the registration page of your ON24 events to disable the ON24 registration form and prevent contacts from using it.

Engagement Hub

The eventid and key are unique per Engagement Hub and can be pulled from the Seamless Registration URL. 

If you're using Pardot Merge Language (PML)
<blockquote> 
<form action="https://seamlessreg.on24.com" method="post" name="hidden_form"> 
<input type="hidden" name="eventid" value="XXXXXX" /> 
<input type="hidden" name="key" value="XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX" /> 
<input type="hidden" name="targeturl" value="" /> 
<input type="hidden" name="email" value="%%email{html}%%" /> 
<input type="hidden" name="company" value="%%company{html}%%" /> 
<input type="hidden" name="firstname" value="%%first_name{html}%%" /> 
<input type="hidden" name="lastname" value="%%last_name{html}%%" /> 
<input type="hidden" name="jobtitle" value="%%job_title{html}%%" /> 
<input type="hidden" name="workphone" value="%%phone{html}%%" /> 
<input type="hidden" name="address1" value="%%address_one{html}%%" /> 
<input type="hidden" name="city" value="%%city{html}%%" /> 
<input type="hidden" name="state" value="%%state{html}%%" /> 
<input type="hidden" name="zip" value="%%zip{html}%%" /> 
</form> 
</blockquote>
If you're using Handlebars Merge Language (HML)
<blockquote>
<form action="https://seamlessreg.on24.com" method="post" name="hidden_form">
<input type="hidden" name="eventid" value="XXXXXX" />
<input type="hidden" name="key" value="XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX" />
<input type="hidden" name="targeturl" value="" />
<input type="hidden" name="email" value="{{Recipient.Email}}" /> 
<input type="hidden" name="company" value="{{Recipient.Company}}" /> 
<input type="hidden" name="firstname" value="{{Recipient.FirstName}}" /> 
<input type="hidden" name="lastname" value="{{Recipient.LastName}}" /> 
<input type="hidden" name="jobtitle" value="{{Recipient.Title}}" /> 
<input type="hidden" name="workphone" value="{{Recipient.Phone}}" /> 
<input type="hidden" name="address1" value="{{Recipient.Street}}" /> 
<input type="hidden" name="city" value="{{Recipient.City}}" /> 
<input type="hidden" name="state" value="{Recipient.State}}" /> 
<input type="hidden" name="zip" value="{{Recipient.PostalCode}}" /> 
</form>
</blockquote>
If you're using an iframe
If not using an iframe, skip this step.
If using the form in an iframe add target="_parent".
<blockquote> 
<form action="https://seamlessreg.on24.com" method="post" name="hidden_form" target="_parent"> 
... 
</form> 
</blockquote>

Target

The eventId, key and experienceId are unique per Target and can be pulled from the Seamless Registration URL.

The targetUrl will be the URL for the specific Target/Experience page that registrant will redirect to after filling out the Pardot form.

If you're using Pardot Merge Language (PML)
<blockquote> 
<form action="https://seamlessreg.on24.com" method="post" name="hidden_form"> 
<input type="hidden" name="eventid" value="XXXXXX" /> 
<input type="hidden" name="key" value="XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX" /> 
<input type="hidden" name="experienceid" value="XXXXXX" /> 
<input type="hidden" name="targeturl" value="XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX" /> 
<input type="hidden" name="email" value="%%email{html}%%" /> 
<input type="hidden" name="company" value="%%company{html}%%" /> 
<input type="hidden" name="firstname" value="%%first_name{html}%%" /> 
<input type="hidden" name="lastname" value="%%last_name{html}%%" /> 
<input type="hidden" name="jobtitle" value="%%job_title{html}%%" /> 
<input type="hidden" name="workPhone" value="%%phone{html}%%" /> 
<input type="hidden" name="address1" value="%%address_one{html}%%" /> 
<input type="hidden" name="city" value="%%city{html}%%" /> 
<input type="hidden" name="state" value="%%state{html}%%" /> 
<input type="hidden" name="zip" value="%%zip{html}%%" /> 
</form> 
</blockquote>
If you're using Handlebars Merge Language (HML)
<blockquote> 
<form action="https://seamlessreg.on24.com" method="post" name="hidden_form"> 
<input type="hidden" name="eventid" value="XXXXXX" /> 
<input type="hidden" name="key" value="XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX" /> 
<input type="hidden" name="experienceid" value="XXXXXX" /> 
<input type="hidden" name="targeturl" value="XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX" /> 
<input type="hidden" name="email" value="{{Recipient.Email}}" />

</blockquote>
If you're using an iframe
If not using an iframe, skip this step.
If using the form in an iframe add target="_parent".
<blockquote> <form action="https://seamlessreg.on24.com" method="post" name="hidden_form" target="_parent"> 
... 
</form> 
</blockquote>

 

For All Products

Once the above is complete, click on the Thank You Code Tab and add the javascript. Then save your form.
Note: Be sure to follow the line breaks as shown or the code may not work properly.

<script type="text/javascript">// <![CDATA[
 document.hidden_form.submit();
// ]]>
</script>

articles.name: July 2025 Product Release Notes
articles.title: July 2025 Product Release Notes
articles.body:
We are excited to publish the next release for the ON24 suite of products.

 

Click on the product name to learn more about each feature's release.

  • Intelligent Engagement Platform
  • Webcast Elite
  • Forums
  • Engagement Hub
  • Target
  • Media Manager
  • Intelligence
  • Connect
  • Go Live

Intelligent Engagement Platform

Customize Key Moments with Platform Branding  

We’ve enhanced the workspace-level Branding section to include new settings for configuring your Key Moment videos. You can customize the intro slide, outro slide, and logos that appear in these videos. To access these settings, go to: Platform Tools > Branding > Video Settings.  

Note: Settings under Platform Tools > Video Builder > Brand no longer apply to Key Moment videos.  For clients currently using Key Moments, the Video Builder > Brand settings will be migrated to Branding > Video Settings to ensure your existing settings are preserved.

 

AI Generated Podcast Support on ON24 Nurture Pages

AI-generated podcasts can now be added to ON24 Nurture Pages, giving marketers another dynamic format to include in their automated content tracks. This update allows for the delivery of audio content as part of multi-asset nurture experiences, helping sustain engagement over time and reach audiences in moments when visual attention may be limited.  

Webcast Elite

Switch Templates in Intelligent Layouts

Console Builder now supports the use of the Template Manager and template dropdown within (Locked) Intelligent Layout events. Previously, these options were only available for Editable Layout events.

Find more information on Intelligent Layouts here.

 

Support for Post-Event Certification Updates

For workflows that require it, the Earn Certification tool now supports limited post-event updates.  

This flexibility can be useful in cases such as:  

  • Updating the downloadable certificate  
  • Adjusting certification criteria based on updated standards or requirements  
  • Updates made after an event concludes are processed during off-peak hours and may take up to 24 hours to fully reflect across Audience Consoles and reporting.  

Important: While this option offers added flexibility, post-event changes are rarely needed and can introduce inconsistencies, so this capability is disabled by default.  If your use case requires it, please contact the Support team to discuss whether it's right for you.

 

Edit Slide Notes

You can now edit or create new slide notes within Elite Studio.   You can also add notes to other storyboard items such as Polls, URLs and Video Clips. Slide notes only work for storyboard items that display in the slides window. Slide notes are not an option for video clips that display in the Media Player.

Note: If you replace the PowerPoint deck, any edits will be replaced with the notes in the PowerPoint deck.

 

Search Box added to Import Media within PMXD

When importing media from another event within PMXD, you can now use the search box to find the source event you need.

 

Manually Approve Registration

You now have the option to manually approve or deny each event registrant. All registrants will be held in a pending state until reviewed. Upon approval, they’ll automatically receive a confirmation email. If denied, they’ll be notified that they are not permitted to attend the event.

Find more information on webinar security settings here.

 

Global Reach with Multi-Language Support – Now GA

Your registration and lobby pages can now be translated into up to five languages, making it easier than ever to accommodate a global audience. The language shown to your audience members will be that of their browser language or the event language if the browser language cannot be detected. Contact your Account Team to learn how to add this service to your account.  Be sure to let our Platform Support team know the primary language your team uses.

Find the list of languages we support here - available languages are the same as the options available when choosing a language on the event's Overview page.

Note: This GA feature will require a contract update with applicable fees. This feature is available only for the new Registration Builder.

 

Miscellaneous Enhancements / Bug Fixes.

  • The ability to block an attendee from joining an event now works as expected for Simu-live events.
  • In the Earn Certification tool:
    • Placement of fields on the downloadable certificate will match what is defined in Console Builder.
    • You can now assign credit values with up to two decimal places. For example, 1.75 credits.

Forums

Only Producers Can pin and spotlight

Presenters will no longer be able to pin or spotlight, allowing them to have less buttons to distract them so they can focus on presenting.

Note: This only available when presenting a Forum from Elite Studio.

 

Informing Users of Status Changes

Users will now be informed when a moderator disables/enables or mutes their mic or camera, or pins/unpins them.

 

Updated Button Designs

Buttons have been updated to have more consistent feedback and modern look.

 

Improved Performance 

To maintain audio and interactivity functionality, users will have video quality automatically reduced or disabled as necessary when their computer resources are stressed. 

Engagement Hub

AI-Generated Podcast Publishing for Engagement Hub

A new capability in Media Manager now allows users to publish AI-generated podcasts directly to Engagement Hub. This update enables seamless distribution of podcast content alongside existing digital experiences, making it easier to diversify content formats and extend audience reach. Audiences can access and consume these AI-generated podcasts as part of their self-directed content journey, offering an additional way to engage with key messages in a format suited to multitasking or on-the-go listening. This integration supports greater content variety without increasing production overhead. 

Target

AI-Generated Podcast Support in ON24 Target

Users can now add AI-generated podcasts to ON24 Target experiences directly from Media Manager. This enhancement expands the types of content that can be curated into personalized content journeys, enabling marketers to include audio-based storytelling and thought leadership alongside webinars, videos, and documents. By incorporating podcasts into Target pages, organizations can deliver a richer, more flexible content experience tailored to how different audiences prefer to engage. 

Media Manager

Translated Article Collections for Multilingual Content Management

ON24 Media Manager now supports the automatic translation of AI-generated content from events into multiple languages, helping global teams streamline localization efforts. When enabled at the account level by Platform Support, translated content is generated for selected languages and organized into separate Article Collections by language. This enhancement simplifies the management and discovery of localized content within Media Manager, reducing manual translation work and supporting more efficient content repurposing across regions. 

Notes: 

  • This feature is available as an add-on. Speak with your Account Team for details on availability and pricing.
  • Translation for transcripts and articles generated from uploaded videos are not supported in this release.

 

AI-Generated FAQs Article for Webinars (BETA available for ACE clients)

A new feature now uses AI to generate a standalone FAQs article content piece for each webinar, presenting a curated list of likely or commonly asked questions based on the webinar topic and content. The AI-generated FAQs help anticipate viewer interests and provides clear, accessible context for on-demand audiences. It can be published alongside the webinar or incorporated into Engagement Hub, Target, or Nurture Pages, offering an additional way to engage audiences and reinforce key themes without requiring manual content creation.

 

Minor Miscellaneous Enhancements:

Content Type Prefixing for AI-Generated Assets

To improve clarity and consistency across AI-generated content, newly created assets will now include a content type prefix in their titles. This update applies to blogs, ebooks, and transcripts, with titles formatted as follows:

  • Blog: [Title]
  • Ebook: [Title]
  • Transcript: [Title]

This change helps users more easily identify and distinguish between different content types, particularly when downloading or managing assets at scale. The prefixing convention applies to all AI-generated assets created moving forward and does not retroactively modify existing titles.

Intelligence

Improved Performance for Analytics Dashboard

We are rolling out a new version of the Analytics Dashboard page, designed to improve loading speed, responsiveness, and overall usability. This update introduces a cleaner, more modern interface while preserving key functionality such as filters, data exports, and real-time updates. There will be a phased rollout across client accounts for this page, and this marks the first step in a broader effort to upgrade all Analytics pages to the new framework in future releases.

Note: This feature will be rolled out to customers in phases. See ON24 Platform Rollouts for details.

 

Send Performance Insights Emails Directly from WIR

A new feature in Webcast Intelligence Reports (WIR) allows users to send ON24 Performance Insights emails directly from a dedicated link. This email icon appears for completed live events and opens a modal where a single email address can be entered to trigger the message send. The email content is based solely on live event data and is stored for consistent delivery.  The link will only be available if someone is logged into the platform and viewing the report. If the necessary email properties are not enabled for event creators, presenters, producers, or the custom email list, the email link will not appear in WIR. At least one designated person must receive the email for it to be generated and available for resending in WIR.

Find more information in the Performance Insights article.

 

Option to Filter Domains from Top Companies in Insights Emails

The Performance Insights email now supports excluding specified email domains from the "Top 5 Companies" section to improve report relevance. This feature leverages the existing “Analytics: ABM - Excluded domains” property which the ON24 support can enable on a per-client request. When configured, it omits participants whose email domains match those listed for exclusion. If no domains are specified, standard behavior applies.

 

Updates to Text in the ON24 Performance Insights Email

Company names now appear without bold formatting to improve visual balance, and the Event ID is displayed as clickable text linked directly to the event’s audience URL, replacing the previous link icon.

 

Updated Smart Tips Logic for Console Top NAV Recommendation

The logic behind Smart Tips for the Console Top Navigation has been updated to ensure more accurate and meaningful recommendations. The uplift calculation now uses a quarterly average and compares Top Nav engagement to Menu Dock, excluding Dropdown configurations entirely. Smart Tips are shown only if Top Nav demonstrates the highest engagement and isn't already the most-used menu type. Displayed tips will now include the uplift percentage and Menu Dock usage rate. This update also refines data filters to include only clients with sufficient usage history, improving the quality and relevance of insights.

Connect

New Timeline Event Support Brings ON24 Engagement Data to All HubSpot Tiers

The ON24 HubSpot App now supports new Timeline Event types that reflect ON24 engagement activities such as polls, surveys, questions, and downloads. This update allows clients using non-Enterprise versions of HubSpot to access the same engagement data currently available through the ON24 custom object for HubSpot Enterprise customers.

 

Improved Validation for ON24 Veeva Network Integration

Enhancements to the ON24 Veeva Network integration improves data handling by validating registrant provider identifiers against both the "License Number" and "Healthcare Provider ID" fields. The integration now also accepts only records flagged as "ACT".

 

Integration Update Adds Support for Salesforce Boolean Fields

We've expanded the Salesforce CRM integration to support boolean fields. This update improves compatibility with Salesforce data models, allowing for mapping any of our custom fields and our Marketing Events field over to boolean field types when creating new Lead records.

 

New “istestevent” Field Added to Event API for Easier Test Event Filtering

The Event API now includes an “istestevent” field in its responses, enabling clients to easily distinguish test events from live ones. This addition supports better filtering and reporting, especially useful in development and QA environments.

 

Unified Registration API Update Adds Support for Virtual and In-Person Designations

The Unified Seamless Registration API will be updated to support passing "Virtual" or "In-Person" designations. This enhancement enables customers using external registration to specify the intended viewing type for registrants, supporting hybrid event formats.

Go Live

Improved Agenda Visibility

To make event navigation more intuitive, we’ve updated the agenda behavior so that any previously applied filters are automatically cleared when a user signs in. This ensures that attendees always start with a full view of the event agenda, reducing confusion and making it easier to discover sessions.

articles.name: Drift Chat Tool
articles.title: Drift Chat Tool
articles.body:
Learn how to integrate Drift Chat into your Webcast Elite experience.

Drift is a leader in the chatbot / conversational marketing space. Chatbots allow your audience to engage directly with your team, or deliver relevant content journeys and information so you can qualify more leads and accelerate buyer journeys.

To take advantage of this functionality, you must be a Drift customer.

 

Set Up

In the Console Builder tab of your event, add the Drift Chat tool from the Tools Manager.

Click the gear icon on the tool to open Configuration and enter the Drift code.

Each Drift account has a unique identifier. Find it within your Drift account at Settings > App Settings > Drift Widget > Install > JavaScript.

Within JavaScript, there is a unique alphanumeric ID located towards the bottom. Look for the drift.load ID, and enter it in the field (do not enter the apostrophes or parenthesis).

As a best practice, you will want to target a Drift playbook to your webinar. To do that, find your webinar event ID. You can find that on the Overview page, or take it from the Audience URL. 

Next, go into Drift > Playbooks. Click on the Playbook you want to use within your webinar. Click on Targeting > Display.

Create a rule to show the playbook when the URL includes the Event ID.

For example, if your webinar Event ID is 6282818, you would want to create the condition “URL includes 6282818”.

Note: The above describes targeting a playbook based on the Event ID. Drift provides a lot of targeting flexibility, so you could set up a rule to target based on something other than the event ID. Refer to this Targeting Conditions article from Drift for further information.

Publish the change after you add the targeting rule.

The end result is a Drift playbook within the audience console.

In this example, a “Schedule a Meeting” playbook has been added.

For additional information about setting up and using Drift playbooks, review the Drift Knowledge page.

 

Reporting

You can view reporting on the overall usage of the chat in the Webcast Intelligence Report. Click on Interactions, then click on the More tab.

The Chat Metrics (Drift) card includes:

  • Playbooks Clicked
  • Conversations Started
  • Meetings Booked (from inside the chat)

articles.name: Webcasting Services
articles.title: Webcasting Services
articles.body:
Whether you want extra support on the day of your webinar, help to prepare presenters, or need ON24 to manage all aspects of an upcoming event, the ON24 Customer Services team can help support and scale your program. This article describes the available webcasting services, including webcast monitoring and event management.

 

Things to Know

  • All of these packages are billable services - contact your Account Manager if you have questions on pricing
  • If you reschedule your event after submitting a request for a Webcasting Service, reply back to the email with the new date and time of the rescheduled event.

 

Available Packages

Event Monitoring for Elite

This package goes by both Express Monitoring Package and Event Monitoring for Elite, depending on when it was purchased.  It will be listed as the Express Monitoring Package in the Order Services form.

Need assistance on your live webinar? This service provides up to 2.5 hours of service for a single live webinar on the live date/time.

  • 30 minutes before the live webinar starts time, an ON24 representative will provide pre-call services
  • 120 minutes of live Webinar monitoring services

An ON24 representative can:

  • Provide pre-call services, including presenter orientation to the Elite Studio tool
  • Guidance and testing of presenter connection types before Webinar start time
  • Escalation to ON24's technical support teams for assistance with troubleshooting
  • Monitor ON24 presenter Q&A tool to assist attendees with any technical issues
  • Monitor ON24 presenter tool team chat to assist presenters with any technical issues, questions, or concerns they may have during the live webinar.
  • Monitoring of the audience console for stream quality
Event Management for Elite

This package goes by both Premium Event Management Package and Event Management for Elite, depending on when it was purchased.  It will be listed as the Premium Event Management Package in the Order Services form.

Need assistance with managing your event from start to finish? This service provides up to 6 hours of service for a single Webinar ID.

An ON24 representative may provide:

  • Webinar kick-off session and check-in session
  • Webinar setup and configuration using features available in the customer's account on the ON24 platform
  • Standard registration page setup including branded graphic uploads, descriptive text, and standard registration field configuration
  • Lobby page setup includes branded graphic uploads, descriptive text, standard help page link, and standard test your system link.
  • Audience console setup - includes branded graphic uploads, choice of background color, and engagement tool configuration as available in the customer's account.
  • Webinar email(s) setup and activation
  • ON24 presenter tool configuration
  • Scheduling of webinar speaker training and dry run before live Webinar date/time (single session up to 1.5 hours)
  • Scheduling of live Webinar monitoring (up to 120 minutes)
  • Provide guidance and testing of presenter connection types prior to Webinar start time
  • Provide escalation to ON24's technical support teams for assistance with troubleshooting
Town Hall Services Package
