This package goes by both High Profile Event Management Package and Town Hall Services Package, depending on when it was purchased.  It will be listed as the High Profile Event Management Package in the Order Services form.

Need assistance managing your high-profile event from start to finish?  This service provides up to 12 hours of service for a single Webinar ID.

  • Webinar kick-off session and check-in session (30 minutes each)
  • Webinar setup and configuration using features available in the customer's account
  • Standard registration page setup including branded graphic uploads, descriptive text, and standard registration field configuration
  • Lobby page setup including branded graphic uploads, descriptive text, standard help page link, and standard test your system link
  • Audience console setup including branded graphic uploads, choice of background color, and engagement tool configuration using features  available in the customer's account
  • Webinar email(s) setup and activation
  • ON24 presenter tool configuration
  • Scheduling of up to 3 webinar speaker training sessions and dry runs before live webinar date/time (up to 1 hour each)
  • Scheduling of live webinar monitoring (up to 4 continuous hours)
  • Providing guidance and testing of presenter connection types before webinar start time
  • Providing escalation to ON24's technical support teams for assistance with troubleshooting
Webinar Speaker Training and Dry Run

This package is ideal for those who need assistance training their speakers for an upcoming webinar and will walk your presenter(s) through the presenter tool interface.    

Note: For this service, all requests must be submitted at least 72 business hours prior to the live event start time. 

A single session (up to 1.5 hours) is provided for a single webinar before the live date/time.

  • Provide presenter orientation with the ON24 presentation tool
  • Guidance and testing of presenter connection types against ON24 connection standards
  • Guidance and testing of ON24 presenter tool and screen share application
  • Escalation to ON24's technical support teams for assistance with troubleshooting
  • Assistance while the presenter team executes a dry run of the webinar
Forums Event Monitoring Package

To order this package, choose the Event Monitoring Package in the Order Services form.  Once someone from the services team has reached out to you, let them know this for a Forums event.

This package provides event monitoring on your event's live day, for up to 2 consecutive hours of service.  

  • Live support provided via web conferencing, text chat and/or phone bridge
  • Direct attendee support not included
Forums Event Management Package
To order this package, choose the Premium Event Management Package in the Order Services form.  Once someone from the Services team has reached out to you, let them know this is for a Forums event.
If you only have Forums in your account and need assistance managing your event from start to finish, this package includes up to 6 hours of service provided for a single Forum event.
A ON24 representative may provide:
  • Kick off and check-in session (30 mins each)
  • Forum set up and configuration using features available in the customer's account
  • Standard registration page setup including branded graphic uploads, descriptive text, and standard registration field configuration
  • Lobby page setup includes branded graphic uploads, descriptive text, standard help page link, standard test your system link
  • Audience console setup includes branded graphic uploads, choice of background color, and engagement tool configuration as available in Client account
  • Forum email(s) setup and activation
  • ON24 presenter tool configuration
  • Scheduling of Forum speaker training and dry run prior to live Forum Event (single session up to 1.5 hours)
  • Includes up to 1 live ON24 Forums Monitoring Package
  • Provide guidance and testing of Presenter connection types prior to live Forum Event
  • Provide escalation to ON24’s technical support teams for assistance with troubleshooting
Single File Archives

Single File Archives are .mp4 files that are separate from the archived on-demand version of your event.  Single File Archives of all webinars may be generated at no fee via your account for up to 30 days after the webinar start date.

If you need a SFA more than 30 days after the start date of the event, submit a request to the Video Editing team using the Video Editing option listed below. 

Note: This is a billable request.

Looking for Engagement Hub or Target Management packages?  Click here.

 

How To Order

From the Overview page of the event you would like to use a service on, click on the Order Services button.

Choose the desired service, enter in additional details about your request, add additional emails to be CC'd if desired, and select Place Order. 

Once the order is placed a confirmation email will be sent notifying you the case has been submitted successfully. A second email notification will be sent once someone from the Services team has been assigned to your request, to discuss specifics.

articles.name: Engagement Hub Custom Domain
articles.title: Engagement Hub Custom Domain
articles.body:
The custom domain feature allows ON24 customers to align their Engagement Hub URL to their corporate domain. Customers may utilize this feature for branding, SEO, and/or technical purposes.

Note: Custom Domain is available as part of the newest version of the Engagement Hub (Engagement Hub Enterprise).  If you are interested in using this feature, please contact your Account Manager.  Customers can also buy this feature as an add-on. Please allow for setup time as custom domains require multiple steps on the ON24 and customer side as well as testing. 

Setup will require your IT/NetOps teams to work with ON24 during the configuration process, which involves creating custom URLs (i.e., hub.customername.com) and acquiring an SSL certificate. Each Engagement Hub will need to have a separate subdomain, and some scenarios require customers also configure a custom domain for Elite Webinars. 

Customers should match their brand styling from their website to their Engagement Hub. ​ Once the custom domain setup is complete, the Engagement Hub will appear to be a natural extension of – and SEO will be properly attributed to – a customer’s website.  

Customers should also enable custom domain for their Elite webinars, which will use the same custom domain for in the Audience URL for webinars. The auto-registration option enables audiences of the event to access the Engagement Hub (which uses the custom domain) listed in the Take Action tool without re-registration.

Note:  If Elite webinars are not enabled with the same custom domain, the auto-registration feature in the Take Action engagement tool will not work. They will have to register if they try to access that Engagement hub.

 

 

Note: Once a custom domain is configured for Engagement Hub, the URLs for any Target pages associated with the Engagement Hub will also display the custom domain.

 

Custom Domain Process - Initial Setup

Step (Party)

Step (Description)

0 (Client & ON24) 

Reach out to your CSM or Solutions Consultant to express your interest in setting up a custom domain. 

1 (Client)​ 

Acquire a subdomain for products to be configured: one for each Engagement Hub URL (e.g. hub.customername.com ) and one for Elite Webinars (e.g. events.customername.com).

2 (Client) 

Submit a case to Platform Support to request the creation of a certificate for the domain to be used (e.g.hub.customername.com ). 


Copy your CSM and Solutions Consultant (if applicable) on the case and include the information below to support the desired type of SSL Certificate. ON24 will generate the certificate on your behalf.   

  • Common name  
    • (If SAN SSL) List all subdomains to be covered 
    • (If single-domain SSL) List the domain name to be covered 
  • Division (e.g. NetOps, Marketing)​ 
  • Organization     ​ 
  • Locality (the city where the business resides)​ 
  • State or Province            ​ 
  • Country​ 
  • E-mail address 

3 (Client)​* 

Client submits a site map to search engines (Google search console).​  

Please note, to submit the site map, you must complete the site verification with the Domain name provider option: https://support.google.com/webmasters/answer/9008080?hl=en  

Engagement Hub site maps can be copied from the “Overview” tab within the platform.

4 (Client)​ 

Add DNS CNAME entries that would point Engagement Hub domains to ON24 Virtual Servers. 

Examples: 
NA datacenter - 
hub.customername.com  >> gatewaywhitelabel.on24.com 
events.customername.com  >> eventwhitelabel.on24.com 

EU datacenter - 
hub.customername.com  >> gatewaywhitelabel.eu.on24.com 
events.customername.com  >> eventwhitelabel.eu.on24.com 

5 (ON24)​ 

ON24 installs an SSL certificate on the custom domains requested.  

6 (ON24)​ 

ON24 configures all Engagement Hubs & Target instances in the ON24 platform to use the desired custom domain (e.g. hub.customername.com​ ). 

7 (N/A)

With this setup in place, Engagement Hub & Target URLs will be displayed and published to end-users using the custom domain, and you will be able to use the full breadth of the features available in Engagement Hub and Target.  

Example of final custom URL:  
hub.customername.com/wcc/eh/3134332/knowledge-hub-name 

Please note that this part of the URL (including Hub ID and name) "/wcc/eh/3134332/knowledge-hub-name" is required.  

8
(Client)

Client must inform IT/Security teams to EXCLUDE each of the configured Custom Domains from any security scanning, vulnerability testing, bug bounties, web crawling, or other similar activities.   

Failing to create these exclusions could result in ToS violations and potential service disruptions to Client. 

9
(Client & ON24)*

Enable “Allow search engines to crawl/index” via toggle on your Engagement Hub Overview tab. With this, ON24 will dynamically construct and maintain sitemap_<customer-domain>.xml of all content across Engagement Hubs & Target pages, and the sitemap can be copied from the Overview tab.

10 (ON24)​* 

ON24 handles robots.txt file mapping.  

11 (N/A)​* 

Search engines will crawl site map content pages and index them for search. Please note that search results ranking depends on various factors and is not something that ON24 can control. ​​ 

*Steps 3, 9-11 are specific to using custom domain for SEO and do not necessarily have to be done if the Engagement Hub won’t be indexed by search engines. 
  

Custom Domain Process - Renewal (Annual)

Note: You may need to renew your domain name. ON24 automated the SSL renewal.

articles.name: Monitoring Simu-live & Sim-2-Live Events
articles.title: Monitoring Simu-live & Sim-2-Live Events
articles.body:
This article contains guidelines for login, how to connect, and how to monitor the event and answer questions in real-time for Simu-live and Sim-2-Live. Producers, Presenters, and Q&A moderators have different functionality in the interface to control presentation elements and interact with the audience.

  • Things to Know
  • Monitor Simu-live Events
  • Monitor Sim-2-Live Events
  • Audience View
  • Watch the Video

Things to Know

  • All Simu-live and Sim-2-live webcasts must be recorded and published at least two before the scheduled start time. Two hours before the set start time, the system will switch to live mode, and no changes can be made during this time, including changing the start date/time, recording audio, rearranging slides, choosing the Rollover to Live option, etc.
  • On the live day, Simu-live and Sim-2-live webcasts will start automatically at the set start date and time. The presenting team may log in 45 minutes before the webinar is scheduled to chat amongst themselves for a pre-call, but there will be no ability to connect earlier than 45 minutes ahead of the scheduled start time.
  • For the live portion of the Simu-live, standard Presenter/Producer login limits and Q&A limits apply: Up to 10 Presenter/Producers and up to 12 Q&A moderators.
  • When using a Presenter Media file:
    • The Media Player will auto-expand by default.  If you wish to override these settings, the auto-expand option must be unchecked in the Media Player configuration.
    • All Producers and Presenters will need to connect to Elite Studio using their webcam or PC Microphone for the pre-call.
      • The dial-in option for speakers will not be available until the webinar rolls over to live.
    • When using a Presenter Media file and video clips together on a single webinar, the video clips will not playback on mobile devices. Only the Presenter Media video file will play.  This is expected behavior at this time.

Monitoring Simu-live Events

Standard Simu-live webcasts are entirely pre-recorded and will play to a live audience at your chosen date and time. While the presentation plays to the live audience, you may have Q&A moderators log in to monitor the event and answer questions in real-time via text using the Q&A tool. Up to 12 people can log in at a time as Q&A Moderators.

Moderators will use the Present URL to log in and have full access to the Q&A, Team Chat, and Attendee Chat  (if available on the Audience Console) and will be able to listen and watch the webcast in the live interface.

The webcast will start automatically at the chosen start time. A Simu-Live in Progress message will appear in the top corner of the live interface for your Q&A moderators so they know when the webcast has begun.

There is no dial-in number or phone bridge for the Q&A moderators while the webinar is live, but they can communicate using the Team Chat. Team Chat messages are not visible to the audience but are captured in the webinar reports.

The webcast will end automatically after the recorded portion has played out. The event archive will be available a few hours later for your audience to watch on-demand.

If you wish for the audience console to remain open after the presentation is complete to allow attendees additional time to submit questions or download certificates, consider extending the event by recording extra silence on the last slide so the console will remain open.

Monitoring Sim-2-Live Events

Sim-2-live webcasts are Simu-live webcasts with the Rollover to Live box checked, providing live time at the end of the recording to address questions. 

Sim-2-live webcasts start automatically at the chosen start time.  After the pre-recorded portion plays out, the system will automatically switch to a live webcast.

While the pre-recorded portion plays to the live audience, Q&A will be text only, but your presenters can address questions from the audience both verbally and via text once the rollover to live occurs.

Elite Studio for Sim-2-Live events works the same as Elite Studio for fully live events.  Check out the Elite Studio User Guide for more details on the using the interface.

 

Logging in and Connecting

Your presenting team will be able to log in and connect to the webcast starting 45 minutes before the set start time, using the Present URL. However, we recommend those with a speaking role log in at least 30 minutes beforehand so they have plenty of time to set up and test their connection.

A maximum of 10 users (mix of Producer/Presenter) may connect to Elite Studio simultaneously. The Q&A Moderator role does not count towards this maximum.

Note:Every Sim-2-live event must have at least one person log in as a Producer to stop the event after the live portion is complete.

Producers and Presenters will connect to Elite Studio just like they would when connecting to a fully live webinar. The connection options available are:  

  • Webcam
  • PC Microphone Only
  • Phone

Find more information on how to connect to Elite Studio.

 

Switch to Live Mode

A Simu-live message will show at the top of the interface once the webinar has begun. The webcast will automatically stream to your live audience based on the chosen date and time.

A countdown will appear two minutes before the pre-recorded portion ends, alerting presenters to get ready.

At the end of the countdown, the interface will automatically go into full live webcast mode. There will also be an automated message that plays for your presenting team that says, "You are now in the live stream," followed by a tone. The audience will not hear the automated message.

Important: The audio message comes after the platform switches to live mode. Producers and Presenters should wait until they hear the message before speaking or pushing slides. This will not align exactly with the live start countdown. A 10-15 second delay is normal and any speaking before the audio alert will not be broadcast. Please wait for the alert to begin speaking.

 

Stopping the Webcast - Producers Only

When the event is over, the Producer will need to press the End Event button. After pressing, a confirmation window will appear. Clicking End Webcast from this point will disconnect the presenting team from the live audience, and the webcast will end. 

Note: If the webcast is accidentally stopped, it cannot be restarted.

The event archive will be available a few hours later for your audience to watch on-demand.



 

Audience View

If you recorded your presentation in Elite Studio, any videos added to the Storyboard will play in the Slides tool, not the Media Player.  

By default, Google Chrome will automatically mute the video within the Slide Area.  Attendees using Chrome will need to unmute the video using the speaker icon in the Slides tool.    

Watch the Video

The UI shown in the video may be different than yours, but the functionality will be the same.

articles.name: Troubleshooting Connecting to a Live Event
articles.title: Troubleshooting Connecting to a Live Event
articles.body:
This article provides troubleshooting tips for using Elite Studio for live webcasts, including browser recommendations (Chrome, Edge), network advice, and device access permissions. It addresses common issues like webcam/mic access, screen sharing problems, and known bugs.

 

Important Notes

  • Our Video Presenter Bridge leverages WebRTC technologies and standards.  To help determine whether any problems may be caused by your network environment or the hardware, please use this WebRTC demo.
  • Google Chrome and Microsoft Edge are the suggested browsers for presenters. Firefox is supported, however Edge and Chrome may have a more consistent experience.
  • Experiences with Breakout Rooms do not support the use of Firefox. Google Chrome and Microsoft Edge are the suggested browsers.

 

Allowing the Browser to Access Your Camera and/or Microphone is required.

Your browser may ask you to grant ON24 access to your microphone and camera. 

Be sure to click Allow before closing the window.  

Once connected you will notice indicators that your microphone and webcam are being used by your browser.

Note: While virtual webcams are not blocked, they are not a part of our supported test matrix.  ON24 cannot guarantee or predict outcomes.  If issues arise, please be prepared to switch to supported physical webcams.

If you are having difficulties with this step please see these resources on how to grant web browser access to your mic and camera:
  • Microsoft
  • Apple

 

Common Webcast Issue Resolutions

  1. A wired connection is encouraged. Strong Wi-Fi connections are acceptable if they are stable. Unstable Wi-Fi due to congestion or other causes can result in connectivity drops that adversely impact a live event.
  2. Make sure ON24 has access to your microphone and camera within the browser, and ensure the browser tab is unmuted (right-click the browser tab unmute).
  3. Try a different browser (Edge and Chrome are suggested). Experiences with breakout rooms, forums, and NextGen Live Video do not support the use of Firefox.
  4. Elite Studio is supported on iPad (version 16+) for Live Video, Simu-live, Sim-2-Live, and Broadcast Video present types.
  5. Clear your browser cache.
  6. Close all other applications and browser tabs. This is especially true of collaboration apps (Zoom, WebEx, Teams). It is okay to use them for audio and chat, but do not attempt to run video, webcam, or screen share in a collaboration app while running the ON24 presenter interface. It will use all of the available CPU resources.
  7. Log off VPN (Virtual Private Network).
  8. Use an Incognito Window.  
  9. Restart your computer (if there is time to reboot).
Note: Elite Studio will time out after two hours of inactivity.

 

Frequently Asked Questions

Note: A refresh of the browser should be completed any time the camera or mic is manually connected or disconnected. Refreshing while trouble shooting can help ensure all changes are seen and that any connection is re-started.
 

What if the presenter doesn't see the webcam or microphone in the dropdown when connecting?

Try another browser.

  • Unplug and reconnect the device(s) to the USB port. Refresh after reconnecting to help trigger detection of the device.
  • Reboot the computer to ensure there are no software conflicts competing for the same device (webcam or PC microphone).

What if the Audience View window displays a spinning wheel or does not display shared content?

  • If you don't need to see the content to present, open a preview mode window to verify that something is displaying.
  • The usual cause for this is that the network is blocking access to wss://h5live.nanocosmos.de.
  • If Audience View is necessary given your use case, submit a high priority case to ON24 Platform Support, and our team will work with your network personnel to address the issue.

What if the presenter console cannot access webcam or screen share?

  • Refresh by pressing F5 on your keyboard. If that doesn't resolve the issue, the most likely cause is content filters are blocking either access to the TURN server or access to the ON24 servers.
    • Refreshing the page will not end the webinar.
    • Note: When refreshing and reconnecting, the microphone and camera are automatically ON when the new connection loads.
  • Submit a case to ON24 Platform Support, and our team will work with your network personnel to address the issue.

What if the webcam is grayed out or frozen in the audience console?

  • Likely caused by insufficient audience bandwidth.
  • Can happen when connected to an unstable Wi-Fi internet connection.
  • Connect to webcam using a stable, high-speed internet connection.

What if the screen share will not display in the Audience View window or preview?

  • Confirm each presenter is indeed connected to the presenter console via dial-in, webcam, or PC microphone.
  • Confirm the presenter has established a connection. The presenter's name should be displayed in the Presenters List.
  • Use a strong and stable internet connection.
  • If using Firefox, confirm the ON24 Screen Sharing Extension has been added to the browser.
  • Close all other applications (including the ones in your system tray), refresh, and clear your cache.
  • Try a different browser (for example, try Firefox instead of Chrome).
  • Restart your computer (if there is time to reboot).
  • Try a different PC/machine if one is available (screen share can be CPU intensive, especially for older machines).

What if the screen share keeps asking to download after installation (Firefox)?

  • Try resetting the browser to factory settings.
  • Uninstall the plugin and reinstall.

What if I need webcam troubleshooting?

  • Make sure the presenter is using Chrome, Firefox, or Edge (Edge and Chrome are suggested). Experiences with breakout rooms, forums, and NextGen Live Video do not support the use of Firefox.
  • A wired connection is encouraged. Strong Wi-Fi connections are acceptable if they are stable. Unstable Wi-Fi due to congestion or other causes can result in connectivity drops that adversely impact connecting to and using a webcam during a live event.
  • Refresh presenter console (F5) and try to reconnect the webcam.
    • Refreshing the page will not end the webinar.
    • Note: When refreshing and reconnecting, the microphone and camera are automatically ON when the new connection loads.
  • Confirm the browser isn't blocking the device from being accessed.
  • Try a different browser (Edge and Chrome are suggested). Experiences with breakout rooms, forums, and NextGen Live Video do not support the use of Firefox.
  • Clear your cache.
  • Close all unused applications, especially those that may have access to your webcam (Microsoft Teams, etc.).
  • Reconnect the device via the USB and refresh.
  • Reboot the computer if time permits.
  • If the issue persists after troubleshooting, it is usually related to network configurations such as VPN or firewalls. Try connecting off of a VPN. If this doesn't resolve the issue, please submit a case to Platform Support so our team can work with your internal IT department to find a resolution.

What if presenters cannot see or hear a single presenter?

  • Refresh the presenter console using the F5 key.
    • Refreshing the page will not end the webinar.
    • Note: When refreshing and reconnecting, the microphone and camera are automatically ON when the new connection loads.
  • Try another browser.

What if no audio is heard when viewing the preview link?

  • Disconnect all phone and webcam connections and then reconnect.
  • Check to see if presenters are listed in the Presenter List in the Connections Panel.
  • If disconnecting and reconnecting does not solve the issue, call the Live Event Emergency Line.

 

Known Issues

Single webcam presenter does not display on the left side of media player in Elite Studio

For a Live Video event/present type with only one presenter connected to webcam in Elite Studio, the presenter will not show on the return view (left side) of the media player. A black window is displayed instead.

There is no impact to the Audience Console, and the presenter can still see themselves on the confidence monitor view (right side).

We are currently actively working to get the single presenter view to be the same on both sides.


Screen share issue with iOS/Mac and Google Chrome browser

Presenters running Chrome Version 99.0.4844.74 on iOS/Mac (Big Sur 11.6 and other versions) may experience an inability to screen share during Live Video and Audio with Screen Share (Video Presenter Bridge) webinars (and in breakout rooms).

To resolve this issue:

  1. Open System Preferences (users may hit CMD + Space and type "pref").
  2. Open "Security & Privacy".
  3. On the left, scroll down to "Screen Recording" and click it.
  4. Users may need to click the lock on the bottom left to make changes and enter their credentials.
  5. Uncheck Chrome on the right.
  6. On the popup, click "Quit & Reopen".
  7. Re-check the Chrome box again.
  8. Click "Quit & Reopen".
     

 

Remember: Don't Panic, It's Just a Webcast

Don't sweat the unexpected. In live television production, for example, mishaps sometimes happen. Reporters and news anchors are used to this and just casually pass to another person if there are technical difficulties.

Your live webcasts are no different. Accept the fact that mistakes and miscues are possible. Handle it like a pro and be ready to roll with it if they happen. Don't call attention to the problem. Just move on with your webcast and what has been planned.

articles.name: May 2025 Product Release Notes
articles.title: May 2025 Product Release Notes
articles.body:
We are excited to publish the next release for the ON24 suite of products.

The release will take place over two days:

  • EU Data Center Release will be May 29, 2025
  • NA Data Center Release will be May 30, 2025

Note: Features are subject to change. Contact your account manager with questions about features included in your subscription. 

 

Click on the product name to learn more about each feature's release.

  • Webcast Elite
  • Forums
  • Media Manager
  • Intelligence
  • Connect
  • Go Live

Webcast Elite

Customize Font Sizes on Downloadable Certificates

We have enhanced the Earn Certification tool to give you greater control over the appearance of your downloadable certificates. You can now adjust font sizes for each text field, ensuring readability and alignment with your certificate design.

These improvements make it easier to produce clean, professional certificates that align with your layout requirements.

 

Move Tools on Console More Easily

For consoles using the Embedded chrome shown on hover style, attendees can now move a tool by clicking and dragging anywhere in the header chrome area - no need to grab the specific Move icon. This applies only to tools configured to allow attendee repositioning.

 

French Canadian Support for Live Captioning

Automated captions are now available for live events presented in French Canadian. As the presenter speaks, captions in French Canadian can be displayed for the audience.

Note: Auto-live captioning is available as an add-on feature.

 

Ability to Reorder Breakout Rooms

Within Console Builder > Breakout Room > Configuration, you can now reorder rooms when multiple breakout rooms are configured. This gives you greater control over how rooms are presented to attendees. 

Find more information in our article Configuring the Breakout Rooms Tool.

 

Advanced Notice: 4:3 Media Player Aspect Ratio Deprecation

Effective June 1, 2025 , ON24 will no longer support the 4:3 aspect ratio for the Media Player in new events. From that date forward, 16:9 will be the only supported aspect ratio for newly created events.

Note: If you play a 4:3 video in a 16:9 Media Player, pillarboxing will occur - black bars will appear on the left and right sides to maintain the video’s original proportions.

Exception: Existing events, including future-scheduled events, that are already configured with a 4:3 Media Player will be allowed to remain at 4:3.

However, if you:

  • Copy an event where the original Media Player is 4:3, or
  • Apply a console template that uses a 4:3 Media Player

The target event’s Media Player will automatically be set to 16:9, with no option to revert to 4:3.

  • In this case, the Media Player width will remain the same, but the height will be reduced to fit the 16:9 ratio.
  • If tools are positioned below the Media Player, you may notice increased vertical spacing between them due to the height reduction.

To ensure a smooth transition:

  • If you copy events, update the source event's Media Player to 16:9 and adjust the layout as needed.
  • If you use a console template with a 4:3 Media Player, create a new version of the template using a 16:9 Media Player and adjust the layout accordingly.

To check your Media Player’s aspect ratio, go to Console Builder > Media Player > Configuration > Media Player. 

Find more information on setting up the Media Player. 

 

Take Full Control of Your Registration Page Translations - BETA

Managing multi-language registration pages just got easier! You can now edit translated pages directly, just like the primary language version. Plus, you have the flexibility to lock translations to prevent automatic syncing with changes to the primary page, unlock them to re-enable syncing, and revert any or all translated pages back to ON24’s default translations.

This update gives event organizers full flexibility over multi-language registration pages, ensuring accuracy, brand consistency, and control over how content is localized. You can confidently edit translations manually, prevent unintended overwrites from primary language changes, and restore ON24’s defaults at any time if needed.

Check out the Language Options tab of the Registration Design and Setup article for more details.

 

Faster Registration Setup with Copy/Paste Multi-Option Fields

Setting up registration forms just got easier! You can now copy/paste a list of options directly into multi-option fields like checkboxes and dropdowns using the new Add As List button. No more manually entering each option—simply paste, edit, and organize your list in seconds!

This update streamlines multi-option registration fields, reducing setup time and ensuring consistency across registration forms. With a faster, more efficient way to populate dropdowns and checkboxes, event organizers can focus on delivering seamless registration experiences.

Find more information on Webcast Elite Registration Fields.

 

Ability to Turn Off Other Presenters’ Webcams

Producers and presenters in a live webinar can now turn off cameras of other producers and presenters by clicking the camera icon next to the presenter’s name in the Presenters list in Elite Studio.

Check out the Other Tools tab of the Elite Studio User Guide for more information.

 

Elite Studio Only Displays Widgets Shown to the Audience

Elite Studio now only displays Q&A and Audience Chat windows if those tools are included in the audience console and shows the Reactions toggle only when Reactions are enabled for attendees.

 

Miscellaneous Enhancements / Bug Fixes.

  • (Locked) Intelligent Layout events now adapt more effectively to available vertical screen space, reducing reliance on scrollbars to display full content.
  • When pushing a question to the Slide tool from Elite Studio, questions containing the "&" symbol were incorrectly rendered as "&amp" in the Slide tool. Similarly, replies to questions in the Ask Question tool displayed the "&" symbol incorrectly. These issues have been fixed.
  • Resolved an issue in the Take Action by Segment tool where reordering personalized content triggered a misleading “Setup Incomplete” error. Users can now reorder content without encountering this error.
  • After adding the Take Action by Segment tool to Console Builder, the tool would sometimes unexpectedly disappear and only reappear after refreshing the browser. This issue has been fixed.
  • For clients using the Cookie Consent Banner feature, some favicons (the small icons associated with websites) were blocked from being shown in the Related Content tool, resulting in broken image placeholders being shown instead of the icons. This has been fixed.

Forums

Improvements to External Headset Functionality

Users will be able to use devices such as Bluetooth or wired headphones automatically simply by connecting them, without needing to refresh the page or make manual changes. Similarly, switching back to the internal device will be automatic when disconnecting the new device. An error message will be displayed if there is no available device to use after disconnection.

 

Volume Control

Desktop users will be able to control the volume of the live event using a new volume control slider.

 

French Canadian Support for Live Captioning

Automated captions are now available for live events presented in French Canadian. As the presenter speaks, captions in French Canadian can be displayed for the audience.

Note: Auto-live captioning is available as an add-on feature.

 

Video Clip Actions Available in Forums Basic

Video clip actions can now be performed in Forums Basic, similarly to regular Forums events.

 

Assorted Minor Visual Enhancements

  • Banner sizes and colors have been improved.
  • The name of the user who is screen sharing will now be available on hover.

Media Manager

New Article Collections for AI-Generated Event Content

To streamline content organization and improve discoverability, ON24 Media Manager now introduces Article Collections. For events and uploaded videos with Smart Plays enabled, all AI-generated articles—including eBooks, Transcripts, Key Takeaways, Blogs, Social Media Posts (for events only), and Follow-Up Emails (for events only)—will be automatically grouped into a single “Article Collection.” Previously managed as standalone assets, this content is now easier to locate, manage, and repurpose. Marketers can now save time and reduce manual overhead by accessing related derivative content in a unified view.

Intelligence

Webcast Intelligence Report Now Accessible Without Archiving Enabled

Users can now access the Webcast Intelligence Report (WIR) even if archiving is turned off for their workspace. Previously, turning off an event archive also disabled the Report URL. Now, these settings are independent—so you can keep the Report URL accessible even when the archive is deactivated. This update ensures that valuable event insights remain available throughout the duration of an active contract, providing greater flexibility and continuous access to reporting tools regardless of archiving settings.

 

“Audience Smart Questions” Now Included in Performance Insights Emails (AI-Powered ACE Customers)

ON24 Performance Insights emails for Elite events can now feature a new Audience Smart Questions section when AI-generated questions from the Elite Studio Q&A screener agent are available. This section appears only for AI-powered ACE customers when the Al-enabled ON24 Performance Insights feature is enabled and the event includes a Q&A widget. If no AI-generated questions are present, the section is omitted. Designed to mirror the style and behavior of the Key Takeaways section, this enhancement provides attendees with richer follow-up insights and helps organizers highlight audience engagement.

 

Improved Visibility into Audience Ratings in Performance Insights Emails

The Audience Rating section in ON24 Performance Insights emails now includes the total number of responses for each rating category—Audio/Video Quality, Ease of Use, Content Relevance, and Presenter Effectiveness. This enhancement displays the number of ratings in parentheses next to the star rating (e.g., “★ 3.00 Stars (4 Ratings)”), giving recipients clearer insight into audience engagement.

 

Enhanced Survey Visibility for Segment-Based Targeting

We’ve added new data to the Survey tab and Survey Detail page in Interactions to improve transparency for surveys created with the Survey by Segment tool. Each survey question now includes a Shown to: row indicating whether it was targeted to specific segments, shown as a fallback to unmatched attendees, or sent to all attendees. On the Survey Detail page, a new Segment column appears (when applicable) to show which segment led a respondent to see a question. This column also appears in export files for more detailed reporting.

 

New Smart Tip Highlights Impact of “New Design” Registration Pages

A new Smart Tip is now live on the Smart Tips page to help users boost event attendance by adopting the modern Registration Page Layout. Tailored to each account, the tip shows how switching to the "New Design" can improve registration-to-attendance conversion rates, with projected gains based on benchmark data. It also includes a personalized estimate of additional attendees that could have been gained in the past month. The tip appears when specific criteria are met, including feature availability and usage thresholds, and includes a "Boost Conversion" button linking to helpful resources.

Connect

Veeva Network Integration for HCP VID Retrieval - BETA

ON24 users can now enable a Beta integration with Veeva Network to streamline HCP identification. Once configured via the ON24 Integration page, registration data fields (e.g., name, email, NPI) can be mapped and automatically sent to Veeva Network. This enables the retrieval of verified Veeva IDs (VIDs) for registrants, which are stored in ON24’s exteventusercd field based on Network’s match logic. This feature enhances data accuracy and supports compliant, automated syncing of registrant information with Veeva's HCP directory.

 

Salesforce Data Cloud Integration for ON24 Event Metadata - BETA

ON24 users can now integrate with Salesforce Data Cloud to replicate event metadata using a new Data Lake Object (DLO) specifically for ON24 Events. This initial phase enables automated syncing of event details—such as titles, dates, URLs, and tags—into Salesforce for downstream use cases like Agentforce. While the DLO schema must currently be uploaded manually via a provided YML file, the integration automates the population of event data from the client’s ON24 workspace. Future releases will introduce full automation of the DLO creation and expand support to include Registrant and Attendee data.

 

New API Endpoint for Retrieving Users and Permissions Across Workspaces

A new API endpoint, GET /v2/client/{clientId}/users, enables API users to programmatically retrieve a comprehensive list of users within a specific workspace and its child workspaces. The response includes key user details such as name, email, job title, account, and last login date, along with an array of permissions for users with “Platform Login” access. This enhancement provides greater visibility and control over user roles and access rights, simplifying user management and supporting automation of administrative tasks across complex workspace structures.

 

Salesforce Picklist Fields Now Mappable for Salesforce Sales Cloud Integration

The Salesforce Sales Cloud integration now supports mapping Salesforce picklist fields as text fields for Campaigns, Campaign Members, and Leads. During schema discovery, picklist fields are detected and made available for mapping as simple text outputs within the integration builder. This update enables greater flexibility in handling ON24 data without enforcing strict picklist validation. Note that this enhancement applies only to single picklist fields (not multipicklists) and does not include label mapping or validation logic.

Go Live

Customize Session & Location Descriptions with Rich Text Editing

Go Live just got more customizable! Admins can now enhance the Session and Location description fields using a rich text editor, allowing for branded formatting like font styles, colors, links, and more. This makes it easier than ever to deliver a polished, engaging experience across your event touchpoints.

Branded event experiences go beyond logos and colors—content formatting plays a big role in how information is perceived. By enabling rich text editing in key areas of the attendee journey, this feature gives organizers more control and flexibility to create descriptions that are clear, styled, and aligned with brand tone.

articles.name: Introduction to Standard and Advanced Analytics
articles.title: Introduction to Standard and Advanced Analytics
articles.body:
Sophisticated, data-driven marketers need to not only generate pipeline and drive revenue but also need to be able to measure and demonstrate the ROI of their marketing programs. This article outlines the features included in ON24 standard Analytics and the valuable additions available with the Advanced Analytics package.
 

Note:  Advanced Analytics is available as an add-on product.  A 30-day free trial is available directly in the platform when you click on Analytics.  Contact your Account Manager for more information.

ON24 Analytics provides unprecedented, actionable buying insights into the engagement and behavior of prospects, the performance of ABM webinar programs. With the added features of Advanced Analytics you are able to define funnel optimization opportunities, buying signal opportunities, engagement prediction, and more, ensuring engagement-driven webinar marketing efforts truly deliver results.

Both Analytics, and Advanced Analytics are accessible via the Analytics tab on your event listing page.



Standard Analytics:

Filtering (Advanced and Standard Analytics)

Standard Analytics options:
Filter by date and product (Elite, Engagement Hub, Target). For Engagement Hub, filter to specific Hub. For Target, filter to specific Target page(s).
Advanced Analytics adds:
Access additional filtering options to drill down on specific details like email domains, tags, created by and registration source in addition to Standard Analytics options.

Account Engagement Report (Advanced and Standard Analytics)

Standard Analytics:
Top 10 accounts listed in the dashboard
Advanced Analytics adds:
Data for all accounts:
• Engagement minutes • Number of leads • Average engagement score • Content leads

Polls and Surveys Report (Advanced and Standard Analytics)

Standard Analytics provides:
Poll and survey data only available in Webcast Intelligence Report (for one webcast at a time)
Includes: • How many leads answered For each lead answered: • Name • Answer • Time of answer.
Advanced Analytics adds:
Aggregates questions and answers across all content that contains those questions, for entire account: • Poll/survey questions • Summary of responses and totals • Percentage of respondents • Number of source events • Number of respondents • Date range • Product • Event ID/name/number of responses • Aggregate questions detail report • Email domains • Tags • Created by • Registration source

Buying Signals Report (Advanced and Standard Analytics)

Standard Analytics provides:
Buying signal data only available in Webcast Intelligence Report (for one webcast at a time) Includes: • Number of views • Number of clicks For each conversion tool clicked: • Name • What they clicked • When they clicked.
Advanced Analytics adds:
For all webcasts across entire account: • Aggregated conversion rates • Conversion tool clicks • Leads (name, email, company, attended, meeting conversions, demo conversions, free trial conversions, most recent click) • Content (name, type, attendees, conversions, conversion rates, date)

Custom Lead Report (Advanced and Standard Analytics)

Standard Analytics provides:
Data available in Power Leads report. However, data for each lead is consolidated into 1 row. Expanded section with event details is not included in Power Leads export file. Includes aggregate data for each lead: • Overall number of events clicked • Time engaged • Sum of Engagement Score.
Advanced Analytics adds:
Custom Lead Report returns lead data, separating each lead and event information into their own row with the following details: • Event ID • Audience type • User ID, email, name, company, company size, job title/function, phone number, address • Registration source • Event ID/title/timestamp • Registration timestamp • Last viewed timestamp • Minutes viewed live/viewed on-demand/total minutes viewed • Marketing email, event email • OS, browser • Custom fields Custom Lead Report also can be filtered by: • Date range • Engagement Hub • Event ID

Advanced Filtering (Advanced and Standard Analytics)

Standard Analytics provides:
Filter only by: • Date • Product (Elite, Engagement Hub, Target) • For Engagement Hub, filter to specific Hub • For Target, filter to specific Target page(s)
Advanced Analytics adds: 
Filter by: • Email domains • Tags • Created by • Registration source

 

Advanced Analytics Additions:

Account Engagement Profile (Advanced Analytics Only)

Access a cumulative report on engagement with webinars and content hosted on ON24 digital experiences from individuals within the same account.

Account engagement metrics (time, score, leads, attendance, content views, unique attendees to leads, registration source) • Funnel stages • Business interests • Most recent content views/viewers • Most recent interactions by lead/prospects

Account Engagement Report (Advanced Analytics Only)

Discover details on every account that has engaged with your content. View the Account Engagement Profile through this report.

Prospect Engagement Profile (Advanced Analytics Only)

See a global view of each audience member’s lifetime engagement with webinars and content hosted on the ON24 platform. Track content journeys, buying signals, CTA clicks and view a prospect’s Engagement Score.

• PII (name, email, phone number, company) • Retention stage • Lifetime activities (engagement level, total time, total touches, webinars attended, resources, CTAs clicked, videos watched, documents read, webpages viewed, buy signals, virtual conferences attended) • Recommended content • Business interests • Content journey • Recent activity

Predictive Engagement Analytics (Advanced Analytics Only)

View the Predictive Engagement Level in the Prospect Engagement Profile to predict and understand how engaged a new prospect will be. Predicts high, medium, or low engagement.

Polls and Surveys Report (Advanced Analytics Only)

Access an aggregate view of all poll and survey results used across webinars and videos within the ON24 platform.

Buying Signals Report (Advanced Analytics Only)

See a combined view of all lead’s interactions with conversion tools and understand the success of each webcast, engagement hub, or content experience.

 

Custom Lead Report (Advanced Analytics Only)

Review the full details of your leads’ interactions and engagement.

 

Funnel Analytics Report (Advanced Analytics Only)

• Awareness, consideration, evaluation, retention filters • Number of webinar registrants at each level • Leads at each level

articles.name: April 2025 Product Release Notes
articles.title: April 2025 Product Release Notes
articles.body:
We are excited to publish the next release for the ON24 suite of products.

The release will take place over two days:

  • EU Data Center Release will be April 3, 2025
  • NA Data Center Release will be April 4, 2025

Note: Features are subject to change. Contact your account manager with questions about features included in your subscription. 

 

Click on the product name to learn more about each feature's release.

  • Intelligent Engagement Platform
  • Webcast Elite
  • Forums
  • Media Manager
  • Intelligence
  • Segmentation
  • Video Builder
  • Connect
  • Go Live
  • Virtual Conference

Intelligent Engagement Platform

IMPORTANT UPDATE

As of April 25, 2025, all previously unavailable features listed in these notes have been restored.

 

Video Formats

Starting June 1, 2025 ON24 will no longer support 4:3 video formats and Media Player Engagement Tool will only offer 16:9 layouts. To ensure the best experience across modern devices, please upload videos in 16:9 or widescreen formats moving forward.

Webcast Elite

Agentic Event Creation - BETA

Introducing agentic event creation, a smart assistant designed to streamline event setup inside the ON24 platform. By asking a few simple questions—such as your use case, event type, and presentation format (slides or screen share), etc.— the agent quickly recommends the best event template and layout for both registration page and the event console, including tools recommendations. This ensures a faster, more intuitive setup process, saving admins time while optimizing webinar experience.

Find more information in the Agentic Event Creation and Template Library article.

Note: This feature will be enabled outside of the core release schedule - it is expected on all accounts by April 11, 2025

 

Event Template Library & Filters - BETA

Introducing a new event template library, a curated collection of pre-configured event templates designed to match your specific use case and preferences with different configurations. Whether you're hosting a demand generation webinar, training session, or product feedback event, you can easily browse and select a template with the ideal layout and settings. Filters are available to sort templates by use case or recently used options, making it easier to find the most relevant template. This feature simplifies event setup, ensuring a seamless experience while giving marketers the flexibility to customize as needed.

As part of the event template library, we are introducing Intelligent Layout Templates. During the event setup process with the agent, users will select from a manually configured, fully editable layout or an Intelligent Layout.

Events based on the Intelligent Layout template have a fixed layout that cannot be modified – users select the engagement tools they want, and intelligent layout handles the rest. While this limits layout customization, it simplifies event setup, keeps the interface modern and responsive, and prevents accidental changes.

Additionally, it enables new UI behaviors, such as a panel on the right side of the console for displaying various supporting tools not directly related to slide or video.

Find more information in the Agentic Event Creation and Template Library article.

Note: This feature will be enabled outside of the core release schedule - it is expected on all accounts by April 11, 2025.

 

Elite Studio AI Generated Smart Questions - BETA

As attendees submit their questions, the Elite Studio Q&A window will feature a dedicated tab that uses AI to group similar inquiries into a single, representative "Smart Question" question. AI also prioritizes the most frequently asked questions, ensuring that the most popular ones are surfaced first.

Note: This feature will be enabled outside of the core release schedule - rollout is TBD and requires AI-Powered ACE.

 

Support for Certificates in Portrait Orientation

In the Earn Certification tool, you can now include a certificate that has a portrait orientation (image is taller than it is wide). This allows for more options for the printable certificate that is provided to attendees. Previously, we only supported certificates that have a landscape orientation (image is wider than it is tall).

 

Easily Change Tool Names from the Tools Selector

From the Console Builder > Tools Selector, admins can now edit the names of the engagement tools. From that single UI, an admin can quickly change the names of multiple tools, rather than having to go to each tool's Attributes settings, one at a time. Note: The name field in each tool's Attributes settings is still available. It is not being removed.

 

Breakout Room Configuration UI Updates

The Breakout Room configuration screen has been updated. Instead of one long list of settings, the settings are now organized into three tabs, making room setup less daunting. This is a cosmetic change, with no functionality changing.

 

Scheduled Event Archiving – Greater Control Over Event Archiving – Now with Custom Start & End Dates

We’re introducing a new level of flexibility for event archiving. Instead of predefined durations, you can now set a custom start and end date for when archived content becomes available. This ensures compliance with industry regulations while maintaining better control over post-event content distribution. If your review period ends earlier than the initially set archive date, you can change the start date to an earlier time to release your content earlier.

Additionally, a new account-level setting allows admins to set a default archive start time for all events. If no selection is made, the system will default to 1/2 hour after the event ends, which can be adjusted via our Platform Support team.

Find more information in the Event Archiving Options article.

 

Theme Customization is Back—HEX Color Selection (BETA)

We’re reintroducing Theme Customization for registration pages. Easily switch between our predefined theme and your custom brand colors for backgrounds, text, and buttons. For the custom colors, we will default to your branding colors, but for ultimate precision, you can enter in any HEX codes you need to ensure your branding is exactly as you want it. Each section of the registration page has its own design settings, allowing for full customization. Real-time previews let you see your changes before publishing.

 

Email Branding Updates

Your Global Branding colors now extend to emails. The header and buttons in event-related emails will automatically adopt your selected branding color whether it’s your Global Brand or selected theme color, ensuring a consistent and professional look. Plus, you can preview these changes in real-time before sending.

Find more information on setting up email notifications.

 

Multi-Language Events - BETA: Account Level Toggle to Disable Event Title Translation

Introducing an account-level setting to disable event title translations, helping you preserve event title accuracy. When enabled, event titles will always display in their original event language for every event in the account, regardless of user language settings. Perfect for ensuring consistency across multilingual events.

Find more information in the Language Options tab on the Using the Registration Page Builder article. 

Note: The Multi-language Events feature (currently in Beta) uses AI to generate translations. When it becomes generally available (GA), it may be offered as a paid add-on.

 

Event Archiving

To align with industry standards, best practices, and meet customer demand Event Archiving limits will be changed to 3-years. This change will take effect upon renewal of your contract. Upon contract renewal:

  • All future events will be limited to a 3-year retention, available shorter durations are unchanged.
  • All past events with an Always retention, will be reset to 3-years. Events set to a shorter duration are unchanged.
  • The Elite Admin UI will be updated to show the Archive Start Date.

Customers wishing to adopt or accelerate this process to meet their internal retention policies should contact their CSM.

 

Miscellaneous Enhancements & Fixes

  • For events where the Related Content tool had the "Show on Launch for Mobile" attribute enabled, clicking the Ask Question tool in the mobile console would incorrectly open the Related Content tool. This issue has been resolved.
  • For on-demand events with video clips, pausing and resuming playback would sometimes cause the video to restart from the beginning instead of resuming from the paused point. This issue has been resolved.
  • In events using the top navigation menu style, tools configured with Show on Launch enabled and Allow Minimizing disabled were incorrectly appearing in the menu. This issue has been resolved.
  • We have increased the character limit of the two-column section to 20,000 so that you can type away without having to worry about adding a new section for all you want/need to say.
  • Add support for French Canadian language translation to the registration and lobby pages
  • Access to registration and lobby pages from the Audience URL is no longer available for inactive events.
  • For the registration pages, we have reduced the padding across the page for a more compact look to get your audience to look at the next section. 

Forums

Support Play/Pause for Video Clips 

The ability to play, pause, rewind, and fast-forward video clips during a live Forum event will now be available for the default Forums configuration, Present from Elite Studio. Support for the other Forums configurations will be available in a future release.

See the Forums Presenter User Guide for details.

 

Tile Click Participant Options

Presenters, producers, and moderators will now be able to use all participant actions, such as mute, pin, disable mic, etc., by simply clicking anywhere on the target user’s tile. A list of actions will appear with all the functions normally found in the participant list. This will enable quicker control, and less effort. Attendees will also be able to change their view of other participants to zoom to fit using the same functionality.

 

Elite Studio Login Page on Tablets

When logging into Elite Studio on a tablet, you will see the new modern design for the login page.

Media Manager

Key Moments Video Clips Generation from Uploaded Videos - BETA

Introducing Key Moments Video Clips generation from uploaded videos, a powerful new feature that automatically identifies and extracts the most important moments from your uploaded MP4 videos. Using AI intelligent analysis, the system generates short, impactful clips that highlight key insights in just a few clicks, making it easy to repurpose content for promotions, recaps, or social media.  A minimum event length and additional requirements may be needed.

Check out the Key Moments Video Clips article for more details. 

 

All Generated Content Returned When Using Tags filter

Now all the content generated from the source event will be returned when custom or funnel tags are used as a filter and the source event has these tags applied. This streamlines content discovery, making it easier for users to find, repurpose, and engage with event materials.

Intelligence

ON24 Performance Insights

The ON24 Performance Insights Email Summary is now available for all clients. This new feature enables customers to automatically receive post-event performance summaries for Live, Simu-live, and Sim-2-Live events via email.

The summary provides quick access to key event insights, including attendance metrics, key takeaways, speaker details, audience ratings, poll responses, and the top job titles and companies of attendees. By consolidating these insights into a single email, stakeholders can efficiently review and analyze event performance without needing to log into the platform.

The ON24 Performance Insights email is enabled by default for event creators, while copies to producers and presenters can be enabled through a toggle on the Account Dashboard. Additional recipients can be included upon request via Platform Support.

Note: Test events are excluded from this feature.

 

Webcast Intelligence Report UTM Chart

The Webcast Intelligence Report will include UTM charts, providing a visual breakdown of attendee UTM parameters. Users can select from UTM Source, UTM Medium, UTM Campaign, UTM Term, or UTM Content via a dropdown menu, dynamically updating the chart to display counts and percentages. This enhancement, available in Live Performance and On-Demand Performance sections, helps users better understand their marketing attribution.

Segmentation

Miscellaneous Enhancements & Fixes

The survey tool was previously enhanced with segmentation capabilities to deliver personalized surveys, but the personalized surveys were not supported in recurring Simu-live events. This limitation has been removed.

Video Builder

Centralized Navigation

Media Manager will no longer have a Video Projects button or Create Video button.

Users can access Video Builder from the Platform Tools menu or by selecting Edit Video from Video Projects in Media Manager.

Connect

Salesforce Marketing Cloud Account Engagement Integration Enhancements

We have enhanced the Salesforce Marketing Cloud Account Engagement integration to support additional field mappings, including Address, Industry, Employee, Do Not Email, Opted Out, as well as custom Prospect fields. Additionally, all newly created Prospect records will have "ON24" set as the Lead Source. This update provides greater flexibility in data synchronization.

 

Salesforce Sales Cloud Integration Enhancements

We have expanded the Salesforce Sales Cloud integration to provide more flexibility in mapping ON24 event data. Users can now map any available event data to Salesforce Campaign records, enhancing event insight storage and utilization.

 

Update Event Endpoint to Include New Custom Metadata Fields

The REST API event response now includes five new custom event fields—custom1, custom2, custom3, custom4, and custom5—aligned with additions to the event setup page in Elite. These fields are grouped under customeventfields and returned as key-value pairs (name, label, value) in the /v2/client/{clientId}/event and /v2/client/{clientId}/event/{eventId} endpoints. This enhancement provides greater flexibility in capturing and utilizing event-specific details.

Note: Advanced Analytics package required.

 

ON24 HubSpot App Custom Workflow Action for Registration

The ON24 HubSpot App now includes a custom workflow action, allowing users to send registration data directly to ON24 without relying on HubSpot webhooks. This new workflow extension enables automation across all HubSpot subscription tiers. Users can define field mappings as add the Action as a step in their workflows to send Contact information to ON24.

Go Live

Centralized Event Resources for Attendees

Navigating event content just got easier. With our new Documents & Links page under Event Resources, you can now access all session-related content in one place. Easily search, filter, and download resources—including the option to save your favorites. No more jumping between pages—just seamless access to the content you need.

Find more information in the Administrator Guide - Content article.

 

Improved Event List Experience on the Homepage

We’ve made it easier than ever to browse all events when you log in. Now, you can clear all filters, so you can see the full list of events up front. You can also input new filters that will persist as you navigate between pages—resetting only when you choose to reset the filters and search criteria. Finally, we’ve added a Created By column that you can now sort by to find events more easily.

Check out the Administrator Guide - My Events article for details.

Virtual Conference

Update Search Bar Behavior to Phrase-Based Search

The search functionality in VC has been enhanced to support phrase-based search, ensuring more accurate and relevant results. Now, when users enter multiple words in the search bar, the system treats the input as a complete phrase rather than individual keywords. This improvement applies across all search options, including content search and briefcase search, delivering results that better align with user intent.

articles.name: Introduction to Go Live
articles.title: Introduction to Go Live
articles.body:
Learn more about ON24 Go Live. Deliver effortless video-centric events without a long runway and with minimal resources.

Quickly create and publish multisession, live-streamed digital events that connect your attendees over video for two-way discussion, networking, and relationship-building.

 

Produce and promote engaging digital events

Create meaningful connections with attendees by running two-way discussions that support effective networking and relationship building.

 

A solution for keynotes, peer networking and breakout sessions that’s refreshingly easy to use.

Set up live stream events faster than ever before with our pre-built, fully customizable event templates.

 

Discover trends and patterns that will bring you closer to your prospects

Track user engagement across every touchpoint and uncover your most qualified lead with our advanced analytics tools.

 

Integrations that deliver data where you need it most

Our platform ‘talks’ to your marketing automation and CRM systems so data is synced in real time and insights can be actioned straightaway.

 

What does Go Live include?

Agenda

Give your audience an advance look at the agenda with the ability to plan and sign up for sessions before the event starts.

 

Speakers' Gallery

Showcase your featured speakers, including their headshots and bios, so your attendees can browse and follow their sessions.

 

Main stage and Breakout Sessions

Build a dedicated location for your keynotes and add an unlimited number of back-to-back or concurrent breakout sessions.

 

Exhibit halls

Recreate a “tradeshow” floor experience for your audience to visit and meet with sponsors and exhibitors at their individual “booths”.

 

Networking

Integrate attendee interaction throughout your event with always-on live session chats, video breakouts and inside a dedicated networking lounge.

 

Registration

Start promoting your event as soon as it’s published with flexible sign-up forms, confirmation emails and event reminders.

articles.name: Simu-live & On-Demand Troubleshooting FAQs
articles.title: Simu-live & On-Demand Troubleshooting FAQs
articles.body:
This article covers FAQs for troubleshooting Simu-live webcasts and On-Demand recordings.

When creating Simu-live events, keep in mind: 

  • Every Simu-live must have at least one slide uploaded to properly stream and archive
    • If you don’t intend to show slides, you can remove the Slides tool from the console, but do not hide the slide or delete the slide in the Storyboard
  • Simu-live webcasts MUST be recorded AND published at least TWO HOURS before the scheduled start time
    • Elite Presentations will switch to live webcast mode two hours before the set time, so NO CHANGES can be made to your webcast during this time, including changing the start date/time, recording elements, choosing Rollover to Live audio, etc. 
    • Preview your Simu-live webcast in full at least 24 hours before the event - if the event does not play back as you would expect for the audience review your setup for accuracy and reach out to support.

My video clip is not playing.

Often this issue is caused when a video clip is uploaded to the storyboard, but the Slides tool is not on the Audience Console.  Video Clips play through this tool in Simu-live webcasts.  Find more information here.

This can also happen if Presenter Media is uploaded but there is no slide present.  A single slide is required for proper live streaming and must be used even if there is no intention to show it.   Upload a slide – a title slide is great - then remove the Slides tool from the console if wanted.  Find more information on Video Only Webcasts.

The audience cannot hear my video clip during the event.

When recording events in Elite Presentations, any video clips added to the Storyboard will play for the audience in the Slides Tool.  For those using Chrome, Chrome will automatically mute the video within the Slide Area by default.  Attendees using Chrome will need to unmute the video using the speaker icon at the bottom left corner of the Slide View Window.    

The wrong slide was shown during the lobby time.

The system will show the first slide of the first deck uploaded in the slide area during the lobby time (the 15 mins before the webcast start time). This will occur even if the slide is hidden or moved in the storyboard.

When uploading slide decks, be sure the first slide of the first deck is what you want your viewers to see first.  

My webcast started on the wrong slide.

This can happen if you don't select the first slide in your storyboard before you publish your webcast.

Always select the first slide in your presentation and make sure it's visible in the Presentation View window in Elite Presentations before you publish.

My Simu-live event is playing at the wrong time – can I stop it and change the time?

Simu-live events play out automatically at the set date and time.  To do so, Elite Presentations will switch to live webcast mode two hours before the set time, meaning NO CHANGES can be made to your webcast during this time, including changing the date or time.  

Be careful when setting up the event to ensure you have chosen the right time zone and time for the webcast to start.

I imported media into my event and it's not playing.

The Import Media functionality is not designed for rapid reproduction of events. If you import an event too early, some of the media may copy over but the streaming files will not.  To ensure all files carry over, allow ample time for the original event to process before importing.

  • If the original webcast was a fully live event, the archive process takes about three hours.  Once the archive is complete, the webcast will be available for importing
  • If the original webcast was a Simu-live event, make sure the original webcast has been created and published

To make sure the original event is ready to be imported, use the Preview URL for the source webcast and review it in full.  If the preview plays as expected the import should work. 

I'm trying to record in Elite Presentations, but the dial-in number says it's unavailable.

Open the Setup tool and check the Presenter Media tab to ensure it is set to Audio.  

Switching to Audio will allow the recording interface to be accessed.

I'm within the two hours before my Sim-2-live webcast is set to start, but cannot connect the audio and video of my webcam.

Simu-live and Sim-2-live events are audio-based, so the Media Player should be set to audio on the audience console, and you should be provided with dial-in numbers to connect to the webcast. However, the live audio will not be released to the audience until the pre-recorded portion of the webcast plays in its entirety to the audience. A two-minute countdown timer at the top of Elite Studio will alert presenters the event is about to roll over to Live Mode. 

I want to populate seed questions into the Simu-live and Sim-2-live events. How far in advance will I be able to add seed questions?

Seed questions can be populated into the event once the pre-recorded event begins and access to the Q&A tool provides the + icon allowing you to add your seed questions. Push them to your audience using the Q&A Tools during the pre-recorded or live portion of the webcast.

I see the message 'Content not available or still being processed' in my media player when I preview the event.

All Simu-live, Sim-2-live and On Demand events should be previewed 24 hours before the event. If you see this message and you have uploaded or imported your media and published the event in the past few hours (to allow for processing) this is an indicator or an issue with your setup or your media file. Check your setup and media to ensure they meet our specifications and reach out to support if assistance is needed. The preview should play just as you would expect it to for your viewers. If it does not, reach out to support before the two hour lock down.

articles.name: February 2025 Product Release Notes
articles.title: February 2025 Product Release Notes
articles.body:
We are excited to publish the next release for the ON24 suite of products.

The release will take place over two days:

  • EU Data Center Release will be February 20, 2025
  • NA Data Center Release will be February 21, 2025

Note: Features are subject to change. Contact your Account Manager with questions about features included in your subscription. 

 

Click on the product name to learn more about each feature's release.

  • Webcast Elite
  • Forums
  • Engagement Hub
  • Target
  • Media Manager
  • Intelligence
  • Connect
  • Go Live

Webcast Elite

Seamless Access to Your Next Event

We’ve made it easier for registered users to stay on track with your events. Now, when a registered user clicks on a multi-registration URL, they’ll automatically land on the next upcoming live event in the series.

This feature is an option initiated by adding a parameter to the end of the multi-registration URL.

This update ensures your audience gets where they need to be without any confusion, creating a smoother and more intuitive event experience.

 

Global Reach with Multi-Language Support - BETA

Your registration and lobby pages can now be translated into up to five languages, making it easier than ever to accommodate a global audience. The language shown to your audience members will be that of their browser language or the event language if the browser language cannot be detected. To enable this beta feature at no additional charge, simply let our Platform Support team know which languages you’d like to activate. Be sure to also let the Platform Support team know the primary language your team uses. Future versions of the multi-language support feature will require a contract update with applicable fees.

Find the list of languages we support.

Note: This feature is available only for the new Registration Builder.

 

Expanded Tagging with New Overview Page Fields

We’ve added 5 new customizable fields (3 text fields and 2 dropdown fields) to the Overview page, giving you greater flexibility to tag events based on your unique needs and metrics. With this update, organizing and tracking your events has never been easier.

To enable this feature, work with our Support team to enable the right parameters so that each field meets your needs.

 

Enhanced Flexibility with Conditional Fields

Event organizers now have the ability to create conditional fields for any custom field using the Display dropdown. This dropdown will be populated with the fields that have been added to the registration form. This update gives you greater control and flexibility in how you collect data, allowing you to tailor the registration experience to your specific needs.

Note: This feature is available only for the new Registration Builder.

 

Track Up to 15 Levels of Certification Completion

In the Earn Certification tool the number of partial credit levels that can be defined has been increased from 6 to 14. With this enhancement, clients can define up to 15 increments (14 partial levels + 1 full level) for tracking certification completion.

 

Picture-in-Picture for Screen Share During Live Events

You now have the option to enable Picture in Picture for screen share during a live webinar. With Picture in Picture enabled in your account, the active speaker will display in the upper right corner of the screen share image. 

Note: This feature will be implemented by request only. Contact Platform Support for details.

 

Elite Studio Light Mode

Producers and Presenters logged into Elite Studio now have the option to switch their interface between the default dark color theme and a new light color theme. The selection is personal to the individual presenter.

Find more information on the Elite Studio Interface. 

 

Miscellaneous Enhancements and Fixes

  • After the event had ended, admins were unable to toggle the Earn Certification tool on / off using the Console Builder > Tools Selector. This will be fixed.
  • For some on-demand events, replying by email to a question that was submitted via the Ask Question tool, would incorrectly populate the TO field with Do_Not_Reply@on24events.com. This has been fixed so that the TO field is populated with the email of the attendee who asked the question.
  • When viewing an event through the Preview URL, the previewer could not submit a response to polls. This will be fixed.

Forums

New Forums Options

You now have three options to configure a Forums event.

  1. Present with Elite Studio - This is the default Forums configuration to present from Elite Studio
  2. Present from Audience Console - Presenters login to the audience console and have access to start and end the event and recording, turn off camera and mute mics of all attendees and control who has access to screen share.
  3. Everyone is a Presenter - All presenters and attendees have the same functionality.

The option is set at the event level within the Forums Configuration Panel.

Find more information on setting up a Forums event here.

Find more information on using the new configuration options here.

 

Remove Name Tags Icons from Archived Events

When viewing an on-demand Forums event, nametags will no longer show such icons as mute, connectivity issue, or presenter badge. This serves to increase engagement and lower distraction when such information is not useful to an on-demand viewer.

 

Name Tag Sizing Updates

Name tags will now hide icons more often and show the enhanced larger name tags more often when appropriate. Nametags will also shrink more aggressively based on the tile height to reduce obstruction of participants’ video.

Engagement Hub

“Already Registered?” Placement on Top of the Registration Form

Now the Already Registered? option that allows users to login with their email address will be placed on top of the registration form (vs. the bottom of the form prior).

Target

“Already Registered?” Placement on Top of the Registration Form
