Now the Already Registered? option that allows users to login with their email address will be placed on top of the registration form (vs. the bottom of the form prior).

Media Manager

Easily Edit Key Moment Video Clips

The Content Builder - Text to Video tool can now be used to edit Key Moment Video Clips. To modify what is in the video clip, simply adjust the highlighted portion of the transcript. If you want to change the layout, you can choose from amongst 6 different layouts. A preview of your change is available to review prior to saving it. You can either overwrite the original video clip or choose to save as a new video clip.

To edit using the Content Builder - Text to Video tool, go to Media Manager, find the Key Moment Video that you want to edit, and from the 3-dot menu click on Edit Video.

Find more information on editing Key Moments Video Clips.

Note: Requires AI-Powered ACE

 

Removal “Create Video” option

Create Video option (for editing with Video Builder) under kebab menu in Media Manager will no longer be available for webinars, uploaded videos, images.  Users will still be able to edit videos directly in Video Builder.

 

Miscellaneous Enhancements and Fixes

  • New default thumbnails for different content types
  • Article subtypes filter wording updates
  • For some Key Moment Collections, there were cases where the Content Builder - Text to Video tool was not loading correctly. This has been fixed.

Intelligence

Executive Summary: ON24 Performance Insights BETA

Note: This feature will be limited to a select group of customers in this release.

ON24 is introducing a new Performance Insights Email Summary feature in Elite (including Forums events). This feature allows customers to automatically receive post-event performance summaries for Live, Simu-live, and Sim-2-Live events (not relevant for on-demand events) via email, streamlining access to key event metrics and takeaways.

The summary includes details such as event attendance, speaker insights, audience ratings, poll responses, and top-performing companies. By default, event creators, as well as presenters and producers from Elite Studio, will receive the post-event email. However, there is an option to add additional email addresses to receive it. This setting can be configured in three ways:

  • Event creators only
  • Event creators, presenters, and producers
  • Event creators, presenters, producers, and a specified list of additional recipients

Individuals also have the option to opt out of receiving the email.

 

Nurture Page Report Include Anonymous Users

The Nurture Page Report will now include anonymous users in the Views and Unique Viewers count, improving data accuracy for event analysis. Previously, anonymous attendees were excluded from these metrics, limiting insight into total audience engagement. With this update, marketers can gain a more comprehensive view of how both registered and non-registered users interact with Nurture Pages. This enhancement is available in ACE: Advanced Content & Nurture and Standard+ packages.

 

Update Toggle Tab Arrow Directions to Match Sidebar Behavior

In Analytics, the sidebar toggle arrow directions have been fixed for better usability. When the sidebar collapses the arrow now points right to indicate expansion. When expanded, it points left to indicate collapsing.

Connect

Salesforce Marketing Cloud Account Engagement (Pardot) Self-Service Integration

A self-service integration for Salesforce Marketing Cloud Account Engagement (Pardot) will be available, reducing bottlenecks and streamlining data sharing. Users can now set up the integration directly from the Platform Tools > Integrations page, eliminating the need to log support tickets. This integration automatically updates prospect records with webinar registration and attendance data, enabling more personalized and timely follow-ups.

 

New API Endpoint for Email Performance Statistics

A new API endpoint is now available that provides detailed email performance statistics for events. The API now exposes key email metrics, including email subject, total sent, total opened, open rate, delivered, delivery rate, clicked, clickthrough rate, and click-to-open rate. With this update, API users can analyze email performance across multiple events, helping them fine-tune messaging strategies.

 

Go Live

Stay Connected with Real-Time and Scheduled Notifications

You can now send out real-time or scheduled event notifications during a live event to keep your audience informed about changes, updates, and other important information. This feature ensures your customers stay in the loop, helping you deliver a smooth and engaging event experience.

articles.name: Request Entity Too Large
articles.title: Request Entity Too Large
articles.body:
This article explains how to resolve a cookie conflict when launching the Webcast Intelligence report.


If launching the Reports URL results in a "Request Entity Too Large" error, it is related to a cookie conflict in the user's browser(s). Please take a moment to completely clear your cache and cookies (make sure you clear both, cookies are key here), close the browser, and then restart.

This should resolve any cookie conflicts currently impacting any specific user.

articles.name: Webcast Elite Seamless Registration
articles.title: Webcast Elite Seamless Registration
articles.body:
Seamless Registration enables you to send registrant information from your remote webcast registration page to your ON24 registration database, using a script.

 

  • Things to Know
  • Best Practices
  • Available Registrant Fields
  • Seamless Multi-Registration
  • Sending Registrant Data
  • Using "HTTP GET" Method
  • Using "HTTP POST" Method

Things to Know

  • Important: The Login Only Option toggle should be ON to use Seamless Registration.  You can find the toggle under Settings on the webinar's Registration setup page.
    • Toggling Enable Login Only to ON causes Registration to appear disabled, however, Registration is still active. In order to update the active Registration Fields the Login Only Option should be first switched OFF. Once any edits are made it should be switched back ON.

  • When using seamless registration, use the same registration fields in the same order, to ensure the data carries over correctly.
    • See below for more information on which fields can be passed to ON24.
    • And find more information on registration mapping here.

Best Practices

The simplest approach and the one we see most clients take is to redirect the user to ON24 once they've finished filling out the registration page on a third party site. The user sees an ON24-hosted "Thank you for registering, this event will start on...." Lobby page and they get an ON24 cookie that serves as the persistent storage to identify the user. With the cookie present, subsequent calls to the event send the user to the ON24 "Audience URL", which bypasses the registration page if a valid cookie is found. You also have the option of having ON24 automatically redirect to a generic third-party hosted "thank you for registering" page instead, by passing in a URL in the optional "lobby" parameter.

To see an example of a Seamless Registration page, click here.

Partner Referral

If you use multiple registration pages for the same webcast, you can include a Partner Referral code to track the individual campaigns. 

For more information visit the Partner Referral page.

Available Registrant Fields

Specific fields can be passed to the ON24 registration database. The parameter names are case-sensitive. Please note that you may have changed the Display Label of the field, but the underlying parameter name is retained.  See this link for seamless registration parameters.

Please associate the fields with the correct parameter name (i.e. pass the email address in the "email" field rather than in some other field). If the data you want to store is not in one of the named demographic fields, use std1-std10.

Any values sent which exceed the designated character limit will be truncated.

Records containing the email address of a registrant who is already in the database for this webcast will be updated with the latest values being sent.

Seamless Multi-Registration

Seamless Multi-Registration is available under Event Series in Webcast Elite. Seamless Multi-Registration supports the same registration field parameters as the Seamless Registration process for individual events.

The seamless registration for multi-registration pages is designed to be used with a single URL without the use of separate event IDs. The seamless registration for Event Series works to register each individual for all events listed on the page. See an example of the html for a seamless POST method Event Series page below, with the shared MRP seamless URL in the form action.

<h3><u><div id="seamlessreg"></div>Seamless Registration Form</u></h3>
<form action="https://event.on24.com/wcc/mrs/5805XX/A7226F80BB5AFD05127EADEF150FFAXXX" method="POST">

<input type="hidden" name="deletecookie" value="true">
<table border="1">
<tr>
<td>First Name</td>
<td><input type="text" name="firstname" value="" required></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Last Name</td>
<td><input type="text" name="lastname" value="" required></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Email Address</td>
<td><input type="text" name="email" value="" required></td>
<tr>
<td>Company</td>
<td><input type="text" name="company" value="" required></td>
</tr>
</table>
<p><input type="submit" value="Submit"></p>
</form>

Sending Registrant Data

The script can be used with an HTTPS GET or HTTPS POST. For both methods, use HTTPS.

The webcast's unique event ID and key parameters must be included regardless of the method you use to send the Registrant data. Those values are contained within the Audience URL. You can access the Audience URL by clicking the Webcast URLs link on the Webcast Overview page; or by clicking the utility icon in the rightmost column on the My Webcasts page.

The EventID is the first numeric value that appears after the r/; the key is the second alphanumeric string which appears after the /. The Session ID will always be equal to 1.

Example: https://event.on24.com/wcc/r/1007892/39C070333A328199706BA21244AD8641

Using "HTTP GET" Method

The GET method relies on JavaScript redirection, which requires a web browser; this method will fail if used for server-to-server requests.
 

Rules to Remember:

If any of the parameters include the SPACE character, it must be defined using URL encoding i.e. "%20", (eg, &country=United%20States). Some special characters embedded within the field values, like "=", "&" etc., should also always be encoded.

When in a batch mode or registering multiple users, you must also send the additional "deletecookie" parameter set to "true" (ie. deletecookie=true).

Example: Sending first name, last name, email, and company.  Be sure to replace the XXXXs with your webinar event ID and Key.



If you are having trouble with the standard HTTPS GET URL, you can try https://event.on24.com/utilApp/r instead.

Using "HTTP POST" Method

You can also send registrant data to the ON24 Event Database by using a POST method.
 

Rules to Remember:

When in a batch mode or registering multiple users, you must also send the additional "deletecookie" parameter, setting it to "true", thus: delete a hidden element for POST method.

UTF input isn't necessary if sending UTF-8, which is the default. If youâ€™re sending characters using a different encoding, change the hidden value to the proper character set.

For example, ISO-8859-1

Example: Sending first name, last name, email, and company.  Be sure to replace the XXXXs with your webinar event ID and Key.

 <form name="myform" action="https://event.on24.com/utilApp/r" method="POST">

<input type="hidden" name="eventid" value="XXXXX">

<input type="hidden" name="sessionid" value="1">

<input type="hidden" name="key" value="XXXXX">

<input type="text" name="firstname" value="john">

<input type="text" name="lastname" value="smith">

<input type="text" name="email" value="john.smith@abc.com">

<input type="text" name="company" value="ABC, Inc.">

</form>

articles.name: Breakout Rooms Overview
articles.title: Breakout Rooms Overview
articles.body:
Breakout Rooms enable attendees and presenters to network with each other face-to-face. This article covers the features of the tool, FAQs, known issues, and links to articles covering how to set up and use the tool.

Breakout Rooms are Engagement Tools you can use in both Webcast Elite webinars and Forums events.

 


Note: Breakout Rooms are an add-on feature. Reach out to your Account Manager with questions.

Features 

  • Available for live, Simu-live, and Sim-2-Live webinars, and Forums
  • Each event can host up to ten Breakout Rooms
  • Each Breakout Room has a capacity of up to 50 attendees
  • Moderators and audience members do not need to download any additional programs
  • Attendees can join no matter what type of device they are on
    • Note: Attendees joining the Breakout Room as a "moderator" role will still need to join using a desktop or laptop. Joining as a moderator is not supported from a mobile device.
  • Attendees can seamlessly enter Breakout Rooms from an event and also just as easily return to the event in progress
  • Option to have moderators with extra controls
  • Breakout Rooms attendance is captured in reporting, including who joined and the duration of breakout attendance

 

FAQs

What types of events support breakouts?

Breakout Rooms can be used in all live webinars, including Simu-live and Sim-2-live webinars and Forums.

Breakout Rooms are NOT available for on-demand webinars, including live webinars that become on-demand after they are archived. For example, during the live broadcast of an event, a Breakout Room can be used. However, once the webinar is archived and becomes on demand, the Breakout Room tool will be hidden so that on-demand attendees will not have access to it.

Do Breakout Rooms carry over when I copy an event?

No.

Can I set up a Breakout Room on the fly or at the last minute?

No.

Breakouts should be configured within the Console Builder at least 30 minutes PRIOR to the start of the event. Do not wait until the last minute to configure your breakout room.

Setting up a new room while the event is in progress is not currently supported.

How can I share an access code with an attendee?

Access codes should be provided to select attendees ahead of time. For example, if you have a set of attendees that you want to have access to a room, you could email the access code to them ahead of time.

During the event experience, the Attendee Chat tool with the Direct Chat capability turned on could be used to message an attendee with an access code individually.

Can presenters join Elite Studio and a Breakout Room at the same time?

Yes, presenters can join a Breakout Room from within Elite Studio.

How long can a Breakout Room be open?

Breakout Rooms are intended to be used for a duration no longer than 60 minutes. If you want to run a Breakout Room longer than 60 minutes, please contact your Account Manager to discuss avoiding non-compliance with your contract.

How little or large can each Breakout Room be?

Each Breakout Room can have as few as two attendees. There is a max capacity of 50 attendees.

Can attendees on smartphones or tablets engage in Breakout Rooms? 

Yes, attendees can join Breakout Rooms with mobile phones or tablets.

Note: Moderators will still need to join via desktop or laptop.

Can interactivity tools like Survey, Take Action, etc., be included in the Breakout Room?

Yes, the following tools can be added to a Breakout Room if they were part of the main event console:

  • Related Content
  • Speaker Bio
  • Take Action
  • Book Meeting/Start Free Trial/Request Demo
  • Donate
  • Take Survey
  • Text with Banner
  • URL
Will recordings of the Breakout Rooms be available?

Yes. Moderators can record Breakout Rooms. After a Breakout Room has been recorded, the recordings are located on the Archive page in the webcast setup.

Note: Only those with a Manage Users login will be able to see and access the recordings.

Is there a way to assign attendees to specific Breakout Rooms? 

No. Currently, joining a room is initiated by the attendee and there is no way to pre-assign attendees to a specific Breakout Room.

If an event has ended but an attendee needs to refresh their browser for any reason, can they rejoin the Breakout Room?

Yes. In the scenario where the main event has ended and an attendee in a Breakout Room refreshes their browser for any reason, they can rejoin using the Audience URL and will successfully be brought back into the room.

 

Known Issues

Issue

Notes

For clients using the Kollective streaming solutions, there is a limitation when using Breakout Rooms configured to show during the event.

Initially, when attendees join the main event, peer-to-peer streaming is utilized.

However, for attendees who join a Breakout Room, when they leave the room and rejoin the main event, the main event will no longer use the peer-to-peer streaming.

For those attendees, CDNs will be used for streaming instead.

As a workaround, refresh the browser and the stream for the main event will use Kollective streaming.

For clients using the Hive streaming solutions, there is a limitation when using Breakout Rooms configured to show during the event.

Initially, when attendees join the main event, the peer-to-peer streaming is utilized.

However, for attendees who join a Breakout Room, when they leave the room and rejoin the main event, the stream for the main event stops (user sees black screen).

As a workaround, refresh the browser and stream for the main event will start again using Hive streaming.

When the Certification tool is configured to have an automated attendance check (AAC), the AAC is not shown in the Breakout Room.  So, if an attendee is in the room when an AAC is triggered, the attendee will miss it.

articles.name: January 2025 Product Release Notes
articles.title: January 2025 Product Release Notes
articles.body:
We are excited to publish the next release for the ON24 suite of products.

The release will take place over two days:

  • EU Data Center Release will be January 9, 2025
  • NA Data Center Release will be January 10, 2025

Note: Features are subject to change. Contact your account manager with questions about features included in your subscription. 

Click on the product name to learn more about each feature's release.

  • Webcast Elite
  • Forums
  • Engagement Hub
  • Media Manager
  • Intelligence
  • Segmentation
  • Video Builder
  • Connect
  • Go Live

Webcast Elite

Update to Managing Tools in Console Builder and Hiding of Inactive Tools

In the Console Builder, there is a new Tools button in the top right corner. From there, the tools that have been added to the console can easily be toggled ON or OFF. If you have the Manage Audience Console Layout permission, you will also be able to add additional tools. This replaces the Tools Manager.

Toggling a tool OFF is equivalent to making a tool inactive. A related change is that tools that are marked inactive (either through the toggle or through the Attributes > Active setting) are now hidden on the Console Builder. Hiding inactive tools on Console Builder removes clutter and makes it easier to work on your console layout.

 

Guided Registration Page Setup

We’re making it even easier to create engaging registration pages for your events. Now, when you add sections to your registration page, they’ll come pre-populated with content to help kick-start your page setup.

These pre-filled sections are designed to simplify editing by providing helpful prompts to guide you in showcasing your event information in the most impactful way.

Note: This feature applies to the new Registration Page Builder.

 

Easily Edit Social Posts

We are introducing a convenient way to update your social posts. When you hover over the floating social icons on your registration page, a pen icon will appear. Click the pen icon to quickly edit the social post with an image and text. This update ensures managing your social posts is straightforward and efficient.

Note: This feature applies to the new Registration Page Builder.

 

Expanded Global Brand Palette in Theme Dropdown

The Theme dropdown on the Overview page will now allow selection of any of the four Global Branding colors that have been selected, as well as ON24-defined color options. Theme colors are applied to the new Registration Page and Audience Console. The Primary brand color will continue to be the default.

Note: This feature applies to the new Registration Page Builder.

 

Miscellaneous Enhancements and Fixes

  • The account name and selection element has been moved to the right of the page (closer to the 'Create Event' button).
  • Registrants that were blocked using the Manage Registrants page were still able to enter the event. This has been fixed.
  • In the Contact Us tool, attendees were erroneously able to modify the To field. This has been fixed.
  • There were cases where custom lobby music was not playing when it should. Now, if there is an unexpected failure in playing custom lobby music, the default lobby music will be played as a fallback.
  • For events with both the Breakout Room tool and Take Survey tool configured to show at the end of the event, the Take Survey tool was not being shown. This has been fixed.
  • For events with tools configured to show at the end of the event (i.e. Take Action tool, Take Survey tool), attendees who reach that post live content are supposed to be automatically exited from the console if there is 5 minutes of inactivity. That was not happening, but it has now been fixed.
  • In the case that the console's chrome style was set to one of the options where the chrome is always shown, for tools that had the Show on launch attribute unchecked, the ability for attendees to reposition the tool was not working correctly. This has been fixed.

Forums

Speaker Names in Forums Transcripts

For Forums events, the transcript now includes the name of the person speaking, followed by the words being spoken. This is useful when there are two or more people speaking in the event, especially when they are having a back-and-forth conversation. Event transcripts can be found in the Media Manager.

Note: This feature will be rolled out to customers in phases. Please see ON24 Platform Rollouts for details.

 

New Forums Options

You now have three options to configure a Forums event.

  1. Present with Elite Studio - This is the default Forums configuration to present from Elite Studio
  2. Present from Audience Console - Presenters login to the audience console and have access to start and end the event and recording, turn off camera and mute mics of all attendees and control who has access to screen share.
  3. Everyone is a Presenter - All presenters and attendees have the same functionality.

The option is set at the event level within the Forums Configuration Panel.

Note: This feature will be rolled out to customers by request only. Please contact Support for details.

 

Screen Share from an iPad (Beta)

You can now share your screen from an iPad during a live Forums event. You will need to first download the ON24 screen share app from the Apple app store.

Note: This feature is in Beta and will be enabled by request only. Please contact Support for details.

Engagement Hub

ALT Text on Category Images

Now admins have the ability to provide alt text on category images with half-width configuration for improved accessibility.

 

Search Label next to Search Icon

Admins can now turn on a Search label next to search/magnifying glass icon. The label can also be localized.

Media Manager

Easily Create Videos of Key Quotes

In the Content Builder - Text to Video tool, you will see that key quotes from your event are now prominently highlighted so that they are readily identified and found. With a single click you can select a key quote and proceed to generate a video of it.

To launch the Content Builder - Text to Video tool, go to Media Manager, find the Key Moment Collection you want to add to, and from the 3-dot menu click on Add Video.

 

Generate Podcast

Now along with other AI Generated Content ordered through Smart Plays, a podcast and its written script with dual speakers (Host and Guest) will be generated and available in Media Manager (applicable for webinars in English only). Users will be able to edit the script and re-generate the podcast audio file. Additional customizations such as host and guest voices, names are available through platform support. A link to the podcast will be included in the email notification sent to event creators notifying them that AI-generated content is available.

 

Updated Default Thumbnails and Subtype Tags for AI Generated content

Now default thumbnails for AI-generated content (eBooks, Blogs, Key Takeaways, Transcripts, Podcasts, Social Media Posts, and Follow-Up Emails) are color-coded, and subtype tags help admins distinguish and navigate between different types of content in Media Manager.

Intelligence

New Smart Tip: Industry Interests

This Smart Tip will provide the top interests found across the ON24 platform for the selected Sector and Industry of the account. (Smart Tips uses the same Sector and Industry selections that are used for Benchmarking reporting in Analytics.) The top interests are the most frequent/important words and phrases that come up in content and customer interactions (questions asked).

 

New Smart Tip: Captioning

For accounts that do not use Live or On-Demand Captioning in their events, a new Smart Tip will show how much their engagement scores could be increased by turning captioning on.

For accounts that do use On-Demand Captioning in events, but do not turn the captions on by default for attendees, a new Smart Tip will show how much defaulting to visible captions could increase their engagement scores.

 

Related Content by Segment

For events that include Related Content set up to be targeted to specific segments, the Related Content report in the Webcast Intelligence Report will now include details about the targeted segments and the segments of leads who download content.

On the top-level Related Content report, the summary for each piece of content will display the list of segments to whom that content was targeted. The summary will also show if a piece of content was defined as the fallback option, or if the content was simply shown to all attendees.

Segmentation

Target a Unique Certification to Different Audience Segments

The Earn Certification tool has been enhanced with segmentation capabilities so that you can deliver more personalized and relevant experiences to attendees. Within the Certification tool you can target certifications to different segments of attendees. Attendees see the same tool container, but the container will be populated with a different certification based on segments.

For example, using our Segment Builder, you define a "California Attendees" segment and an "Arizona Attendees" segment based on a registration field. Then, you can configure the Earn Certification tool so that "California Attendees" are presented with a certification that requires watching 30 minutes and passing a test. If they fulfill the criteria, they earn a California specific downloadable certificate. Meanwhile, "Arizona Attendees" are presented with a certification that only requires 25 minutes of viewing. If they fulfill the criteria, they earn an Arizona specific downloadable certificate.

You can still easily define a certification that is shown to all attendees.

Note: We are working on enhancing the certification report to better report on personalized certifications. The enhanced report will be available in the February release.

Note: This feature is currently on hold. It will be rolled out to customers in phases once roll out begins. Please see ON24 Platform Rollouts for details.

Video Builder

Resolved issues where pages would hang while loading content or not play back when clicked for some time.

Connect Integrations

Folder Organization Metadata for Audience Questions in API Responses

A new enhancement to the attendee activity API response now includes folder names for audience questions, as organized by presenters in Elite Studio. This update provides additional context by indicating the folder location of each question, including Inbox and Trash, alongside the attendee's submitted question and any presenter responses. This feature streamlines activity tracking and improves data clarity for more efficient analysis.

Go Live

Make a Statement with Vanity URLs

Vanity URLs give you ultimate control over how your events are presented online, putting your brand front and center. Now you can use Vanity URLs in Go Live events, providing a polished experience that aligns with your brand.

 

Create Elite Webinars on the Fly

We’ve streamlined your workflow to save you time and effort. Now, you can create a new Elite webinar directly within your Go Live session setup. Previously, you had to configure webinars in Elite before creating a session. Webinars created from Go Live must be edited in Elite.

 

Introducing the Light Mode Logo Option

You can now upload a light mode logo to enhance the appearance of your event pages when using a lighter background theme. This update ensures your branding is maintained regardless of the display mode. Easily add your light mode logo for a polished, professional look that complements every setting.

articles.name: Troubleshooting A Poor-Quality Stream in Broadcast Video
articles.title: Troubleshooting A Poor-Quality Stream in Broadcast Video
articles.body:
Steps to look more closely at the encoders to troubleshoot a poor-quality stream. Look at “&streamtest=Y” to verify how the “A” and “B” streams look on Primary and Backup CDN feeds.  Do the Primary and Backup streams look and sound the same or is only one of the streams having an issue?  Make note of any discrepancies seen/heard between the streams.

 

Troubleshoot Encoders Onsite

Look at the local encoder.  How does the program feed look within the encoder?

  • If the input feed looks poor / out of sync, check that your capture card is working correctly
    • Work with the onsite crew to fix the signal so it looks correct and is in sync going into the encoder.
  • If the input feed on the encoder looks good, check the encoder settings.
  • Double check the output stream settings / quality match event specifications.
  • Look for extraneous encoder settings that should not be there, i.e. frame delay or effect that is impacting the stream. Return encoder settings to “normal”, restart the encoder and then check stream quality.
  • Stop/Restart your A encoder. Does this improve the stream? Please wait at least 30 seconds between stopping and restarting your encoders.  This allows servers to process the disconnect before you try to reconnect.
  • Close encoding application or even reboot the computer if problem persists.

 

Engage the roll over to “B” stream

Stop the A encoder to engage the webcast console to roll over to the “B” encoder stream. (may take 30-40 seconds to see the roll over on the console).  If the quality improves, and you are in the middle of a live event, you should stick to the B encoder through the rest of your webcast.

If you restart the A encoder after the system has rolled to the “B stream”, A console refresh is needed in order to see the “A stream” on the Preview /Audience console.
 

If limited bandwidth is the issue:

  • You may want to stop the “B” Encoder and have it run on a backup wireless hotspot or alternative connection than what the A encoder is running on.
  • Alert the local IT contact to assist by allotting more bandwidth or opening ports, etc.

articles.name: Go Live Administrator Guide – Seamless Registration
articles.title: Go Live Administrator Guide – Seamless Registration
articles.body:
Go Live supports using external registration forms such as with a marketing automation platform (MAP). This enables you to send registrant information from your external/MAP registration page to your Go Live event's registration database, using a script.

 

This article covers the legacy version of seamless registration.  We recommend using Unified Seamless Registration instead.  

Find more information on using Unified Seamless Registration here. 

 

Things to Know

When using seamless registration, use the same registration fields in the same order, to ensure the data carries over correctly. 

See below for more information on which fields can be passed to ON24. 

And find more information on registration mapping here. 

 

Step 1 - Specify the URL of the Registration Form

  • Select External and paste the registration page URL in the text box.

Step 2 - Sending Registrant Data

A script can be used with an HTTP GET or HTTP POST.  For both methods, you may use either HTTP or HTTPS.

Important: The unique Go Live Event ID (eventId), Audience Key (key), and Session ID (sessionid) parameters must be included regardless of the method you use to send the Registrant data.  

Contact Platform Support if you need help identifying your Audience Key.

For the submission process on your registration form, use the Seamless Registration URL (located on the Go Live > Registration setup page) to pass the user data to the Go Live event, and register the user.

 

Using "HTTP GET" Method

The GET method relies on JavaScript redirection, which requires a web browser; this method will fail if used for server-to-server requests.

Example: Sending First Name, Last Name, and Email 
https://golive.on24.com/wcc/autoreg?eventId=XXXXXX&sessionid=1&key=XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX&firstName=paul01252&lastName=test2&email=paul01252@paul.com&targetUrl=

 

Using "HTTP POST" Method

You can also send registrant data to the ON24 Platform by using a POST method.

Example: Sending First Name, Last Name, Email and Company
<form name="myform" action="https://golive.on24.com/wcc/autoreg" method="POST">
  <input type="hidden" name="eventId" value="XXXX">
  <input type="hidden" name="sessionid" value="1">
  <input type="hidden" name="key" value="XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX">
  <input type="text" name="firstName" value="john">
  <input type="text" name="lastName" value="smith">
  <input type="text" name="email" value="john.smith@abc.com">
  <input type="text" name="company" value="ABC, Inc.">
             <input type="hidden" name="targetUrl" value="">
  <input type="submit" value="Submit Form"/>
</form>

What fields can be passed to ON24?

Specific fields can be passed to the ON24 registration database. The parameter names are case-sensitive. Please note that you may have changed the Display Label of the field, but the underlying parameter name is retained.  See this link for seamless registration parameters.

Be sure to associate the fields with the correct parameter name (i.e. pass the Attendee's first name in the "firstname" field rather than in some other field).  If the data you want to store is not in one of the named demographic fields, use std1-std10.

Any values sent which exceed the designated character limit will be truncated.

Records containing the email address of a registrant who is already in the database will be updated with the latest values being sent.

articles.name: Webinar FAQs
articles.title: Webinar FAQs
articles.body:
Frequently asked questions about using the ON24 webinar platform.

 

Can I run two or more webinars at the same time? This will depend on the terms of your contract.  Please reach out to your ON24 Account Executive or Account Manager for additional information.
How will my audience experience/see/hear the webinar?

The audience experiences/sees/hears the webcast by logging into the audience console. After registering, a cookie will be saved in their browser and they will be directed to the lobby page.

Fifteen minutes before the live event, a Launch Presentation button will automatically appear on the lobby page. Upon click, attendees will enter the audience console and watch the webcast. Based on the features made available in the audience console, they can see slides, hear presenters, submit questions to the presenters via the Q&A box, etc.

For tips on building your audience console, click here.
How many registrants/attendees can I have?

This will depend on the terms of your contract.  Please reach out to your ON24 Account Executive or Account Manager for additional information.
How many presenters can I have?

Up to 10 presenters may join the webinar.  If you are expecting a large audience, a supporting staff member can log in to the presenter tool/console as a Q&A moderator to help manage questions.
In Elite Studio, do attendees see the Team Chat?

No, only the presenters can see and participate in the Team Chat.  If an open dialogue with the audience is desired, consider adding the Attendee Chat engagement tool to the audience console.
Are emails included with our webcast?

Yes, your webinar includes a registration confirmation email that will be sent immediately upon registering.  

Up to four additional reminder emails may be configured before the event.  After the live webinar concludes, two post-event emails will be sent to all registrants based on attendance (Thank You for Attending and Sorry We Missed You).  

Learn more about how to configure the ON24 webinar emails here.
Can we use our logos and branding?

Absolutely! Use the Upload Banner section on the Registration Page to upload a high-resolution logo.

Supported file formats are JPG, PNG, and GIF. The recommended banner size is 990x 100.   Wider images may be uploaded to support higher screen resolutions.  

The banner will be displayed on both the registration and lobby pages.
Is there a dial-in number I can distribute to participants?

All audio, video, and webinar content is streamed directly through the audience console using their internet browser so there is no dial-in option for participants.

The dial-in & passcode provided to the presenters should never be given to participants.
Can the webcast format be changed?

You can change the webcast presentation type at any time you’d like, up until 2 hours before your live event.

If you originally choose Simu-live and change the presentation type, you will lose any recorded media that has been saved.

Changing the presentation type does not impact your registrants.  
How can I preview the slides and video clips for my live event once I've uploaded them?

Open the Preview URL (located in the Webcast URLs) in a separate window of your Internet browser. Launching the Preview URL will open your audience console.

As you advance through your slides, video clips, polls, screen share demonstrations, etc., the audience console will show all of your activity from an attendee's perspective.  
What is the best practice for rehearsing/practicing for my live event?

Elite Studio remains in practice mode as long as the Start Live button has not been pressed.

You may log into Elite Studio anytime before your live event, as  many times as needed, to rehearse. Once logged in, you can review your presentation content and practice pushing slides, polls, etc.

Once the attendee registers what link do they use to get to the live event?

How can the same link get us to different places?

The audience member will continue to use the same URL they received during the event promotion. We call this URL the Audience URL. Once an audience member registers for the event using the Audience URL, a cookie is saved to their browser. The cookie will recognize them the next time they launch the Audience URL and allow them to enter the webinar.

Can we make changes to our slide deck after the recording? I want to modify a few things before the Simu-live webcast.

Yes, the slide deck can be replaced post-recording. In order to do so, it’s important for the new deck to have the same number of slides as the deck that was originally used to record.

Find more information on Simu-live webcasts here.

Can we add a calendar appointment to the registration confirmation email?

Yes, a calendar reminder is automatically populated for you in the registration confirmation email.

Can I add an index to my archived presentation?

Yes, the Table of Contents Tool allows you to display chapters during the on demand viewing of your webcast.

Chapter designations are tied to slides (versus keywords); you determine which slide(s) designate a chapter. This allows the on-demand attendee to jump around within the event.

I want to offer a PDF of my slide deck for download by audience members, but I need to leave some slides out for proprietary reasons.

By adding a Resource List engagement tool to the audience console, you can share documents, presentations, URLs, podcasts, and other content with your audience.

Can I host the archive on my website?

The archive of the event will be hosted on ON24. The registration and lobby URLs will remain the same as the live event. We do offer a Single File Archive that you can host on your site. Depending on your event type, the SFA can either be a recording of the entire console or just the slides with audio.

What is the difference between a poll and a survey?

A poll is a single question sent out to the audience at a specific time during the webinar.  By default, the poll question will appear in the slide window on the audience console, but a pop-up window option is also available. You can also share the live poll results with the audience if desired.

A survey is a series of questions on a single form that you can present to your audience. The survey can be configured to show during the webinar, after the live webinar ends, or both. There is no mechanism to show the survey results to the audience during a live event.  

All poll and survey responses are available in the Webcast Intelligence report.

articles.name: Planning and Presenting an Event
articles.title: Planning and Presenting an Event
articles.body:
Helpful list of Knowledge Center articles that cover how to plan and present your webinar, best practices and guides along with resources that describe how to use Elite Studio and engage and interact with your audience.

 

Planning Your Event

  • Ten Steps for Planning a Successful Webinar - Ten critical steps including action items and timeline to ensure your webcast is a success. 
  • Five Creative Ideas to Propel Your Webinars - Five ideas to spice up your webinar program in order to break out of old routines, try new things and push the envelope.
  • How to Build a Killer Presentation - How to put your audience first in creating your webinar and deliver a "killer" presentation.
  • Recommended Gear for At-Home Webinars - Recommendations on types of webcam or microphone to purchase for your at-home webinar studio. 
  • Webinar Producer Checklist - Provides details for your presenting team to ensure the webcast runs smoothly. Includes system requirements and a series of checklists to prepare for the delivery of your live webcast.
  • Webinar Presenter Preparation - Provides the key steps for rehearsal, preparation and execution to ensure you are prepared to deliver a great webinar.
  • Slide Deck Guidelines - Covers the basics of slide design, settings to avoid, what to do in the unlikely event that a slide deck fails to upload, and any known issues with .PPT and .PPTX files.
  • Presenting By Webcam - Review ON24's best practices for presenting by webcam. We cover wardrobe, pre-webinar rehearsals, lighting, and how best to set up your webcam feed, so attendees receive the best possible webcam produced experience.

 

Blog Posts

  • How to Plan a Webinar Strategy to Win - Check out this blog for information on how to plan, target and optimize your webinar programs to attract, engage and convert.
  • How to Make a Webinar Look Professional - The webinar console is really the main element you should focus on when you want to make a webinar look professional. Your webinar’s background, interactive tools and even the icons attendees see can have an impact on how your brand is perceived. What to do? Don’t worry. We’ve broken down the essential elements of an amazing webinar console.
  • Webinar Best Practices - Resources to learn everything from planning out your program to optimizing webinars for engagement and turning that engagement into actionable data.

 

Elite Studio Resources

  • Elite Studio User Guide - Elite Studio is the presenter side of Webcast Elite and allows presenters to deliver and drive an interactive and engaging webinar for your audience. This Knowledge article provides an in-depth overview of Elite Studio including system requirements, user roles, and an overview of all the features and tools.
  • Elite Studio Interface - Overview of the Elite Studio interface.
  • Virtual Backgrounds- How to set up a virtual background when presenting by webcam.
  • Using Screen Share - This article covers how to use screen share during a webcast. 
  • Troubleshooting Your Connection to a Live Event- If you or your presenters are having trouble connecting to or using Elite Studio to present your live webcast, this article can help you troubleshoot the issue. Find a list of common webcast issue resolutions, help with connecting your microphone or webcam, and answers to frequently asked questions.

articles.name: Elite Studio Webcam Layouts
articles.title: Elite Studio Webcam Layouts
articles.body:
Four video layout options are available when presenting via webcam, allowing multiple presenters to be shown in the Media Player simultaneously, with voice-activated switching.

If multiple presenters are connected via webcam, the Producer may change the layouts on the fly by opening the Media Player and hovering over the Presenter tab.

Note: You must have multiple presenters connected by webcam for the layout options to appear.


  

Things to Know

  • Google Chrome and Microsoft Edge are the suggested browsers for presenters.  Firefox is supported however, Edge and Chrome may have a more consistent experience. Internet Explorer is not supported and lacks the required WebRTC APIs for webcams.
  • The Video Presenter Bridge is cloud-based and can only receive inbound connections.  It cannot "dial out," so each presenter must connect to the webcast independently, using their preferred device.
  • Only those logged in to Elite Studio as a Producer may change the webcam layouts. 
  • If a presenter is muted, the voice-activated controls will not hear their audio.
    • Presenters must UNMUTE themselves when it's their turn to talk, so the voice activation can detect their audio and display them to the audience. 
    • Voice activation is used for Layout 1, Layout 2, and Layout 3.
  • If a video presenter turns off their camera, their video tile will not display in the video window, but they will still be heard.
    • If you have a single webcam presenter and they turn off their camera, the video window will display an image with a speaker icon to indicate that they are audio only.

 

Layout 1

Layout 1 displays one presenter at a time and is ideal for showing presenters in sequential order. 

Voice-activated switching will automatically display the presenter who is actively speaking to the audience.

Audience View

 

Layout 2

Layout 2 will display up to 10 presenters within the video window and will dynamically give more screen space to the more active-speaking presenters.  This layout also uses automatic face detection and framing to optimize the view of the presenter, or presenters (if the video feed features multiple people).

The placement of presenters in the layout is based on a combination of speaking frequency and the number of faces detected in the video feed.  Those that speak more frequently or have more people in the feed will have more screen space. 

Notes:

  • Face detection/framing cannot be disabled.  If you do not want to use this feature, choose a different layout.
  • Presenters will be automatically removed from the layout if there is no face detected in the video feed.  This will happen if the video feed is muted, the camera lens is covered/moved away from the presenter, or the presenter has walked away.
  • Pinning a Presenter is not supported with this layout.

Audience View

 

Layout 3 

Layout 3 will display up to eight presenters at once. 

This layout is an excellent option for collaborative webinar formats.   All presenters will be audible and visible simultaneously, similar to a conference bridge.

Using voice-activated switching, the presenter who is actively speaking will be visible above the remaining presenters in a larger window.  

Audience View

 

Layout 4

Layout 4 will display up to four presenters simultaneously, each in a separate square.   

All presenters will be audible and visible simultaneously, similar to a conference bridge.  This layout would be ideal for a roundtable or collaborative discussion when four presenters (or less) are connected via webcam.

If there are five presenters or more on a webcam, we suggest using Layout 2 or 3.

Audience View

articles.name: Go Live Participant Guide – Chat and Polls Tabs
articles.title: Go Live Participant Guide – Chat and Polls Tabs
articles.body:
Learn how to navigate the chat and polls tabs in Go Live.

 

A chat and polls box will appear on the right side of the screen in the Auditorium, Exhibit Hall, and Lounge areas. For this example, we are in the Auditorium area in a Breakout session. 
 

Chat 

Attendees can type messages into the chat or answer the polling questions. There are several different features in the chat area.  

During Go Live events there are multiple chat channels available (assuming chat is enabled), one for the overall event (Event Chat) one for the session (breakout or keynote) you are attending (Session Chat), and one to direct chat with other attendees (Direct Chat). Navigation tabs appear at the bottom-right. 
 

 


Selecting the “Direct” button in the bottom-right navigation bar allows you to chat directly with another participant. 
 


Click on the “Attendees” tab in the bottom-right navigation bar to see all attendees in the session. 
 

 

Polls 

As a participant to respond to a session poll, click the “Polls” tab in the top right of the screen. Click the purple “Take” button. 
 

 
 

Select your answer and click the purple “Submit” button. 

 


After the poll is submitted, the results will appear (if the poll has been configured to display the results by the moderator or presenter).
 

 

You can click the show/hide toggle button in the toolbar to hide the chat sidebar.  
 

The direct chat feature has a small “X” button to close.

 

 

Pre-Event and Post-Event Chat

Attendees have the ability to participate in chat activities before and/or after an event takes place. Event organizers and attendees can connect and engage prior to an event's first live day and registrants can network prior to the start of an actual event. There is also a communication channel after the live portion of an event has concluded.

When enabled, event-level chat will be available for all attendees to participate in at any time prior to an event’s scheduled start day/time or after the scheduled conclusion of a live event (until the replay is scheduled to expire).

Steps for Pre and/or Post-Event Chat:

  • To configure pre and/or post-event chat, an admin simply needs to enable/disable the applicable toggle(s) in the Features panel of the Overview page .  
  • Visitors will then have access to the chat panel when viewing the Agenda page (before an event) or the Replay page (after an event).



Hiding the Polls and Chat tabs

If Chat has been disabled on the Overview page and there are no poll questions set up for the session, the Chat and /or Polls tabs will not appear.

articles.name: Power Leads
articles.title: Power Leads
articles.body:
Identify your best leads using Power Leads. The Power Leads report provides sortable metrics and details on which webinars a specific user has viewed, for how long, and whether or not it was viewed live, on demand, or both.


You can filter and sort leads across a custom group of webcasts using the Power Leads section under Analytics > Engagement.  This allows you to drill down to find exactly the leads that meet specific criteria and follow up on those leads with the help of our Prospect Engagement profiles.  

Metrics included: 

  • Total Engagement Score - calculated by adding together all engagement scores from each webinar attended by the attendee
  • Number of Webinars Attended
  • Total Minutes Watched
  • User Name 
  • Email Domain 
  • Company
  • Tags
  • Prospect Engagement Profile - click on the user's name to access - available with Advanced Analytics
  • Custom Leads Report - available with Advanced Analytics


Use the Filter icon to filter the report.  Use the download arrow to export the report to Excel.  You can export all columns of the Power Leads report to Excel by selecting the export icon at the top right corner. The Excel document takes filters into consideration. 
 



Click on the arrow located to the right of the viewer's name to see which webinars they've attended, for how long, and whether or not it was viewed live, on-demand, or both.  You can also see what they have registered for.

articles.name: Using Virtual Backgrounds in Elite Studio
articles.title: Using Virtual Backgrounds in Elite Studio
articles.body:
Virtual backgrounds for your presenters are useful tools that can contribute to the delivery of a consistent and branded experience to your audience. Virtual backgrounds can help add a high level of professionalism to your event. Virtual backgrounds are available for use during the following experiences:

 

Using Virtual Backgrounds

You can choose a virtual background when you first join the event in Elite Studio, or you can choose a background later in the top toolbar. You can change the background during the event if desired.

From the Join Panel or the top toolbar in Elite Studio, click the Background button to choose a background or blur.

For best results with virtual backgrounds:

  • Use a high-contrast, solid-color backdrop, preferably a green screen. A matte, non-reflective background works best.
  • Keep your background surface close to you.
  • Don’t wear clothing that’s the same color as your background or the virtual background.
  • For best results, have a solid color wall behind you. A lot of clutter behind you will yield lower quality results.

Review our presenter bandwidth requirements and technical information.

 

Uploading Virtual Backgrounds

To use a custom virtual background that aligns with your brand or console design, log in to Elite Studio as a Producer. You must also be logged into Webcast Elite in order to upload a background.

Note: Custom backgrounds will only be available to the presenting team in Elite Studio. Attendees of the event/Breakout Room will only have access to the stock images and blurring.

To add a virtual background in Elite Studio, click Setup in the top toolbar to find the Virtual Backgrounds settings.

In Setup, click the Virtual Backgrounds icon on the left menu. Here, you can click to upload or drag and drop one or more background images. Once you have uploaded an image, you can hide or delete it to make it unavailable to presenters. Only uploaded images can be hidden or deleted.

 

Image Specifications

  • We recommend 16:9 aspect ratio on the background images, and a file size not exceeding 5MB
  • Minimum Size - 360x360 px
  • Maximum Size - 2048x2048 px
  • File Type - jpeg, jpg, png, bmp (file extension is case sensitive - please use lowercase extensions)

articles.name: Go Live Participant Guide - Viewing an Elite Webinar
articles.title: Go Live Participant Guide - Viewing an Elite Webinar
articles.body:
This guide shows Go Live participants what an Elite Webinar looks like within Go Live, including how the interface looks before, during, and after a session.

When running an Elite webinar session in Go Live, the Elite webinar console will be fully embedded in the Go Live session. This allows Go Live attendees to view the same audience experience as standalone webinar viewer. This will slightly change the look and functionality of the Main Stage area. 
 

Before the session starts

Before the session starts, participants will see the console, including presentation area, the About, Speakers, and Related Content tabs, Chat, and the Next in MAIN STAGE session preview. The Entry Open tag will display from 15 minutes before the session starts.

Note: Once the session time begins, the label will change to Live Now. A banner notification stating that Entry to next session is now open along the top of the screen from 15 mins before the session start time.

 

When the session begins

When the Elite webinar lobby opens, a blue Join Now button will appear for participants to enter the session.


Inside the Webcast Elite session

The Webcast Elite console will appear when participants join the session. The console will include all Engagement Tools enabled in the Webcast Elite Audience Console.

 

Using the Go Live Chat

The Go Live chat feature is a separate channel from the chat embedded in the webinar console. If the Go Live chat is enabled, a chat icon will display in the toolbar. The Go Live chat display can be toggled on / off by clicking on the icon. When toggled on, the Go Live chat will display as a moveable layer on top of the audience console. The Go Live chat display can include Chat and Polls, and is divided into Event Chat, Session Chat, Direct Chat, and Attendees tabs along the bottom.

 

Using the Webcast Elite Chat

If chat has been enabled in the Webcast Elite console, attendees will see the webinar chat tool within the embedded webinar. This is a separate channel from the Go Live chat feature above.

 

Full screen

Click on the full screen icon on the top right to see the full screen experience. Click the same icon to return to normal view.



 

When the Webcast Ends

When the webcast ends, participants will see a message stating that the event has concluded. If a post-event survey has been enabled, participants will see the survey. If there is no survey, participants will see a message showing any upcoming sessions and any assets available from the session that concluded.

articles.name: Troubleshooting Your Webinar
articles.title: Troubleshooting Your Webinar
articles.body:
This article provides some basic steps for troubleshooting your webinar.

 

Common Webinar Issue Resolutions (Best Practices)

  1. Use a stable high-speed Internet connection. Test your connection here as needed.
  2. Use another web browser.
  3. Clear your cache.
  4. Close all other applications.
  5. Restart your computer (if there is time to reboot).

 

What to Do if...

Attendees Cannot Register

If you are hearing that attendees can’t register usually the user is trying to access the wrong URL. The Event/Audience Link is the correct URL.

  1. Go to the event’s Event/Audience Link and verify that you can register.
  2. Prospective attendees should double check the URL and try again.
  3. If they are still unable to register, advise them to try with a different browser.

 

There is No Audio Coming From the Audience Console 

If no audio is coming from the audience console:

  1. Confirm that Start Live was clicked and confirm that End Webcast was not.
  2. Confirm that presenters are connected to the event and not on mute.

 

Presenters Are Unable to Access the Phone Line

When dialing into a phone bridge, if Presenters or Producers are getting a message that the event is “not available for live streaming” or that the “passcode is unrecognized”, ensure the event ID and passcode have been entered correctly. All presenters use the same Dial-in Number and Passcode.

 

 Presenter Drops

  1. Remind your team to continuously monitor Team Chat in Elite Studio during the live event, as it is the only way to communicate.
  2. If a speaker drops during the event, immediately chat this specific Presenter in the Team Chat. Use the Presenter tool to confirm if they have dropped.
  3. As long as someone is still connected to the live event, the event will continue.

 

My PowerPoint Slides Won't Upload

All PowerPoint slides should be finalized and uploaded to the event at least 24 hours prior to the start time. This will provide enough time to troubleshoot in the unlikely event there are any issues with the deck needing to be addressed.

  1. Prior to uploading, be sure the PPT deck:
    • Is a .PPT or .PPTX
    • Is not password-protected
    • Does not open in "protected mode" due to security constraints
    • Does not have read-only embedded fonts
    • Does not have links to external content
    • Is no more than 50 MB in size
    • Has the first Slide Animation set to Start on Click
  2. Note that embedded objects (i.e. Audio, Video, Excel, SmartArt graphics, etc.) are not supported.
  3. If the PowerPoint file does not upload and the slides don’t appear in Elite Studio, most likely there is some type of conversion issue.
  4. Please do not continue to try uploading your original PowerPoint as this will exacerbate the issue and prolong the time it takes to reach a resolution.
  5. Open the file in PowerPoint to flatten the deck and try the upload again. Save the file as a PowerPoint Picture presentation .pptx, which will flatten all slide elements into one for each slide and remove all animations.
  6. If your PowerPoint does not upload successfully after this step, immediately submit a case to technical support and attach your slide deck to the case.

 

A Presenter Cannot Advance Slides

If a presenter is unable to advance slides:

  1. Have another presenter push the slides.
  2. Refresh Elite Studio (Click refresh in your browser, or F5 on your keyboard).
    • Refreshing the page will NOT end the webinar as long as another Producer or Presenter remains connected.
    • When refreshing and reconnecting, the microphone and camera are automatically ON when the new connection loads.
  3. Clear your cache.

 

The Start Live Button Isn't Active 15 Minutes Before My Live Webcast

If you have logged into the event 15 minutes prior to the live webcast and the Start Live button is not active (e.g. flashing green) and you're unable to press Start Live:

  1. Start Live is only active 15 minutes prior and up until the start time of the live webinar.
  2. Check that the Live Start Date, Time, and Time Zone are accurately set to the desired date and time for the webinar. If one or more of these happen to be incorrect, the Start Live button may not yet be active.

 

Video Presenter Bridge Troubleshooting

*The section below only applies to webinars using the Live Video Present Type.

 

No Audio Is Heard When Viewing the Preview Link

  1. Disconnect all phone and webcam connections and then reconnect.
    • A refresh may be needed after reconnecting. (Click refresh in your browser, or F5 on your keyboard.)
      • Refreshing the page will not end the webinar.
      • When refreshing and reconnecting, the microphone and camera are automatically ON when the new connection loads.
  2. Check to see if presenters are listed in the Presenter List in the Connections Panel in Elite Studio.
  3. If disconnecting and reconnecting does not solve the issue, call the Live Event Emergency Line.

 

The Presenter Doesn't See the Webcam or PC Mic in the Dropdown When Connecting

  1. Try another browser.
  2. Unplug and reconnect the device(s) to the USB
    • A refresh may be needed after reconnecting. (Click refresh in your browser, or F5 on your keyboard.)
      • Refreshing the page will not end the webinar.
      • When refreshing and reconnecting, the microphone and camera are automatically ON when the new connection loads.
  3. Reboot the computer to ensure there are no software conflicts competing for the same device (webcam or PC mic).

 

The Audience View Window Displays a Spinning Wheel or Does Not Display Shared Content

  1. If you don’t need to see the content to present, open a preview mode window to verify that something is displaying.
  2. The usual cause for this is that the network is blocking access to wss://h5live.nanocosmos.de
  3. If Audience View is absolutely necessary, submit a high priority case to ON24 Platform Support and our team will work with your network personnel to address the issue.

 

The Presenter Console Cannot Access Webcam or Screen Share

  1. Refresh Elite Studio by clicking refresh in your browser, or F5 on your keyboard. If that doesn't resolve the issue, the most likely cause is content filters are blocking either access to the TURN server, or access to the ON24 servers.
    • Refreshing the page will not end the webinar.
    • When refreshing and reconnecting, the microphone and camera are automatically ON when the new connection loads.
  2. Submit a case to ON24 Platform Support and our team will work with your network personnel to address the issue.

 

The Webcam is Grayed Out or Frozen in the Audience Console

  1. Likely caused by insufficient audience bandwidth.
  2. Can happen when connected via an unstable Wi-Fi Internet connection.
  3. To resolve, connect using a stronger and more stable Internet connection.

 

Screen Share Will Not Display in the Audience View Window or Preview

  1. Confirm each presenter is indeed connected to the presenter console.
  2. Confirm the presenter has established a connection.
    • The presenter's name should be displayed in the Presenters List in Elite Studio.
  3. A wired connection is encouraged. Wi-Fi connections are acceptable if they are strong and stable. Unstable Wi-Fi due to congestion or other causes can cause technical issues.
  4. Close all other applications and clear your cache.
  5. Try another browser.
  6. Restart your computer (if there is time to reboot).

 

Screen Share Keeps Asking to Download After Installation (Firefox)

  1. Try resetting the browser to factory settings.
  2. Uninstall the plug-in and reinstall.

 

Webcam Troubleshooting

  1. Make sure the presenter is using Chrome, Firefox, or Edge.
  2. All presenters should be on a strong and stable Internet connection. Wireless connections must be stable and reliable if used. Congestion and excessive traffic in Wi-Fi connections can cause issues when attempting to use a webcam. Test your connection here as needed.
  3. Refresh Elite Studio by clicking refresh in your browser, or F5 on your keyboard, then try to reconnect the webcam.
    • Refreshing the page will not end the webinar.
    • When refreshing and reconnecting, the microphone and camera are automatically ON when the new connection loads.
  4. Confirm the browser isn't blocking the device from being accessed.
  5. Try another browser.
  6. Clear your cookies/cache.
  7. Close all unused applications, especially those that may have access to your webcam (Microsoft Teams, etc.).
  8. Reconnect the device via the USB.
    • A refresh may be needed after reconnecting. (Click refresh in your browser, or F5 on your keyboard.)
      • Refreshing the page will not end the webinar.
      • When refreshing and reconnecting, the microphone and camera are automatically ON when the new connection loads.
  9. Reboot the computer if time permits
  10. If the issue persists after troubleshooting, it is usually related to network configurations such as VPN or Firewalls. Try connecting outside of a VPN. If this doesn't resolve the issue, please submit a case to Platform Support so our team can work with your internal IT department to find a resolution.

 

Presenters Cannot See or Hear a Single Presenter 

  1. Refresh Elite Studio by clicking refresh in your browser, or F5 on your keyboard.
    • Refreshing the page will not end the webinar.
    • When refreshing and reconnecting, the microphone and camera are automatically ON when the new connection loads.
  2. Try another browser.

 

Remember, Don't Panic

Don’t sweat the unexpected. In live television production, for example, mishaps sometimes occur. Reporters and news anchors are used to this and just casually pass to another person if there are technical difficulties.

Your live webinars are no different. Accept the fact that mistakes and miscues are possible and be prepared to handle them like a pro if they happen. Remember, don't panic! There's no need to call attention to the problem. Keep moving forward with your webinar and return to what has been planned once troubleshooting is complete.

articles.name: Drift to ON24 Registration
articles.title: Drift to ON24 Registration
articles.body:
The bot will ask the visitor for their email to register them for the event (Example Below - Revgrowth Staging) The visitor provides their email address in which a button is presented to that states, "To finalize this registration, please click this button." ON24 captures the email of the visitor and registers them for the event through the email encode at the end of the ON24 URL.

Note:  This set up is done by Drift. If interested in setting up for External Registration, contact your representative at Drift for assistance. 

 

Example

1. The bot will ask the visitor for their email to register them for the event (Example Below - Revgrowth Staging)



2. The visitor provides their email address in which a button is presented to that states, "To finalize this registration, please click this button." This button has the ON24 URL embedded into the finalization button.

http://event.on24.com/interface/registration/autoreg/index.html?eventid=#EVENTID#&sessionid=1&key=#KEY#&email={{user.email|urlencode}}

3. ON24 captures the email of the visitor and registers them for the event through the email encode at the end of the ON24 URL:

4. The visitor is taken to an ON24 landing page that states the registration was successfully completed. (Example Below)

articles.name: Using Screen Share in Elite Studio
articles.title: Using Screen Share in Elite Studio
articles.body:
This article outlines how to use the screen share tool within Elite Studio. Screen share is only available when using the Live Video present type.

Note: Beta screen share is available for iPads. This feature will be enabled by request only. Please contact Support for details. The ON24 Screen Share app will need to be downloaded from the app store. Screen share is not available on Safari browsers v15 and older or any other operating system.
 

Using Screen Share

Log into Elite Studio and connect to the presenter interface. Once connected, you'll see the Screen Share icon in the tool bar.

Note: Screen Sharing requires the presenter who is intending to share to connect to the event via the connection panel to support the sharing stream.

Choose to share either an entire screen, tab, or just an application window.

Once a screen/application is selected, your screen will be visible in the Media Player for both attendees and presenters.

An alert appears at the bottom of your first monitor to confirm screen sharing is in progress. 

  • The alert itself may be hidden by clicking Hide. Once hidden, hold down ALT+TAB to select the extension and re-open the control bar.

To stop screen share, click the Stop Sharing button in Elite Studio or the Stop Sharing button on the screen share alert.

  • If a presenter fails to stop screen share, another presenter or a producer can start a screen share from their user interface to overwrite the current session or quickly begin and end screen share to end the current session.

If you have Picture in Picture enabled in your account, the active speaker will display in the upper right corner of the screen share image.  

Note: This feature will be implemented by request only. Contact Platform Support for details.

 

Best Practices

  • Complete a technical rehearsal to test screen share before the event
    • The sooner you complete your technical rehearsals, the more time you have to troubleshoot and engage ON24 Support, should you need assistance
    • During technical rehearsals, presenters wanting to test screen share must be connected to the Control Panel / Video Presenter Bridge in some fashion for the screen share to display in the Preview URL and/or the Media Player in Elite Studio
  • If you plan to share most of the desktop area, reduce screen resolution to 1152x648 (16:9) to conserve audience bandwidth and minimize delays
    • Also, consider reducing the color depth to "High Color (16-bit)"
  • Screen Sharing requires the presenter who is intending to share to connect to the event via the connection panel to support the sharing stream
  • Be sure to close instant messaging applications, Outlook email alerts, meeting reminders, and other windows you do not want visible to viewers
  • Remove or hide confidential files and folders from your desktop
  • Open the applications you'll need for the screen share before the webinar starts and shut down all other non-essential applications
  • Screen sharing is bandwidth-intensive and designed to demonstrate applications
    • It is not appropriate for showing videos or animations - these elements are best displayed using different methods, like pushing pre-recorded video clips
  • Screen share does not support two presenters sharing at the same time
    • Wait for the current presenter to stop screen sharing before the next presenter begins to screen share
    • If a presenter turns on their screen share before their cue, they will take control of the event, and the audience will see their screen

 

Audience View

When using screen share, the Media Player on the audience console will automatically maximize when the screen share starts and automatically minimize once the presenter stops the screen share.  

No plug-ins or downloads are required for the audience.

 

Known Screen Share Issues 

Presenters running Chrome Version 99.0.4844.74 on iOS/Mac (Big Sur 11.6 and other versions) may experience an inability to screen share in Breakout rooms (and during Live Video and Audio with screen share (Video Presenter Bridge) webinars).

To resolve this issue:

  • Open System Preferences (users may hit CMD + Space and type “pref”)
  • Open “Security & Privacy”
  • On the left, scroll down to “Screen Recording” and click it
  • Users may need to click the lock on the bottom left to make changes and enter their credentials
  • Uncheck Chrome on the right
  • On the popup, click “Quit & Reopen”
  • Re-check the Chrome box again
  • Click “Quit & Reopen”

articles.name: Earn Certification Tool
articles.title: Earn Certification Tool
articles.body:
The article details the use of the Earn Certification tool for webinars, allowing organizers to set criteria such as passing a test, answering polls, or spending a specific amount of time in a webcast for attendees to earn certification. The tool can be added via the Console Builder and configured in advance. Certificates can be customized and downloaded by attendees who meet the criteria. The tool supports both live and on-demand events, and multiple tools can be used for different criteria. The article also covers reporting features, allowing organizers to track certification statistics and download certificates.


Add the Earn Certification tool to the Console via the Tools Selector in the Console Builder tab of your event. Next, click the gear icon in the Earn Certification tool to open Configuration.

  • Things to Know
  • Full Credit Options
  • Partial Credit Options
  • Certificate & Settings
  • Using Multiple Tools
  • Certificate Download
  • Personalized Certification
  • Reporting

Things to Know

Important: The Earn Certification tool will be locked down 15 minutes prior to the event start time, and no further changes to the tool will be allowed to ensure accurate certification data. All criteria must be fully configured before this time.

Once the tool is locked, you will not be able to:

  • Make changes to the configuration of an Earn Certification tool that was previously added to the console
  • Add a new Earn Certification tool to the console

If you need to make a configuration change after an Earn Certification tool has been locked down, submit a Support ticket to find out additional options. 

 

Additional Things to Know

  • Multiple Certification tools for LIVE events may result in unexpected reporting data due to system processes. We recommend using only one tool for the live event and set up the credit options as needed.
    • If you need to use different criteria for LIVE events vs. ON-DEMAND events, see the Using Multiple Tools tab
  • Changing any criteria in the tool post event will impact historical data and is not recommended.
  • The Earn Certification tool supports limited updates after an event ends - if enabled, you can update the downloadable certificate and adjust certification criteria based on revised standards. 
    • Note: Post-event changes are disabled by default, as they can cause inconsistencies. If your workflow requires them, contact Support to enable. Updates may take up to 24 hours to appear in the Audience Console and reports.
  • If you're using the Download Certificate token in your Thank You for Attending emails, we recommend scheduling the emails to be sent the day after your event. This allows time for the system to load the data from the Certification tool.

Full Credit Options

Click the Full Credit tab to set up criteria attendees must meet in order to get full credit.

Decide how this set of criteria will be labeled in the tool for the audience. By default, it will be labeled Criteria for Full Credit. Click the pencil icon if you'd like to enter new text for the label.

Next, set the criteria attendees must meet in order to get full credit by toggling the following criteria on/off.

Required Viewing Minutes

  • The amount of time an attendee must view the event
  • Live and on-demand minutes are tracked and calculated separately - viewing minutes are NOT cumulative. For example, an attendee cannot watch 5 minutes of a live webinar and 10 minutes on demand to meet a 15-minute viewing requirement.
    • Live viewing minutes are based on how long the console was open, starting at the scheduled webinar's live start time. The lobby time and any activity after the webinar ends is not counted.
    • On-demand viewing minutes are based on the duration of the play of the Media Player. There is the possibility of cheating if the user were to use the seek bar to skip through the event - the Media Player can be configured to prevent this.
    • If you need one set of criteria for live viewers and one set for on-demand viewers, you can set up multiple instances of the Earn Certification tool in your console - see the Using Multiple Tools tab.

Required Number of Polls to Answer

  • Standard poll questions are created in Elite Studio
  • This option should be toggled ON if you set up an Automated Attendance Check that is required in order to earn certification in LIVE events
    • See the Certificate & Settings tab for more information

Require Completed Test

  • The test information is based on the passing requirement set in the test criteria of the Take Test tool

Message for Full Credit

  • Customize the message attendees will see once they meet the certification requirements

Earned Credit for Completion
