  • Enter the number of credits attendees can earn for meeting all of the certification criteria

Partial Credit Options

Click the Partial Credit tab to set up criteria attendees must meet in order to get partial credit.

You can offer up to 14 levels of credit for a webinar. Click Add Partial Credit Option to add a level of credit.

By default, it will be labeled Criteria for Partial Credit Option 1. Click the pencil icon if you'd like to enter new text for the label.

Just like with the Full Credit option, next you'll decide the criteria attendees must meet in order to get partial credit by toggling the following criteria on/off:

  • Required Viewing Minutes
  • Required Number of Polls to Answer
  • Message for Partial Credit Complete
  • Earned Credit for Completion

The tool will track an attendee's progress through the certification levels.

When configuring multiple credit levels, make sure to define increasing criteria for each subsequent level. The example shown in the screenshot below has increasing criteria and is configured correctly.

  • Correct Configuration - e.g., Level 1 requires 10 minutes, Level 2 requires 20 minutes, Level 3 requires 30 minutes, etc.
  • Incorrect Configuration - e.g., Level 1 requires 30 minutes, Level 2 requires 20 minutes, Level 3 requires 10 minutes

Certificate & Settings

Certificate

Click on Certificate to choose if you want include a certificate and to set up the certificate.

Include Certificate

Attendees can download or print a PDF certificate upon meeting the certification requirements. Use the certificate image provided or upload your own.

First, toggle on Include Certificate. 

Click Change Certificate Image if you'd like to upload a custom certificate image.

  • JPG or PNG
  • Custom image dimensions should be 11 x 8.5 inches (landscape) or 8.5 x 11 inches (portrait)
  • 150 DPI recommended
  • 500KB Max file size

Next, click Add to add any of the following information to populate in the certificate:

  • Participant Name and Event Name - will pull information from the registration
  • Date - will automatically fill with the date the certificate was earned
  • Credit - will fill with the number of credits the attendee earned watching the webinar
  • Free Text - allows you to add custom text. See below for how to add custom text or additional registrant information to the Certificate.

Once added, you can drag and drop the field where you'd like it to appear on the certificate image. You can change the font and the size of the text if desired.

To preview the certificate, use the Preview Link to preview the Console, then open the Earn Certification tool and click on the certificate icon. Previewing the certificate is an important step to ensure tokens are displaying as expected. You can adjust the alignment as needed and preview until you are satisfied with the display.

Add Custom Text or Additional Registrant Information

When customizing your certificate, the Free Text option under Add will allow you to add custom text to the certificate, including information captured from any registration fields set up on your Registration page (using the tokens listed below).

The example below illustrates how to include the License # and Job Function registration fields. 

Click and drag the Free Text option onto the certificate image. Then click into the box and begin typing.

Using the tokens listed below, Job Function and License # have been added to the certificate.

From the Registration tab of your event, hover over the field on the registration fields to see the database ID so you can use the appropriate token. For example, License # is in field STD1 in the registration setup, so STD1 token was used on the certificate.

 

Supported User Tokens

The list below indicates the Registration Field and Database ID followed by the Token.

  • First Name | @@FIRSTNAME@@
  • Last Name | @@LASTNAME@@
  • Company | @@COMPANY@@
  • Email | @@EMAIL@@
  • Job Title | @@JOBTITLE@@
  • City | @@CITY@@
  • State | @@STATE@@
  • Postal Code | @@ZIP@@
  • Country | @@COUNTRY@@
  • Street Address1 | @@ADDRESSSTREET1@@
  • Street Address 2 | @@ADDRESSSTREET2@@
  • Industry | @@COMPANYINDUSTRY@@
  • Job Function | @@JOBFUNCTION@@
  • Custom Field 1 through 10 | @@STD1@@
    • For each custom field replace the number with the number of the custom field ie: Custom Field 2/STD2 | @@STD2@@, Custom Field 3/STD3 | @@STD3@@

 

Settings

Click on Settings to set up Automated Attendance Checks, Group Viewing, and when Certification is allowed.

Automated Attendance Checks

Enable the Automated Attendance Check to automatically deliver a single answer pop-up for attendees to answer during the event. This check will not interfere with the delivery of the content, so presenters will not see or be required to manage this.

 

Things to Know

  • If you want Automated Attendance Checks to be required in order to earn certification, you will also need to enable the Required Number of Polls to Answer in the Credit option(s)
    • Automated Attendance checks are considered polls. If you do not enable Required Number of Polls, the attendance check will still be shown to attendees, but will not be a requirement for earning certification
    • All responses to attendance checks will be captured in the Interactions section of Webcast Intelligence reports under Polls
  • Supported for Live and Sim-2-live webinars only. Automated attendance checks are NOT supported for Simu-live and on-demand viewings.
  • If you have multiple Certification tools on your console, the Automated Attendance Check option only has to be turned on in one of the tools. Once you have turned it on in one of the Certification tools, the field will be disabled in the other Certification tool(s).

 

Set Up

  • Enter a custom message to display to the attendees
  • Choose how often you want the check to appear
    • The attendance check will display within 10% of the interval entered, meaning the checks will be less predictable, so attendees can not cheat the system
  • Choose the display duration (30 seconds, 1 minute, or 2 minutes)
    • The check will disappear once the time has passed
  • Choose if an audio chime will play for the attendees when the check displays on their console
    • Due to an inherent limitation in iOS browsers and how they work with ON24, the audio notification chime will not work for anyone using iOS

Attendee view

Allow Certification

Choose whether attendees can earn certification during Live events only, On-Demand events only, or Live and On-Demand viewings.

Select one of the following options under Allow Certification when:

Live Only

  • Choose this option if your certification program requires viewers only to earn credit during a live webinar
  • Users who receive their certificate during a live webinar will still be able to access and download their certificate on-demand

On-Demand Only

  • Choose this option if your certification program allows viewers to earn credit when watching the event on-demand

Live and On-Demand

  • Choose this option if viewers can earn credit either watching the event Live OR on-demand
  • The same set of criteria will be used for both types of viewers - changing any criteria in the tool post event will impact historical data
  • If you need one set of criteria for live viewers and one set for on-demand viewers, you can set up multiple instances of the Earn Certification tool in your console - see the Using Multiple Tools tab
Allow Group Viewing

Audiences can view webinars as a group to earn certifications. 

Select On under Allow Group Viewing.

When Group Viewing is enabled in the Certification tool and all certification criteria is met, viewers will see a Open Group Viewing Form button.

When an audience member opens the Group Viewing form, they must attest the additional participants met the certification criteria. Attendees who are part of this group can access their certificate can access their certificate just like any other certified attendee.

Using Multiple Tools

The intended design and use case for the Earn Certification tool is to support one set of criteria, per event, with the same criteria expected for everyone for life of the event.

If you need to have one set of criteria for certification for LIVE viewers, and one set of criteria for certification for ON-DEMAND viewers, you can use multiple instances of the tool in your event.

 

Set Up

To start, add two instances of the Earn Certification tool to your console. 

Configure each tool as needed, setting one up with the requirements for your LIVE viewers and the other up with the requirements for your ON-DEMAND viewers.

We recommend changing the names of the tools, calling one Live Viewing Certification and one On-Demand Viewing Certification, to help you keep track of which tool is which on the console and in the reporting.

You will still be able to see both tools using the Preview URL.

 

Earn Certification Tool for Live Viewing

Set up the Live Viewing Certification tool with the live viewing certification criteria, then choose Allow Certification when: Live Only option under Settings in tool Configuration.

Attendees who meet the Live Viewing Certification criteria can download their certificate when they have met the required criteria during the live event. A certificate icon will appear in the Live Viewing Certification tool window for download. The icon will open the certificate as a PDF in a new browser tab.

Note: The Live Only version of the Earn Certification tool will be automatically hidden during on-demand viewing, unless that attendee already earned the live viewing certificate during the live event. If they earned the live viewing certificate during the live event, it will still be available in the console for them to download during on-demand viewing.

 

Earn Certification Tool for On-Demand Viewing

Set up the On-Demand Viewing Certification tool with the On-Demand event criteria, then choose Allow Certification when: On-Demand Only option under Settings in tool Configuration. 

Attendees who meet the On-Demand Viewing Certification criteria can download their certificate when they have met the required criteria during the on-demand event. A certificate icon will appear in the On-Demand Viewing Certification tool window for download. The icon will open the certificate as a PDF in a new browser tab.

Notes:

  • The On-demand Only version of the Earn Certification tool will be automatically hidden during the live event.
  • If an attendee earns both the live viewing certificate during the live event, and the on-demand viewing certificate during the on-demand event, both versions of the tool will be available to them in the console during on-demand viewing to download either certificate they've earned. 

Certificate Downloads

There are several ways to access earned certificates for both attendees and event creators.

 

From the Audience Console

  • Attendees can download their certificate when they have met the required criteria for the event
  • A certificate icon will appear in the tool window, allowing the attendee to download their certificate
  • Clicking the icon will open the certificate as a PDF in a new browser tab
  • It can take 1-2 minutes after meeting the criteria for the tool to show the attendee they have passed

 

From a Post-Event Email

  • You can add a Download Certificate token to the Thank You For Attending email so attendees can easily download their certificate after the event, even after they've exited the console
  • If the attendee earned multiple certificates, all versions of certificates earned will be available at the link

 

From the Earn Certification Reporting

  • Certificates are available in the Earn Certification Reporting in the Webcast Intelligence Report Interactions Tab. See the Reporting tab for more information.

 

Certificate Access for Simu-live Webinars

The audience console will close automatically after the recorded presentation is complete. To ensure your attendees have enough time to download their certificate at the end of the webinar, include extra time in your pre-recorded media.

Attendees can also access their certificate once the webinar is available on-demand, or by using the download link in the Thank you for attending email if configured.

Personalized Certification using Segments

Note: This feature is available with the AI-Powered ACE package. 

With AI-Powered ACE, the Earn Certification tool has segmentation capabilities that allow you to deliver personalized and relevant certificates to attendees in different segments created in Segment Builder.

For example, you could define a "California Attendees" segment and an "Arizona Attendees" segment in Segment Builder based on a registration field, then configure the Earn Certification tool so that attendees in each segment receive unique certificates:

  • Attendees in the "California Attendees" segment are presented with a certification that requires watching 30 minutes and passing a test. If they fulfill the criteria, they earn a California-specific downloadable certificate.
  • Attendees in the "Arizona Attendees" segment are presented with a certification that only requires 25 minutes of viewing. If they fulfill the criteria, they earn an Arizona-specific downloadable certificate.

While all attendees will see the same tool container in their audience consoles, the content displayed within it will vary for each attendee based on their segment. The same applies to reporting—if an attendee did not see a version of the tool, they will not be included in that version’s report.

 

Setting Up Personalized Certification For Different Segments

To begin, open the Earn Certification Tool Configuration menu by clicking the gear icon on the tool in your console. 

Under the Certification tab, you can select Certification for All or Personalized Certification.

  • If you choose Certification for All, you can set up the tool to display one standard, single certification to all attendees.
  • If you choose Personalized Certification, you can choose segments and certification criteria to deliver a unique certification to attendees in the selected segments. You can add multiple Personalized Certifications with different segments and criteria.

Click Personalized Certification to begin selecting your segments and criteria.

Select the desired Segments for the Personalized Certification from the dropdown menu and Save.

Note: The Fallback Option is available for attendees who do not fall into a selected segmentation category. This can be configured after Personalized Certifications have been configured.

Once segments have been selected, you can configure unique certification criteria for the Personalized Certification by adjusting the options in the Full Credit, Partial Credit, and Certificate tabs (in the same way you'd configure one single Certification for All.)

Once set up is complete, you'll see all Personalized Certifications you've created in the Certification tab. For Each Personalized Certification, you'll see the name of the segment(s) it's shown to and the criteria for certification. You can also can view the certificate, reorder the certifications in the list, and edit or delete the Personal Certification.

When previewing your audience console from the Console Builder, you can select segments to see the audience view for attendees in that segment, then review how the Personalized Certificate will look in the tool.

Reporting

The certification results are available within the Reports URL of the webcast, under the More tab in Interactions.   Click View Details to see attendee information.

The certification report can be exported to Excel.  Data may be delayed up to 300 minutes.

The top of the View Details section will provide a summary of the certification statistics, including:

  • Unique Views - The number of unique individuals who viewed the tool
  • Total Views - The total number of times the tool was viewed by attendees
  • The number of attendees who received full credit, partial credit, or not certified

Below the graph you will see a detailed list of attendees.  Use the column picker at the top of the list to select which fields are shown, including any registration fields used in the event.   

Quickly see who has earned their certification using the symbols next to each attendee: 

  • Green Checkmark - this attendee met all of the requirements
  • Yellow Circle - this attendee only met some of the criteria
  • Red X - this attendee did not meet the requirements

The criteria you set for the event will also be shown, with detailed information about the number of minutes attendees watched live and/or on-demand, how they met the criteria you set, and how many credits they have earned for this event.

Note: If a viewer used an increased playback speed when watching the event on-demand, the number of minutes may differ between the Audience tab and the qualification for certification.  For example, a 30 minute viewing requirement is part of certification.  An attendee watches the full 30 minutes at 2x playback speed, meaning they were in the event for 15 minutes.

  • The certification report will show as qualified since they watched the full 30 minutes
  • The Audience tab will only show 15 minutes viewed 
  • Both are correct

If an attendee received certification, the Attended column will show when they got certified, either during the Live or On-Demand Viewing.

If an attendee did not receive certification, the Attended column shows simply how they watched the event.

When using the Group Viewing option in the tool, you'll be able to see the email address of the individual who entered the additional names using the Attester column, found in the column picker.

To print a single certificate for one of your attendees use the download icon at the end of their row.  This will open a PDF of the attendee's certificate.

To bulk download certificates, check the box next to the name of the applicable attendees then click Download Certificates.  We will create a download file and send an email with the PDF links to the address associated with your ON24 account when the file is ready.

Maximum of 50 selectable rows per download.

articles.name: Webinar Presenter Preparation
articles.title: Webinar Presenter Preparation
articles.body:
Regardless of how good your content is, the success of your webinar ultimately lies in the quality of your speakers. Great presenters can make the difference between a mediocre webinar and an exciting and engaging conversation. Experienced speakers combine talent and preparation.

The following information can help prepare presenters with key steps for rehearsal, preparation and execution to ensure you are ready to deliver great webinars.

 

  • Rehearsal
  • Preparation
  • Execution
  • Connecting
  • Best Practices
  • Troubleshooting

Rehearsal

Rehearse like you are in front of an audience 

  • Script out the first slide or two of your presentation to get you started
  • Rehearse like you are in front of an audience
  • Time your rehearsals and adjust as needed
  • Have a dress rehearsal to determine the flow of the presentation
  • Leverage interactive tools like polls and Q&A to engage your audience
  • Prepare seed questions for Q&A session
  • Have support staff help you manage Q&A queue
  • Hosts: Script out intro and housekeeping

Preparation

Use this checklist to make sure everything is ready to go

  • Review slides after they have been loaded into the webinar platform
  • Print out a copy of slides
  • Find a quiet place to present from
    • Avoid presenting from large rooms or public places
    • Turn off air or heating systems, your audience will hear the noise
    • Put a “Do not disturb” sign on your door
    • Turn cell phones off
  • Plug computer into a charger
  • Have a glass of water close by
  • Present from headset or handset, no speakerphone or cell phones
  • Have all speakers log into event 30 minutes before your webinar to run sound checks and make sure everything is working properly

Execution

Remember to make your webinar conversational

  • Speak TO your audience, not AT them. Don’t give a presentation, have a conversation.
  • Sneezes, coughs, throat clearing, or even the occasional mistakes are ok.  They simply remind people that you are real.
  • Have someone dedicated to reviewing and prioritizing questions, so you can focus on the presentation
  • Don't multi-task during the event - resist the urge to answer emails, etc. outside of Elite Studio
  • Use the Team Chat tool in Elite Studio to communicate with other team members during the event
    • Keep in mind, everything entered into the chat is recorded in the webinar report
  • Keep an eye on the time
    • Don’t extend the length of the webinar beyond the advertised time
    • Leave at least ten minutes for Q&A
  • Don’t forget to breathe
  • Have fun! If you have fun, your audience will too.

Connecting

First Time Presenter Access:

When presenter’s first access Elite Studio, they will be prompted to grant access to their webcam and microphone:

Presenters MUST click Allow in order to successfully connect to Elite Studio.
After clicking Allow, Elite Studio will automatically attempt to connect to the presenter’s default camera and microphone. If the presenter has multiple webcams and/or microphones available, they will be able to change the device that is used after the initial connection has been established.

After selecting the desired webcam and microphone, clicking Join Now will connect the presenter to the event.

Best Practices

  • Clear the browser’s cache prior and restart your computer prior to the event.
  • A wired connection is encouraged. Strong Wi-Fi connections are acceptable if they are stable. 
    • Unstable Wi-Fi due to congestion or other causes can result in connectivity drops adversely impacting a live event.  We recommend testing your connection to ensure you meet the 1 Mbps requirement for presenters.
  • In order of preference, Google Chrome, Firefox, and Microsoft Edge are the supported browsers presenters MUST use. 
    • Ensure your browser is up to date.
  • Close unneeded browser tabs/windows and applications prior to accessing Elite Studio as a presenter or producer.
    • This will free up resources/bandwidth and result in an overall better experience during the presentation.
  • Do not run another video/webcam/screen share in any other collaboration app while running Elite Studio.
  • Connect to the event 30 minutes prior to the scheduled start time to test your connection.

 

Audio 

  • If you are speaking into a microphone and have speakers on, the speaker sound return is suppressed by Echo Cancelation software; this software varies in efficiency. 
    • If not fully effective, the audio seems to stutter or get clipped a little. 
    • This can be avoided by wearing headphones or earbuds, anything that avoids the audio return being heard by the microphone.
  • If joining the event by phone, avoid: 
    • Shared DSL/telephone line service - your voice will sound compressed and have a very limited frequency range.
    • VOIP vendors that offer service over the public internet - Most of the top VOIP manufacturers have very good voice quality within the company LAN, but there are a number of smaller manufacturers of VOIP gateways and systems that over-compress and gate their voice calls to gain bandwidth. This results in poor-quality audio.

Troubleshooting

If you are experiencing connection issues during your practice session or live event try the following steps. Typically connection issues are due to Network or Browser security settings.

  • Check to make sure ON24 has access to your camera and microphone in the browser tab and verify the browser is unmuted. 
  • Disconnect from VPN if possible.
  • Close any open browser tabs and computer applications not needed for the presentation to ensure your browser has dedicated bandwidth.
  • Clear Cache/Cookies from all time - this will ensure a 'Fresh Start' for the connection.
  • Restart your computer.
  • Try a different browser (Edge, and Chrome are suggested). Experiences with Breakout Rooms, Forums, and 'NextGen Live Video' do not support the use of Firefox.
  • Lower the Data Rate of your connection in Elite Studio
    • Click on Settings from the Connection Panel , and adjusting the Data Rate to Low or Medium.  Find more information on Presenter Bandwidth Requirements here.
  • Try a different network. Your connection may be blocked by network security settings that need to be resolved before the event with the help of your IT team via allow listing and network setup. Trying a new network or "hotspot" often resolves connection issues.
  • Try a different machine. If your company issued machine has certain security settings that can not be modified a personal machine will likely be a better option.
  • If you are unable to resolve the issue please contact support. If you are unable to submit a ticket email support@on24.com. Please include event ID and specifics about your issue.

articles.name: HubSpot to ON24 Registration
articles.title: HubSpot to ON24 Registration
articles.body:
HubSpot can be set up to send registrant data from HubSpot to ON24. Follow the steps below to set up a new registrant form in HubSpot, register a user, and send the data to ON24 Webinars.

  • ON24 Set Up
  • HubSpot Set Up
  • Test Connection
  • Review & Publish
  • Confirm Data Has Synced

ON24 Set Up

Event ID and Event Key

You'll need the Event ID and Event Key from ON24 in order to set up the connection with HubSpot.  Click on the accordion to see where to find this information in each ON24 product.

Webcast Elite
For webinars, grab the Event ID and key from the Audience URL of the event.  The highlighted text is the portion you need.
Example HubSpot URL:
https://seamlessreg.on24.com/hubspot?eventid=1969483&key=7B54B410106033B31B21B3F14389F66B
Engagement Hub
For Engagement Hub, grab the Event ID and key from the Seamless Registration URL, located in the Registration tab of your Hub.  The highlighted text is the portion you need.
Example HubSpot URL:
https://seamlessreg.on24.com/hubspot?eventId=1429158&key=78D4FBB7CB810F87E2A48A3B2F7CDFFA
Virtual Conference
For Virtual Conference, grab the Event ID and key from the Overview, located in the Overview tab.  The highlighted text is the portion you need.
Instead of a key, you'll use the Preview Password.
Example HubSpot URL:
https://seamlessreg.on24.com/hubspot?eventid=3759786&key=on24testing
Go Live
For Go Live events, grab the Event ID and key from the Seamless Registration URL, located in the Registration tab of your event.  The highlighted text is the portion you need.
Example HubSpot URL:
https://seamlessreg.on24.com/hubspot?eventid=4416679&key=0CA7C60C6976A589E4BD4F96960EDF3C

 

ON24 Registration Form

Important: The Login Only Option toggle should be ON to use Seamless Registration. You can find the toggle under Settings on the webinar's Registration setup page

Set up the event's registration fields in to match the fields used in HubSpot, then toggle Login Only ON in the event's registration page settings.

Once the user fills out the HubSpot registration form, the user sees an ON24-hosted "Thank you for registering, this event will start on..." lobby page and they get an ON24 cookie that serves as the persistent storage to identify the user. 

If the registrant clears their cache, their previously stored cookie will be removed. If they try to enter the event, they will see the registration page, asking them to enter their email address.  They can access the event by entering the same email address used when registering for the event.  

HubSpot Setup

Log in to HubSpot and navigate to Automation on the top menu bar, then select Workflows from the drop-down menu.

To set up a new Webhook, click on Create Workflow.

Under the Start from Scratch tab, select the Contact-based option > Blank workflow > Next.

Click Set up triggers, then choose Form Submission under Filter Type and select the form you'd like to apply to the workflow.  Click Apply Filter then Save.

Click on the + 

You'll have two options for sending registrants to ON24 - using the ON24 app or using webhooks.  Click on the accordion that fits your use case to see more information.

Send Registrants to ON24 using the ON24 App
Select Send Contact Data to ON24 under Integrated apps > ON24 App.
Enter the required Event Id and Event Key in the provided text boxes; also map the required Email address.
You can map additional fields of data you wish to pass to ON24. We recommend First Name, Last Name, and Company as a minimum.
The workflow is ready to be published.
Send Registrants to ON24 using a Webhook

Select Send a Webhook. Under Method, select POST.

Enter your Webhook URL:

  • For NA database: https://seamlessreg.on24.com/hubspot?eventid=xxxxx&key=yyyyy
  • For EU database: https://seamlessreg.eu.on24.com/hubspot?eventid=xxxxxx&key=yyyyyy

Once you have the full Webhook URL, enter into HubSpot and click Save.

Test Connection

To test the connection, click on Test in the top corner, then choose a contact to test.  

Click Send me the HubSpot emails this workflow would deliver and click Test.

A Workflow completed message will appear to let you know the test was successful.

Review & Publish

Once the workflows are setup and tested, you'll need to review and publish.  Click Review and Publish

Confirm Existing Contacts selection

Click TURN ON in the upper right corner:

Click the MARKETING tab and then select LANDING PAGES from the drop-down menu:

Select the Landing Page you would like to use:

Drag and Drop the Form icon onto your Landing Page:

Select the form that you would like to use from the drop-down menu:

Click Save in upper left-hand corner:

Click Publish in upper right- hand corner:

Click Publish now:

You should see a successfully published message:

 

Confirm Data Has Synced

Copy the URL and paste it in your browser:  

Complete the Registration Form and click SUBMIT:

You will receive confirmation that the Registration form was submitted:

Confirm Registration data has synchronized in ON24.

Webinar Data

From the Settings of your event registration page, click on Manage Registrants and check the data has synchronized.

Engagement Hub Data
From the Registration tab of your Engagement Hub, open Manage Registrants and check the data has synchronized.
Virtual Conference Data
From the Reports tab of your Virtual Confernence, launch VC Analytics. 
From there, open the Registration tab and click on the filter icon to populate registrants.

articles.name: Go Live Participant Guide - Pinning and Spotlighting a Presenter
articles.title: Go Live Participant Guide - Pinning and Spotlighting a Presenter
articles.body:
Learn how to pin (display one or more video streams even if not presenting) and spotlight (show only those who are pinned) in Go Live.

Pinning a Presenter

Pinning allows presenters and moderators to always display one or more video streams in the video grid for Go Live even if they are not speaking.

For example, if three people are pinned, their video will always display in the video grid and the other video slots will dynamically display based on voice activation.
 

To pin people, click the dots next to their name in the video player (visible when hovering over a participant) and click Pin. Note that the presenter's webcam must be on to be pinned.


 

Spotlighting a Presenter

Spotlighting allows you to only display those who are pinned. Presenters must be pinned to be spotlighted.  For example, if three people are pinned and you enable Spotlighting, only those three people will display in the video grid.

While Spotlighting is enabled, you can add people to the Go Live video display by pinning them or remove people from the video display by unpinning them.

To enable Spotlighting, click the Spotlighting icon in the header of the video window with one or more participants pinned.


 

Note: Spotlighting only works during the live session. The archive will still display all non-spotlighted webcams as well. This will be fixed in a follow-up release.


 

Blurring Your Background

To blur your background, click the ... in the toolbar and then the Settings wheel.

From the webcam preview, click the blue circle to blur your background.

articles.name: Engagement Hub & Target Troubleshooting
articles.title: Engagement Hub & Target Troubleshooting
articles.body:
Help Page covering ON24 Engagement Hub and ON24 Target to answer technical questions and FAQs.

Minimum Requirements 

The following requirements may exceed your current system configuration however, these specifications are recommended in order to provide an optimal experience listening to/and or viewing events and experiences powered by ON24.

Supported System Configurations

  • Windows 10+ (Microsoft Edge, Firefox, or Chrome)
  • Apple Mac OS 10.10+ (*Latest Firefox, Safari, or Chrome)
  • Android 9+ (Chrome Browser Only)
  • Apple iOS 12+ (*Latest Safari Browser)

* Official support for the "latest" version of a newly released browser, among those noted above, will be added within 8 weeks of public release. Until then, the previous version will continue to be supported instead.

If you are using an unsupported version of a Windows, Mac, or Linux operating system, you may experience difficulty in accessing an event, engagement hub, content experience and/or listening to a webcast.

Internet Browsers 

  • Microsoft Edge (*Latest)
  • Mozilla Firefox (*Latest)
  • Safari (*Latest, Mac Only)
  • Google Chrome (*Latest)

* Official support for the "latest" version of a newly released browser, among those noted above, will be added within 8 weeks of public release. Until then, the previous version will continue to be supported instead.

Note about Internet Explorer: The ON24 platform no longer supports Internet Explorer. Audience members that try to access Engagement Hub and Target content in Internet Explorer will see a new landing page that instructs them to download and use a supported and recommended browser. 

 

 

Internet Connection

Before you access the event, you should ensure that your browser is configured to stream media.

  • For Audio events, we recommend a minimum Internet connection of 128 Kbps.
  • For Video events, a minimum Internet connection of 800 Kbps is recommended for an optimal experience.

Frequently Asked Questions 

The Engagement Hub or (Target) Content Experience URL in the confirmation email doesn't work.

The URL to access may have wrapped in your email program thus, truncating the URL into two sections. Check to see that the URL has not been broken, or is missing characters. You may need to cut and paste the full URL into the address field of your Web Browser.

Here is an example of a URL that has been truncated: 
https://on24.lightning.force.com/ligh
 tning/r/Knowledge__kav/ka04U0000010z7yQAA/view

I am prompted to register again even if I already registered.

If you have already registered for the event, you can enter your e-mail address in the registered user's area of the registration page to access, if applicable.

Note: You must have cookies and JavaScript enabled on your computer. If you require help or additional information on cookies and/or JavaScript, please refer to the Cookies and Javascript Link in the Event Help.

I keep receiving an error message stating that the form is missing information or I am entering an invalid e-mail address when I register.

When the registration page refreshes with an error message, check to see that all of the required registration fields have been filled out and resubmit the form.

Can I access the Engagement Hub or Target Content Experience from a different computer than the one I registered from?

If you have already registered, you can gain access regardless of the computer you initially registered from by entering your e-mail address in the returning registrant portion of the registration page.

I am asked for a password when I try to view the event.

If you are behind a firewall, your company may require a user ID and password in order to access the Internet or you may have to reconfigure your proxy settings to access this event, please check with your network administrator.

If you are behind a firewall and you are trying to view an event that is in HTTP format, you may have to reconfigure your protocol to stream this event. Please check with your network administrator.

Some Engagement Hubs or Target pages have restricted access and require a password, please contact the sponsoring company or customer support if you did not receive a password after you initially registered for the event or the password was not included in your invitation to access the event.

I can't get back to the registration page!

After you register, a cookie is placed on your computer which lets us know you've already registered. When you return to the site, you will automatically bypass the registration page. If you need to re-register, or register a different person for an event, please delete cookies from your machine, and click on the link again.

Pressing the "Launch Presentation" button doesn't do anything.

If you have pop-up blocking software installed and it is active, it may be blocking the content from opening from the Engagement Hub or Target page. You may need to temporarily disable the pop-up blocking software in order to participate in the event.

I receive an "HTTP not found" error message.

128-bit Encryption is required for your operating system and browser.

The sound works continuously, but the video freezes.

This is usually because of network congestion. Network congestion manifests itself in different ways:

  • The sound works continuously, but the video freezes.

  • The program continually stops and rebuffers.

  • The player automatically stops, requiring you to click play again to resume.

Please refer to FAQ The media player automatically stops or it continually stops and rebuffers for reference on network congestion.

The media player automatically stops or it continually stops and re-buffers.

Network congestion can occur at any time due to the following problems:

  • If you are connecting to the Internet through a slow connection, or over a congested wi-fi or cellular (mobile) network.

  • You are trying to access an event during peak Internet traffic hours.

  • If you are connecting to the Internet from a mobile device.

  • There is heavy traffic on your company network.

To alleviate network congestion, you may want to consider the following:

  • Upgrading to a faster connection

  • Minimize the number of applications you are running while accessing the event

  • Try accessing the event during an off-peak Internet traffic period.

To alleviate these problems, consider reducing the number of applications you have running. If you are accessing an on-demand event, try accessing the presentation at a later time when network congestion may have lessened.

I can't hear anything (or the sound is very faint).

Please make sure you have an up-to-date modern browser, that your speakers are connected, and the volume is turned on by clicking over to an alternate website (like YouTube).

Verify that your speakers are plugged into the power outlet, turned on and up, and connected to the audio port of the computer. Your speakers or headphones must be plugged into the sound card, not into the sound out jack of the CD-ROM drive. If you still cannot hear any audio, check your system volume settings and the volume on the media player. If your system is properly set up, then try adjusting the system volume settings:

  1. Click Start, Settings, and then Control Panel.

  2. Double-click on the Multimedia Icon.

  3. On the audio tab, turn up the volume.

If it is grayed out, this is an indication that the system is not configured for sound.

I can access an event, but I cannot view the slides or the slides are not flipping.

Try refreshing the presentation by either hitting F5 on your keyboard or closing out and re-launching the presentation.

If you have joined an event, which has already started, please wait a few moments. The slides are either synchronized with the event and will flip with the next timed marker in the presentation or are user-controlled and can be flipped using controls located below the slides.
 

My computer crashes when I try to access an event.

  • Verify that your settings meet the minimum event requirements.

  • Try clearing your browser's cache and restart the event.

I can see the video but I can't hear anything (or the sound is very faint or choppy).

Please see FAQ I can't hear anything above.

I can't see the test video.

Your media player may not be installed correctly or your player may not be configured to stream the format of the event. Verify that the player has been installed correctly and check the player settings to verify that it is set up to receive the stream format.

I can see the test video but I can't see the event video.

This may be related to incorrect proxy settings in the media player. Please see FAQ I can't see the test video above. Or you may not have permission to access live streams.

The video is choppy.

Video may be received in a choppy format due to network congestion. Please refer to FAQ The media player automatically stops or it continually stops and rebuffers for reference on network congestion.

articles.name: (Legacy) Using Presenter Media in Elite Presentations/PMXD
articles.title: (Legacy) Using Presenter Media in Elite Presentations/PMXD
articles.body:
Presenter Media is a pre-recorded video or audio file of a presentation that you can upload to use in Simu-live, Sim-2-Live, or On-Demand webinars. You can set timing for each element in your webinar storyboard to align with the timing of the pre-recorded presentation. Presenter Media files can be recorded offline, or pulled from a previously run webinar in Webcast Elite.

Note: As of June 1, 2025 ON24 will no longer support 4:3 video formats.  The Media Player tool only offers 16:9 layouts now. 4:3 videos will be letterboxed.  To ensure the best experience across modern devices, upload videos in 16:9 or widescreen formats.

Click each tab below to learn more about setting up and using Presenter Media in your events.

  • Things to Know
  • Media Player Tool Setup
  • Uploading Presenter Media
  • Setting Slide Timings
  • Publishing the Presentation
  • Downloading a Presenter Media File

Things to Know

  • This option is an alternative to recording over the phone in Elite Presentations. The Presenter Media file serves as the entire presentation, so you will not be able to record audio via dial-in.
  • Only one Presenter Media file is allowed per webinar. The file cannot be deleted, but can be replaced with a new file.
  • Presenter Media is not available to add to fully LIVE webcasts. It is used only for Simu-live, Sim-2-Live, or On-Demand webinars.
  • A short lead in and lead out buffer is suggested for Presenter Media and video clips used for Simu-Live events. As a best practice add a several second buffer to the beginning (3 - 10 second) and end of the media files. This allows for greater compatibility with other ON24 services such as Go Live, smoother transitions when needed, and more flexible conversion.
  • Presenter Media is NOT the same as Video Clips you can pull into the storyboard. Presenter Media and Video Clips are used for different purposes and require different configuration.

 

Video Clips vs. Presenter Media 

Video Clips Presenter Media
  • Added to the storyboard of the webinar
  • Used in combination with other elements such as PowerPoints, polls, and URLs
  • Supported for all webinar present types
  • Generally shorter clips
  • Recordings of ENTIRE presentations
  • Can be either audio or video files
  • Used for Simu-live, Sim-2-Live, or On-Demand webinars

Review our Video Clip specifications and instructions for formatting, uploading, and managing Video Clips.

Review our Presenter Media specifications for audio and video files.

 

Media Player Tool Setup

When setting up the Audience Console in Webcast Elite for your pre-recorded event, you'll want to configure your Media Player tool based on the file type.

In the Console Builder for your event, hover over the Media Player tool, then click the gear icon > Configuration > Media Player to open Media Player configuration options.

 

Audio-Only Presenter Media

When using Audio-Only Presenter Media, configure the Media Type as Audio Only. Configuring as Audio Only can help save space on your console.

 

Video Presenter Media

When using Video Presenter Media, configure the Media Type for video by selecting the appropriate Engagement Tool Size.

Uncheck the Auto expand while video clip or screen share is playing box if you'd like the Media Player to stay locked at the set size for the webinar duration. This ensures your audience will be able to see your slides and all the tools in the console during the event.

Uploading Presenter Media

To upload your Presenter Media file, use your event's Presenter Link to log in to Elite Presentations as a Producer.

  1. Click Setup at the bottom of Elite Presentations to open the Setup window.
  2. Under the Media tab, click the Presenter Media tab.
  3. Choose Audio (.wav) or Video (.mp4).
  4. Click Upload.

You can also upload and configure all other presentation elements for your storyboard in Setup (PowerPoint Slides, Video Clips, Polls, and URLs).

Find more information about setting up your storyboard in Elite Presentations.

Setting Slide Timings

Once the Presenter Media file is uploaded, set timings for ALL storyboard elements, including slide animations. You can add the timings manually, or capture them in real-time as the Presenter Media file plays.

The time code is when the element will display to the audience. For example, if the time code entered is 00:01:30, the storyboard element will show to the audience one minute and 30 seconds into the presentation. All time codes must be unique. You can always go back and change the time codes, if necessary.

Note: One PowerPoint slide must be uploaded for the webinar to begin streaming to your audience. This element should have a time code of 00:00:00.

If you do not plan to share a PowerPoint deck during your event, review our instructions for setting up video-only events.

 

Option 1 - Setting Slide Timings Manually for Each Element

  1. Select the element in the storyboard.
  2. Type in the time code for that element into the Time Code box.
  3. Click out of the Time Code box to automatically save the timing.
  4. Complete this process for each storyboard element, including any animations where applicable.

 

Option 2 - Capture Slide Timings in Real-Time as the File Plays

  1. Toggle ON Capture Timings in the Presentations View window.
  2. Click the Play button to start the Presenter Media file.
  3. Click the yellow Next arrow while the file is playing to capture the timings automatically, including slide animations.
  4. Click the Stop button to stop the Presenter Media file.
  5. The system will automatically save the timings.

Publishing the Presentation

Although the presentation file will save automatically once the timings are set, the presentation still needs to be published once every element has a time code.

Click on the first element in your storyboard (so the system knows to start your presentation there) and click Publish in the Presentations View window. A message window will open to confirm you want to publish.

Once the publishing process is complete, wait approximately 10 minutes, then use the Preview Link to review your entire presentation.

You can make changes to your presentation and re-publish as many times as needed. Once changes have been made, click Publish again. A message window will open to confirm you want to publish over the existing presentation. Click Continue to publish.

Note: Simu-live webinars MUST be recorded AND published at least TWO HOURS before the scheduled start time. Elite Presentations will switch to live mode two hours before the scheduled start time. NO CHANGES can be made to your event once live mode is activated, including changing the start date/time, recording elements, choosing Rollover to Live audio, etc.

Downloading a Presenter Media File

You can download and reuse a Presenter Media file from a previous event (no matter the original Present Type) to repurpose it for another presentation, or to edit it for on-demand viewing after a live webinar has run.

Note: Editing a Presenter Media file cannot be done within Webcast Elite. You must download the file, edit it offline, and upload the revised version. Once the revised version is uploaded, you'll need to republish the presentation so the revised version plays to your on-demand viewers. Learn more about editing your webinar for on-demand viewing.

Using the Presenter Link, log in to Elite Presentations as a Producer. Click Setup to open the Setup window. Under the Media tab, click the Presenter Media tab, then click Download to download the video file of your presentation.

To add the Presenter Media to the new event, log in to the new event as a Producer and follow the process for uploading a Presenter Media file. At least one PowerPoint slide must be uploaded to the new event. Publish the event when set up is complete.

articles.name: Go Live Participant Guide - Event Replay
articles.title: Go Live Participant Guide - Event Replay
articles.body:
After your Go Live event has concluded, the audience can view replays of the live event content. Replays include all recorded (and published) sessions within an event, such as keynotes, breakouts, Elite webinars, and sessions recorded within a sponsor location.

The Event Replay is a single landing page available after the event has concluded and will be available to all customers.  On-demand sessions will include the resources, speakers, and About sections that were visible in the location where the session played during the live event.

To access the Event Replay, use the Audience URL of the Go Live event. You’ll see an option to sign in, if you previously registered, or register for replay. You’ll also see how long the replay is available. 


Once you’re logged in, you’ll see the agenda with session tiles and featured speakers.

To watch any of the session replays simply click on any of the session tiles. All content will be played in a lightbox on top of the Event Replay page.


 

Post-Event Chat

Attendees have the ability to participate in chat activities after an event takes place.

When enabled, event-level chat will be available for all attendees to participate in at any time after the scheduled conclusion of a live event (until the replay is scheduled to expire).

Find more information on setting up post-live chat here.

articles.name: (Legacy) Importing Media in Elite Presentations/PMXD
articles.title: (Legacy) Importing Media in Elite Presentations/PMXD
articles.body:
This article covers importing media if you have Elite Presentations. Importing media allows you to re-purpose the Storyboard and Presenter Media from a previously run webcast and use the webcast again in a newly created Simu-live, Sim-2-Live, or On-Demand webinar.

Importing media will copy any existing storyboard elements (slides, polls, etc.) and the Presenter Media from the original webcast into the new webcast.  It will also carry over any caption files and any metadata (such as whiteboarding or highlighting and centering of engagement tools) that were part of the original webcast.  

Note: You cannot import media into a fully LIVE webcast (Live Audio, Live Video and Audio, or Broadcast Video present types).  This option is only for pre-recorded present types. 

 

Reusing a Previously Run Webcast

Create a Simu-live, Sim-2-Live, or On-Demand Recording, login as Producer, and open the Setup Window.  

Click on the Import Media tab to find a list of all the webcasts in your account that have already gone live or been recorded previously.   Scroll or use the search box to find your event.

Note: Webcasts may show in the import list before they are fully available.  To ensure all files carry over properly, review the original webcast in full using the Preview URL before using it as an import source.  Once you see the event play completely and properly in the preview, it is ready to be imported.   

Select the radio button of the webcast you want and click Import Storyboard & Presenter Media.  The entire presentation of the original webcast will copy into the new one. 

Please allow a minimum of 15 minutes for imported media to fully convert before publishing your new event. Thumbnails of media may show immediately, however files may still be copying in the background. Publishing before full import may cause truncated content and issues within the event.

Once the presentation has been imported, click Publish.  Wait approximately 10 minutes, then use the Preview URL to review the webinar in its entirety.  Your new webcast is now ready for an audience.

articles.name: Webinar Benchmark Reports
articles.title: Webinar Benchmark Reports
articles.body:
The Webinar Benchmark Report allows ON24 customers to compare their webcasts to a collection of peer webcasts and to use that data to improve the performance of their own webcast programs.

Maximize Your Webcast Performance by Using Industry Comparisons

The Webinar Benchmark Report will allow you to compare the performance of your webcasts to other users on our platform in your industry.

The report is based on data aggregated from ON24’s database of more than 23,000 customer webcasts delivered in the last 12 months.

Key performance metrics are compiled to deliver a comprehensive webinar report card, which allows you to:

  • Evaluate your webcast results relative to industry peers
  • Identify where webcast best practices can be applied
  • Measure performance improvements as you optimize webcast delivery
  • To help you optimize your overall webinar performance, ON24 offers a complete set of resources

 

Analytics Benchmark Categories

The Benchmark Report is broken into the following:

  • Engagement Score
  • Conversion Rate
  • Average Registrants
  • Average Attendees
  • Average Live Attendees
  • Average On-Demand Attendees
  • Average Live Attendees Durations
  • Average On-Demand Duration

The Benchmark Report can be exported to a PDF file by clicking the download icon in the top right corner, thereby allowing you to keep a static copy of the dynamically changing data.

 

Navigating the Benchmark Report

Click on the Analytics tab and select Benchmarking.

Upon the initial launch, a pop-up will display prompting users to choose their company’s Sector and Industry. Once the categories are selected, click Done to view the benchmark report.

If you would like to set a default Sector and Industry for your account, click the edit icon in the gray box of the legend. These settings can be changed at any time in the future.

You can also update you Sector and Industry in the Account Dashboard.

To toggle between different sectors and industry benchmarks, simply use the drop-down menu in the legend at the top of the report to select your desired category. You can also see different event use cases with the Webcast Purpose toggle.

The dashboard will provide you with graphs for each of the categories.  The dashed lines represent the benchmarks, the solid lines are your webcasts.

Hover over either line to see the score for that month.

 

Webinar Industry Benchmark Reports 

Our Webinar Industry Benchmarking Report includes a broad set of best practices, usage, and success metrics, broken down by industry and updated monthly. You will find useful data including the best days and times to run webinars and when to promote them, how fast are webinar viewers moving to mobile devices, and what are the standards for engagement and attendance across your own industry.

To get the report, simply go to the standard Benchmarking page in Analytics.

articles.name: Webinar Producer Checklist
articles.title: Webinar Producer Checklist
articles.body:
Our Webinar Producer Checklist will prepare you and your presenters for a successful webinar by taking out all the guesswork.

As you prepare for your live webcast, it’s important to review all the “need to know” details with your presenting team to ensure the webcast runs smoothly. Below you’ll find a list of system requirements for presenters, as well as, a series of checklists to help you prepare for the delivery of your live webcast. Each checklist contains a list of items you’ll want to discuss with your presenting team leading up to the live event day.

 

Minimum System Requirements For Presenters (10 Max)

Operating System Windows OS 10+ running the latest version of Chrome, Firefox, or Edge.
Mac OS 10.10+ running the latest version of Chrome or Firefox 
Browser Configuration Must accept cookies and have JavaScript enabled
Screen Resolution 1024 x 768
Connection A stable high-speed Internet connection is strongly encouraged. Wi-Fi connections are acceptable if they are stable. Unstable Wi-Fi due to congestion or other causes can result in connectivity drops adversely impacting a live event.
Connection Speed 3 Mbps or faster (upload and download speed)

 

1-2 Weeks Before Event Schedule A Rehearsal

  • Send a calendar invite to all presenters for the rehearsal
    • Include the Present URL and Dial-in Instructions
  • Send a calendar invite to all presenters with the live event details
    • Include the Present URL and Dial-in Instructions

 

One Week Before Live Event

  • Conduct a rehearsal with all producers, presenters, and Q&A screeners.  Presenters should be logged in from a desktop or laptop.  
  • Define Roles:  Who is the producer? Who is(are) the presenter(s)? Who is monitoring Q&A?  
  • At least one person must be logged in as a Producer to start and stop the webcast.
  • Define event flow (Producer begins and turns it over to X…Will the producer handle the polls or will the presenters? Will the producer read aloud questions to presenters?)
  • Share the introduction and conclusion with everyone
  • Train everyone to push slides, use the chat feature, manage Q&A (if necessary)
  • Confirm everyone received the calendar invite with the live event details
  • Confirm everyone knows to dial in 30 minutes before the live event start time
  • Remind everyone they must be on a stable high-speed Internet connection. Wi-Fi connections are acceptable if they are both strong and stable. Unstable Wi-Fi due to congestion or other causes can result in connectivity drops adversely impacting a live event.
  • Remind everyone cell phones are not ideal; connections are easily dropped
  • What if a presenter loses internet connection? What is plan B? ALWAYS have a plan B!
  • If pushing out a video clip – test it!
  • If using screen share - test it! And install the plug-in if necessary (for Firefox users)

 

30 Minutes Before Live Event

  • Confirm everyone is on a stable high-speed Internet connection
  • Confirm everyone is presenting from a headset or handset, no speakerphone or cell phones
  • Soundcheck – have presenters slowly count to 10. If there is a bad connection, they should hang up and dial back in, or choose a different connection method.
  • Have presenters confirm they can push slides at this time. Also, have them submit a message in the Team Chat.

 

15 Minutes Before Live Event

Note: A reminder that best practice is to conduct a rehearsal before the live day of the event.  Everyone should be clear on roles, responsibilities, and expectations for the live day of the event. 

  • Confirm the polls are in the correct location (if applicable)
  • Prepare seed questions for Q&A session (if applicable)
  • Remind everyone on the call to turn off all cell phones, email alerts, smart home devices (e.g., Alexa)  etc.
  • Have a glass of water handy
  • Review the flow of the event. Who will press Start Live? What’s the presenter's cue to begin speaking?
  • Remind presenters what to do when the Producer presses Stop Live. Everyone needs to understand that stopping a webinar permanently ends the webcast, and it will not have a restart option.

 

During Event

  • Be aware of the mute button – make sure you are NOT MUTED when it is your turn to speak
  • Be conscious not to breathe heavy
  • Remember to stay on the Team Chat when possible
  • Listen for your cue to begin
  • ENJOY YOURSELF! If you have fun, your audience will too!
  • When ready, stop the event. Producers and Presenters should have discussed prior the procedures for ending the webcast.  
    • Note: Stopping a webinar permanently ends the webcast. Once a webinar has been stopped (even if it has been accidentally or prematurely stopped), it cannot be restarted.

For more specifics on using Elite Studio, see the Elite Studio User Guide.

articles.name: Using Lower Third Banners in Elite Studio
articles.title: Using Lower Third Banners in Elite Studio
articles.body:
Use lower third banners to highlight speakers or introduce new sections of your presentation during webinars or Forum events. Event Producers can create lower third banners with information such as speaker name, title, credentials, or presentation section, then push the banner to appear in the Media Player at the desired time during the event.

Review each of the tabs below to learn more about setup, best practices, using lower third banners during an event, and the audience experience:

  • Things to Know
  • Setup
  • Best Practices
  • Using Lower Thirds
  • Audience Experience

Things to Know

  • Producers can create lower third banners before or during an event in Elite Studio, so they're ready to be pushed to the audience in the Media Player at the right moment.
  • Lower third banners can be removed by a Producer at the desired time after they have served their purpose in the presentation.
  • Lower Third Banners are only available in live video events or the live portion of Simu-live events.
    • To use them in Simu-live or on-demand events, copy or import from a live event archive.
  • When running a Forums event, the Lock Aspect Ratio toggle must be turned on in the Media Player Attributes.
  • All banners created will be included in the replay when you create a copy of the event.

Note: When using lower third banners, select the single webcam layout. The lower third banner applies to the Media Player.

Setup

Banners can be created before or during an event, and you can create multiple banners.

To add a lower third banner, log into Elite Studio as a Producer and open the Setup tool.

Click the Lower Third icon on the Setup navigation menu, then click Add Lower Third Banner.

Choose one of the Banner Design options, then click on the Banner Text to open a customization window.

Enter the desired text, then choose the banner color, opacity, and font styling.

The single-row full-width banner design can be configured in small, medium, or large sizes. If you are using the double row banner, you can configure each row's styling individually.

After changes are complete, click outside the editor to close it. Once all changes for that banner have been made, click Save.

When pushing a banner, it will scale with the Media Player's size.

Best Practices

Typically, most companies will have designated brand guidelines around lower third banners. Check with your company guidelines first and use those instructions when available. In the absence of specific requirements, we recommend the following best practices.

 

Webcam Setup

  • When using lower third banners, be sure your presenters adjust their webcam to show themselves in the top of the visual area.
  • When lower third banners are pushed, they can cover the speaker's face if adjustments aren't made.

 

Typography

  • Readability is incredibly important for lower third banners.
  • Avoid cursive or ornate fonts – instead, choose a sans-serif font like Lato, Segoe, Helvetica Neue, or Avenir.
  • If your brand has a specific font that works well, this is a great way to have a cohesive look throughout the event.

 

Color

  • When selecting colors, it’s usually best to select colors that won’t distract from the video you’re working on. Many lower thirds are as simple as black text on a white background, sometimes with a slight opacity.
  • If you’re working on a commercial or brand video, remember to use your company’s established color scheme. Lower third text is often added onto a background color for readability, but not always. If your lower third does have a background, make sure it provides a significant contrast to the text (e.g., if your text is white, using a black or dark color background will provide good contrast).
  • Colored text can be challenging to read, so if you use it – be sure to maximize readability by using a simple background for contrast. For dark text colors, a white background is usually best.

 

Length

  • Remember that typography on screen is different than typography in print. Your audience will only see the text on screen for a brief time, usually between 3-6 seconds.
  • It's best to keep the text in the lower third banner as concise as possible by shortening long titles and other information (e.g., CEO instead of Chief Executive Officer).

 

Placement

  • Lower thirds must be placed in the Title Safe area of the video.
  • Be sure not to cover important graphical information or people’s faces.

Using Lower Thirds

Once the event has started, Producers can push lower third banners in Elite Studio. Add the Lower Third Banners tool to the right panel from the right panel toolbar, under More Tools. 

When you're ready to push a lower third out, click Push next to a banner to activate it. A green check mark will appear when it has been pushed to the Media Player. Click Remove to remove the banner from the Media Player. 

You can also click Push on a different banner to replace the currently displayed banner with a new one.

Throughout the live webinar, use the Media Player in Elite Studio to monitor the look of the lower third banner.

 

Countdown Timer

Note: This is an Account Level setting that must be requested by submitting a Support ticket.

A default countdown timer of either 10, 20, or 30 seconds can be applied to lower third banners for all events in an account with this account-level setting.

Once a lower third banner has been pushed, the Producer will see a countdown timer appear for that lower third banner. They'll also see a Remove button next to the timer, allowing them to remove the banner from the Media Player before the timer expires if needed.

Audience Experience

The images below show different display options for lower third banners and how they display in the audience console during an event.

articles.name: Presenter Dial-in Numbers For All Present Types
articles.title: Presenter Dial-in Numbers For All Present Types
articles.body:
This page provides the international dial-in numbers for all present types for both the North America and European Union Data Centers. View the numbers by selecting the tab of your event type Live or Pre-Recorded (Simu-live/On-Demand) and expand the data center you use.

 

  • Things to Know
  • Live Events
  • Simu-live/On-Demand Events

Things to Know

  • Do not distribute these numbers to your audience.  The numbers provided are for presenter use only.
  • Up to 10 Presenters may dial-in
  • Toll-free numbers are indicated as such.  All other numbers may incur local or long distance charges depending on caller location.
    • You must be located within the named country to use the toll-free numbers 
  • Use the number closest to your location to reduce long-distance costs
  • If the number does not connect, try the next closest number to your location
  • These numbers are available for use once the event has been created in Webcast Elite
  •  

Data Centers

Most ON24 customers are under the North America Data Center, but check to see which data center your account is under before using a phone number.

North America Data Center

  • North America (NA) Data Center Present URLs begin with "https://wcc.on24.com/"
  • NA Data Center Event IDs are 7 digits long

EU Data Center

  • EU Data Center Present URLs begin with "https://wcc.eu.on24.com"
  • EU Data Center Events IDs are 10 digits long
  • EU Data Center customer accounts have a .eu in their login URL

If you aren't sure which data center your account is in, submit a Support ticket.

Live Events

The Live Video present type allows each presenter to connect to the presenter bridge using their preferred method (phone, webcam, or PC microphone). We recommend connecting via PC microphone or webcam, but you can also use the numbers below to dial-in.

NA Data Center
If your Present URL begins with https://wcc.on24.com/ use these numbers.
If there is no dial-in number for the desired country or you experience difficulty connecting to your preferred number, consider connecting to the webinar using a PC Microphone.
EU Data Center
If your Present URL begins with https://wcc.eu.on24.com/ use these numbers.
If you are based in a country with no number listed, use the Netherlands or United Kingdom local numbers or consider connecting to the webinar using a PC Microphone.

Simu-live/On-Demand Events

Use the numbers provided below to pre-record your event for Simu-live, Sim-2-Live, and On-Demand Recordings.

NA Data Center

If your Present URL begins with https://wcc.on24.com/ use these numbers

  • If running a Simu-2-Live webinar that was recorded slide by slide in the platform, the numbers in the table below will also be used on the live day when the presentation rolls over to live.  
    • Presenters who connect on webcam for the live portion cannot dial in over the phone.  They must use their PC or webcam microphone to speak live to the audience.
  • Note: For events where Video Presenter Media is uploaded for the presentation, presenters will need to dial in to these numbers instead on the day of the live event: 
    • Dial-in: 833-508-4355 (US/Canada), +1 415-481-6010 (Direct)
EU Data Center

If your Present URL begins with https://wcc.eu.on24.com/ use these numbers.  If you are based in a country with no number listed, use the Netherlands or United Kingdom local numbers or consider connecting to the webinar using a PC Microphone.

  • If running a Simu-2-Live webinar that was recorded slide by slide in the platform, the numbers in the table below will also be used on the live day when the presentation rolls over to live.  
    • Presenters who connect on webcam for the live portion cannot dial in over the phone.  They must use their PC or webcam microphone to speak live to the audience.
  • Note: For events where Video Presenter Media is uploaded for the presentation, presenters will need to dial in to these numbers instead on the day of the live event: 
    • Dial-in: +442071891872 (Direct - UK), +31208908779 (Direct - Netherlands)

articles.name: Breakout Rooms Troubleshooting
articles.title: Breakout Rooms Troubleshooting
articles.body:
If you are experiencing technical issues using a Breakout Room, please follow these troubleshooting tips.

 

Things to Know

  • Breakout Rooms can be used in Webcast Elite events and Forums events.
  • We recommend designating a separate Breakout moderator from the event's Presenter/Producer team. ON24 Breakouts was designed to facilitate a two-way conversation between the audience and a guest speaker, sales rep, product consultant, or other relevant subject matter expert during the live event.
  • Testing of Breakouts is not supported through the Preview URL or before the live event. We recommend you create a separate test event and rehearse the Breakout functionality in a live event after the Start Webcast button is pressed.
  • If you have more than 50 attendees in a Forums event, some participants may lose the ability to join on video when they return to the Forums event from the Breakout Room. When participants return to the main Forums event, the first 50 attendees will have the option to connect with a webcam.

 

System Requirements

  • Initiating screen share is not supported on the Safari web browser. If you are a moderator who needs to screen share, use a different browser (Chrome or Edge). There is no Safari limitation for attendees viewing someone else’s screen share. It is only initiating a screen share on Safari that is not supported.
  • Firefox is not a supported browser for Breakout Rooms.
  • Presenters running Chrome Version 99.0.4844.74 on iOS/Mac (Big Sur 11.6 and other versions) may experience an inability to screen share in Breakout Rooms (see fix below).
  • If joining a Breakout Room as a Moderator, you must join from a desktop or a laptop - joining via a mobile device is only supported for those joining a Breakout Room as an attendee.

As a general best practice, close all unnecessary applications and clear your cache before logging into each event.  This will improve browser performance while ensuring all recent uploads and changes are visible.

 

General Troubleshooting Steps 

Step 1:  Determine whether the problem may be caused by your network environment or hardware

You can use the following steps to determine this:

  1. Make sure you are using the latest version of a WebRTC-capable browser. Check here for a list of known major supported browsers.
  2. Make sure you have the right ports open. You can check this by running a pre-call test. The pre-call test will be at the login screen of the Breakout Room. This test should identify where there are any network issues connecting to the servers. If something is blocked, proceed to Step 2.

 

Step 2:  Confirm you are connected to the Internet

  1. Are you connected to the Internet?
  2. Web browser security settings (especially for Internet Explorer) may be too restrictive or blocking certain content
  3. There may be a security restriction enforced on your network. Review our guide on restrictive networks here.

 

Step 3:  Confirm the browser can access the camera/microphone

If the earlier pre-call test didn't show any issues with the network, but you weren't able to see the camera/microphone functioning correctly:

  1. Confirm you are allowing access to your camera and microphone when prompted by the browser. You may have blocked access and saved this preference. Check your browser settings.

  1. Check if the camera works on a different browser. For instance, if you are using Firefox, see if Chrome works instead.
  2. If you have multiple cameras, make sure the correct camera is selected.
  3. Does the camera work in the WebRTC demo?
  4. Does the camera work in another application, such as Microsoft Teams or Photo Booth?
  5. Still no luck? Contact Support - we'd love to help.

 

Known Screen Share Issue with iOS/Mac and Google Chrome Browser

Presenters running Chrome Version 99.0.4844.74 on iOS/Mac (Big Sur 11.6 and other versions) may experience an inability to screen share in Breakout Rooms (and during Live Video and Audio with screen share (Video Presenter Bridge) events).

To resolve this issue:

  1. Open System Preferences (users may hit CMD + Space and type “pref”)
  2. Open “Security & Privacy”
  3. On the left, scroll down and click “Screen Recording”
  4. Users may need to click the lock on the bottom left to make changes and enter their credentials
  5. Uncheck Chrome on the right
  6. On the popup, click “Quit & Reopen”
  7. Re-check the Chrome box again
  8. Click “Quit & Reopen”

articles.name: Presenting by Webcam
articles.title: Presenting by Webcam
articles.body:
Review ON24's best practices for presenting by webcam. We cover wardrobe, rehearsals, lighting, and how best to set up your webcam feed, so attendees receive the best possible webcam produced experience.

  • Wardrobe
  • Pre-Rehearsal Connections
  • Video Framing
  • Background & Lighting
  • Audio Settings
  • Logistics
  • Presenting a Live Event

Wardrobe

In addition to having your PowerPoint presentations prepared in advance, it's a good idea to start thinking about your wardrobe before the live webcast.

Avoid solid black clothing

  • Details on black clothing are very difficult to render on the video of a webcast.

Avoid solid white clothing

  • White reflects a great deal of light and can cause the camera to wash out your video; in effect, you will look overexposed.

Be aware of the color of your webcam background

  • Wear a complementary color so that you don't blend in with the background.

Avoid tight patterns

  • Pinstripe and herringbone patterns can cause an effect that creates strobing and can't be adjusted on camera. 

Pre-Rehearsal Connections

We strongly recommend that you conduct a technical rehearsal with all of the presenters at least two days before the live webcast. We recommend the presenters use the computers and webcams they would use on the live event date.

Below are some troubleshooting tips when connecting to the presenter tool via webcam.

Video and Audio Sources

  • Ensure the video source and audio source are the same ones you intend to use for the live event
  • When connecting a laptop to a docking station, be sure to connect any external USB Webcams or Mics to the docking station's USB ports, rather than the laptop's USB ports
  • ON24 Supports physical webcam devices
  • Note: While virtual webcams are not blocked, they are not a part of our supported test matrix.  ON24 cannot guarantee or predict outcomes.  If issues arise, please be prepared to switch to supported physical webcams.

Computer Volume

  • If you cannot hear the other presenters speaking through the presenter tool, check your computer speaker volume
  • If your volume is adjusted correctly, you may need to ask the other presenter to increase the gain on their microphone

Connectivity

  • We strongly recommend a high-speed and stable connection for webcam presenters. Wired connections using an Ethernet cable are encouraged. 
  • Congestion and excessive traffic with Wi-Fi connections can cause instability, connectivity drops, and technical issues

Close Other Applications

  • To conserve bandwidth, we recommend closing all applications except for Elite Studio while presenting. 

Video Framing

During the rehearsal, presenters should only have Elite Studio open in their browser

  • If you are using the audience view to frame your shot, either the Audience Console or the presenter should be muted to prevent an audio feedback loop.
  • Once you have the appropriate framing, you MUST close the audience view.

Position the camera at eye level

  • Position the camera right in front of you and at eye level. Engage with your audience by looking naturally into the camera.

Look into the camera

  • While you will be managing slides within the presenter tool and glancing at your screen to advance slides or content, be sure to look into the camera to connect with the audience.  

 

Background & Lighting

Avoid bright sunlight

  • Avoid sitting in front of a window with bright sunlight shining directly into the camera. This can cause the webcam to auto-adjust and will make the presenter's face look very dark.

A solid dark background works best.

  • If you do not have a solid colored background, you can position yourself in front of a wall with a clean background (whiteboards, busy artwork, and bookcases can be distractions within your video window).
