Sit in a well-lit room

  • A well-lit room will provide for better video quality and a contrast between the background and the speaker. If you have an additional table lamp, you can use that to highlight your face. Place it at a 45-degree angle in front of you.

Audio Settings

Hear an echo?

  • If you hear an echo effect, verify that you only have one instance of the presenter console open, and you do not have the audience console open.

Check your microphone beforehand

  • You should conduct a microphone check during the technical rehearsal to check your audio levels.
  • Webcam microphones will pick up room noise and echo. Rooms with reflective surfaces like windows and hard floor surfaces are not ideal. Please try to find a location with carpet and curtains, which can deaden the room noise and improve overall audio quality. 

Logistics

Dedicate someone

  • You may consider a dedicated slide pusher so the presenters can focus on the presentation.

Practice handoffs

  • We suggest you work on the verbal handoff from presenter to presenter. Verbal cues are vital in managing these transitions.

Rehearse Q&A time

  • Also, the Q&A will need to be orchestrated so that each presenter will know when to transition his/her webcam video to answer questions. We recommend having one person moderate questions and verbally queue the questions for the appropriate presenter.

Make a backup plan

  • Coordinate a backup plan just in case you are unable to present, or you lose Internet connection during the presentation. 

Presenting on the Live Webcast

Once you have completed the technical dry run, you should log into the event 60 minutes in before the live start time, to work on the framing of your video and testing your audio.

Maintain the positioning of your video frame

  • Try not to rock in your seat or move drastically outside of your video frame.

Mute external resources

  • Turn off any cell phones, tablets, or alarms that may sound off in the middle of your presentation.

Be aware of your speaking volumes

  • If you speak softly, the audience won't be able to hear you, and if you talk too loudly, the audio will be over modulated.

Conserve bandwidth

Close all nonessential browser tabs, email, and other applications.  It's essential you reserve that bandwidth for your webcam transmission.

Review the backup plan

  • Make sure everyone knows what to do if you or your co-presenters lose connection.

Keep any other distractions at bay

Unlike audio events, with a webcam, we can see and hear what's happening in your environment. Pets, family, and friends may not realize you are presenting and could easily steal the show.

articles.name: Forums Troubleshooting
articles.title: Forums Troubleshooting
articles.body:
This article provides troubleshooting steps for connecting to Forums events, covering internet connection, supported OS and browsers, security settings, bandwidth requirements, camera and microphone permissions, sound settings, and running pre-call tests. It also addresses known issues like recording limits and screen sharing bugs on iOS/Mac Chrome. For unresolved problems, users are advised to contact ON24 support or event hosts.

  • Confirm How You're Connected
  • Security Settings
  • Bandwidth
  • Camera and Microphone Issues
  • Sound Settings
  • Running a Pre-Call Test
  • Known Issues

Confirm How You're Connected

As silly as it sounds, making sure you are connected to the internet via a strong connection is the first step to ensuring you can join an event.  You also need to use a supported operating system and browser.

As a general best practice, close all unnecessary applications and clear your cache before logging into each event.  This will improve browser performance while ensuring all recent uploads and changes are visible.

Note: The Firefox browser is not supported for the Forums experience. To ensure full functionality and platform reliability, please use the latest version of Google Chrome or Microsoft Edge.

 

Security Settings

If you meet all the system requirements, check to make sure your browser security settings are not too restrictive or block certain content.

Check to make sure your browser has access to your camera/microphone.

Also check to see if there is a security restriction enforced on your network. Some networks restrict some browser actions through VPN.  If you are on a VPN, try logging off the VPN and reconnecting. 

You may want to connect with your IT team for assistance as well, especially if you know you have heavy restrictions.  

 

Bandwidth

Check to make sure you have sufficient bandwidth to run the event, which can cause the event to lag or not run at all.  You can run a bandwidth test here. 

The following problems can contribute to having insufficient bandwidth:

  • If you are connecting through a slow connection or over a congested WiFi or cellular (mobile) network
  • You are trying to access a webinar during peak internet traffic hours
  • If you are connecting to the internet from a mobile device
  • There is heavy traffic on your company network

To optimize bandwidth, consider doing the following:

  • Upgrading to a stronger, faster, and more stable connection
  • Minimize the number of applications you are running while accessing the event
  • Try accessing the event during an off-peak internet traffic period when network congestion may have lessened

 

Camera and Microphone Issues

The browser must be able to access your camera and microphone in order to attend the event.

We recommend you close out any other applications that may use your camera or mic (eg: Teams, Photobooth, Zoom) during the Forums event. 

Your browser may ask you to grant ON24 access to your microphone and camera. 

Be sure to click Allow before closing the window.  

Once connected you will notice indicators that your microphone and webcam are being used by your browser.

Note: While virtual webcams are not blocked, they are not a part of our supported test matrix.  ON24 cannot guarantee or predict outcomes.  If issues arise, please be prepared to switch to supported physical webcams.

If you are having difficulties with this step please see these resources on how to grant web browser access to your mic and camera:
  • Microsoft
  • Apple

Check your browser settings to see if you have granted access.  Click on the arrow below if you need help checking your settings.

Check Your Browser Permissions

Chrome

Click on the browser menu, and choose Settings.  Choose Privacy and Security, then click on Site Settings and scroll down to Permissions.  Click on the arrow to make sure sites can ask to use your camera and microphone.

 

Edge

Click on the browser menu, and choose Settings.  Choose Cookies and site permissions, then scroll down to All Permissions.  Click on the arrow to make sure sites can ask to use your camera and microphone.

 

Sound Settings

You also need to make sure your headphones/speaker/microphone are not being used by another program at the same time.  To make sure your headphones/speaker/microphone can be used by the Forums event while you are in it, close out of any other programs that may have access to the devices.  

You can also update the sound settings on your computer to ensure applications cannot take exclusive control of your device. Click on the arrow for help with your sound settings.

Check Your Sound Settings
Open Settings and search for Sound Settings.  Scroll down and choose Sound Control Panel under Related Settings.

Complete the following steps in BOTH the Playback and Recording tabs.

  1. Choose the device you want to use and open Properties
  2. Click on the Advanced tab
  3. Uncheck Allow applications to take exclusive control of this device
  4. Click Apply
  5. Click OK
  6. Repeat as necessary for all devices

 

Running a Pre-Call Test

If you have a strong internet connection, are using a supported operating system and browser, you can run a pre-call test.  This test can help identify where you're having issues. 

Click on this link to open the pre-call test.  Make sure you are using the specific network/browser, camera, and microphone you want to test.  Click on the arrow below if you need help running the test.

Run a Pre-Call Test

Click on Run Test.

Allow the pre-call test to access your camera and microphone.

When the test is complete, it will let you test your camera and microphone connections, provide details on your browser compatibility, your connectivity to servers, and your expected call quality.  It will also provide steps to troubleshoot any issues.

If something on the test is blocked, reach out to your internal IT team, as there is probably a firewall/security measure in place they should be able to help with.   

 

Known Issues

Recordings of Forums Events That Run Over 4 Hours

The maximum duration for a Forums event is 4 hours.

After 4 hours, a new recording session will start and overwrite the previous recording. It is recommended to end the Forum before reaching the 4-hour mark.

If you anticipate your event will last longer than 4 hours and you need the recordings, be sure to end the Forum before the 4-hour limit and continue with a second Forum.

Known Screen Share Issue with iOS/Mac and Google Chrome Browser 

Presenters running Chrome Version 99.0.4844.74 on iOS/Mac (Big Sur 11.6 and other versions) may experience an inability to screen share in Forums (and during Live Video with screen share (Video Presenter Bridge) webinars).

To resolve this issue:

  1. Open System Preferences (users may hit CMD + Space and type “pref”)
  2. Open Security & Privacy
  3. On the left, scroll down to Screen Recording and click it.
  4. Users may need to click the lock on the bottom left to make changes and enter their credentials.
  5. Uncheck Chrome on the right.
  6. On the popup, click Quit & Reopen
  7. Re-check the Chrome box again.
  8. Click Quit & Reopen

 

 

Still Having Trouble?

If none of the troubleshooting steps in this article help, reach out for additional assistance.  

  • For those with an ON24 login - contact Support
  • For those without an ON24 login (such as attendees) - reach out to those hosting the Forum for assistance submitting a ticket on your behalf

articles.name: Key Moments Reporting
articles.title: Key Moments Reporting
articles.body:
The Key Moments report shows you the moments in each event which were the most engaging and interactive moments for your audience.

ON24 utilizes the vast amount of first-person data gathered during your webinars and forums to analyze the times your audience was most likely to react to the content, ask questions, or signal real interest in your products and services. The result is a list of moments found in your event, with special emphasis given to those moments found that were exceptionally engaging.  The Key Moments report presents the results of our Key Moments analysis in an easy-to-understand format.

Note: Reach out to your Account Manager for more information on how to add this feature to your account.

  • Things to Know
  • Requirements
  • Key Moments Report
  • Summary Section
  • Grid Views
  • List View
  • Sharing Key Moments

Things To Know

  • Key Moments data is retroactive and will populate if the event met the requirements prior to the Key Moments being enabled. 
  • It may take up to 24 hours for the Key Moments analysis to appear in the event's Webcast Intelligence Report
  • On-demand audience data is not included, but AI-based text analysis can be used to generate Key Moments for an On-Demand-only webinar.
  • The Key Moments from an event can be automatically created into video clips. The automated pipeline ingests the SFA (Single File Archive) file with speaker and slide windows, creating Key Moment video projects based on the Key Moment analytics report time codes. 
    • See  the Key Moments Video Clips article for more information.

Requirements

There are two different ways Key Moments can be created, depending on the activity in your event - AI based and/or Interactions based.

 

AI Based Key Moments

If you have automated on-demand captioning and it is active for the event OR you requested the creation of Key Moments Video Clips, the platform will pull info based on the transcript of your event and map it against the Moments created in the event. 

There is no minimum attendee requirement but events must be live for at least 10 minutes with a minimum of 400 spoken words to provide enough data for AI analysis.

Note: AI-generated content is created using OpenAI's GPT technology. You retain full ownership of the information you provide and full ownership of the generated assets. ON24 and OpenAI do not use this information for anything other than the requested service.

 

Interactions Based

Even if you don't have automated on-demand captioning or request Key Moments Video Clips, Key Moments can still be created based on interactions in your live event.  The platform uses info based on reactions used, questions asked, and buying signals/CTAs clicked during the event and maps it against the Moments.

In order to create Key Moments your live event MUST have:

  • A minimum of 50 attendees
  • An active Reactions tool on the Audience Console
  • Receive at least 20 reactions, questions, and/or conversion tool clicks

 

Combination Key Moments

Key Moments can be based on both AI and Interactions.  Use the chart below to see if your event will have Key Moments created.

  Are there greater than 50 attendees in the event AND Reactions?
Yes No
Does your account have automated on-demand captioning or were Key Moments Video Clips requested? Yes AI based & Interactions based AI based only
No

Interactions based only

No Key Moments data available

 

Possible Reasons You Don't Have Any Key Moments

There are several reasons why an event may get processed successfully without any Key Moments being found. Some possible reasons include:

    • The event was not long enough
    • There were not enough attendees
    • If there is no transcript for AI-based Key Moments, and there was not enough engagement from attendees to base Key Moment processing on
    • The only Key Moments found were either the first or last moments of the event
      • First and last moments are excluded from Key Moment consideration, since they are generally just introductions or farewells, which generally do produce engagement but are not substantive highlights of an event

Key Moments Report

To create Key Moments, events are first divided into Moments based on the times the presenter moved from one slide to the next in your accompanying slide deck.  For sections that do not include slides, the report will be broken up into 2-minute blocks.  

From these Moments, ON24 analyzes all Webcast Elite and Forums events that meet the data requirements after the live event has ended and creates Key Moments, moments your attendees were most likely to react or showed the most interest in your event.  

 

Where to Find Key Moments 

Once created, Key Moments are found in the webcast or Forum event Reports URL.  From there, click on the Key Moments tab.  

If no Key Moments were found for the webinar, either because your event does not have the requisite data for analyzing Key Moments or because there were just no qualifying moments during the event, there will be no Key Moments tab in the navigation.

Summary Section

The Summary at the top of the report shows Key Moments data for each slide in your webinar.  The numbers are for the time during which that slide was displayed for your audience.

To view different moments in the Summary, use the Previous and Next arrows on either side or click on any box in the grid below to go directly to that moment, or click on the desired slide in the grid at the bottom of the page. 

Snapshot of the Slide

  • The largest image is a snapshot of the slide shown during this moment of the event
  • If no slide was shown (if a video or screen was being shared, or no slides were used), a generic image will be shown here.  

Moment Summary

  • Shows the number of interactions from your audience during the time this slide was displayed
  • Use the metrics to determine what type of engagement you are getting from your audience
  • The following are included here:
    • Questions asked by attendees using the Q&A tool
    • Clicks on Conversion tools
    • Clicks on the Reactions tool

View Clip

  • Clicking on View Clip takes you directly to that moment of your event so you can see what was being said during that time.
  • Note: You must register for the event using your standard registration before using the View Clip feature.

Grid Views

The Grid view displays under the slide Summary and shows all moments that were analyzed for this event.

The Grid displays the moments in chronological order, from left to right, top to bottom. Each box in the grid includes a thumbnail picture of the slide displayed during that moment (the same picture is displayed in the Summary) and the start time of the moment (duration after the start of the event when that moment began).

The Grid is color coded with a heatmap - yellow represents the least engaging parts of your event, going up through orange to deep red, representing the moments determined to be the Key Moments of this event.

Key Moments are given a gem badge, so you can easily find the most engaging and interactive parts of the event.

You can filter the Grid view using the dropdown menu to show all moments or show only Key Moments.

List View

The List View provides more data about actions that happened during a time period.  Click on the List View icon to view data for Key Moments in the event.

Note: The List View only displays data for the Key Moments found in the event.

For each Key Moment, the data table below the Summary will show:

  • Slide Thumbnail – If there was a slide showing during this time period, this column will have a thumbnail image of the slide, as shown in the Summary above.
  • Time – The timing of this moment (represented as duration from the start of the event).
  • Slide Text – The text we were able to pull from the slide that is showing during this time period.
  • Questions – The text of the first few questions that were asked during this time period. If there were more questions asked than fit in this space, clicking on the More link will take you to the Q&A report for this event, to see the full text of all questions.
  • Chat – The text of the first few chat messages that were entered during this time period. If there were more chat messages than fit in this space, clicking on the More link will take you to the Chat report for this event, to see the full text of all chat messages.
  • Buying Signals – The number of clicks on any conversion tools during this time period.
  • Like, Heart, Clap, Celebrate – The number of clicks on each of these emojis in the Reactions tool.
  • Action (Eye icon) – Clicking on the Eye icon will open this Webcast or Forum and take you directly to the time of that moment in the event so you can see what was being said during that time. This is the same as clicking on the View Clip link in the Moment Summary above

Sharing Key Moments

The Share Key Moments feature in the Key Moments report allows you to select a set of Key Moments from the event and generate embeddable HTML for those Key Moments.  

Enter titles that will be displayed along with slide thumbnails and deep links into the event. This can be used on web sites, blogs, or email reminders.  Viewers can click on the Key Moment and the media player automatically advances the event to that moment.  

  • If users have previously registered, the event launches
  • If they have not registered, users need to complete registration in order to access the Key Moment

From the Grid View, click on Share Key Moments

Check the boxes of the Key Moments you would like to include.  Add a title in the box shown on the Key Moment, then click Continue with Key Moments.

Click Copy HTML to grab the code, then embed where desired. 

Note: For use in an email, you will need to drop the HTML into your HTML source code for your email.  Make note of where the embedded HTML goes, inside the correct HTML tags  in your source.

Click Preview to see it from the audience's perspective.

articles.name: Creating and Managing Polls in Elite Studio
articles.title: Creating and Managing Polls in Elite Studio
articles.body:
Polls are a great way to capture the voice of the audience. Not only do they provide important information about attendee interests, but they also enable the audience to compare their own experiences with those of their peers attending the webinar.

Poll questions are created in Elite Studio, can be used for all present types, and are available during on-demand viewing. 

  • Things to Know
  • Creating Poll Questions
  • During the Event
  • Audience View
  • Simu-Live Tips

Things to Know

  • Only those logged into Elite Studio as Producer can add or edit poll questions.
  • Polls pushed during the lobby period will not capture results in the ON24 reporting.
  • Once a webcast is completed, poll questions should not be edited or deleted. Doing so will remove the poll results from the ON24 report.
  • Responses to all questions can be viewed in the Interactions Report after the event.

Creating Poll Questions

Once you're logged into Elite Studio as a Producer, open Setup from the Menu, then click on the Polls tab. Click Add Poll to add a question - you can create a new question or choose a question from the library.

To delete a poll, click the trash can icon.

 

Type

Choose your question type first.  Choose from one of the following: 

  • Multi-Choice Single Answer - allows viewers to select a single answer from a multiple-choice list
  • Multi-Choice Multiple Answer - allows viewers to select a multiple answers from a multiple-choice list
  • Single Answer - allows viewers to check a single checkbox to confirm they are engaged in the event
  • Free Text - allows viewers to enter a text response
  • NPS Rating - a customer satisfaction metric based on responses to questions like "How likely is it that you would recommend X?"
Display

Polls can be configured for display in the Slide Window or in a Popup Window.  See below for examples.

If you're using Popup Window,  choose a Duration for the popup from the dropdown. 

  • Choose an amount of time the popup window will display and be available for the attendee to respond to the poll.   
  • If the Duration is set to Off the popup poll question will appear on the audience console until it is either responded to or dismissed/closed by attendees.
  • You can also check Play Audio Alert to audibly notify attendees a poll has been pushed into view.
Questions and Answers

Enter your poll question and answer options (if applicable, depending on the question type chosen).  Use the Add Answer button to add additional answer options.  Use Exclude From Results if you don't the results shown to attendees.

  • Poll questions are limited to 1,200 characters, including spaces
  • Answers to multiple-choice questions are limited to 256 characters

Poll Styling

Click on Poll Styling to choose the color of the question text, answer text, and answer background. 

Use the color picker or enter a Hex Value.  This will set the colors for all poll questions in this event.

Click Image to add a background image to your poll questions.

  • Image Size - between 360x360px and 2048x2048px
  • File Type - JPEG, JPG, PNG, BMP

Once the image has been added, choose if the question is shown on the card, light or dark mode, and card opacity.

When copying an event, poll styling will copy over.  

Question Library
You can use questions you created for the Survey library in the Console Builder as poll questions.  
From the Polls tab in Elite Studio, click on Add Poll, then choose Add from Question Library.
Select the question you'd like to add.  Click on the arrow to see the answer options available for this question (if any).  Then click Add Question.
Notes:
  • You are not able to edit the questions in Elite Studio.  If needed, questions must be edited from the Survey library.
  • Anyone logged into Elite Studio as a Producer will be able to add questions from the library.  Submit a Support ticket if you want only those with Webcast Elite logins to be able to access this feature.

Once you've created your polls, they will show in the Storyboard.  Click and drag the element to its desired location.  

  • When a Multi-Choice Single Answer or Multi-Choice Multiple Answer poll is created, a Poll Question element (blue) and a Poll Results element (orange) are created in the Storyboard
    • These can moved individually
    • You can hide the Poll Results element or put it at the end of your presentation and choose not to share your poll results.
  • Free text, Single Answer, and NPS Rating polls only have a Poll Question element

All poll responses can be viewed in the Interactions Report.  

During the Event

When the event is live, polls will be pushed out to your attendees like any other element in the Storyboard.  

  • For fully live events and the live portion of Sim-2-Live events, use the Previous/Next arrows to move through your Storyboard or click on the individual element in the Storyboard to push it out
  • For Simu-live and on-demand events, polls will automatically push to attendees just like any other element in the Storyboard, based on when they were pushed during the recording

 

Previewing Poll Results

When using multiple choice poll questions, you can view the percentage of attendee responses during the live event.

When the poll question is pushed to the attendees, you can hover over the element in the Storyboard or use the Preview tool to see the results as they come in real time. 

This option is available during fully live events and the live portion of Sim-2-Live events, as long as the poll result element was not hidden in the Storyboard.

Responses to all other question types can be viewed in the Interactions Report after the event.

 

Audience View

Depending on how you choose to display your poll questions, they will display in one of two ways when pushed to the audience: 

Example audience view of Slide Window

Example audience view of Popup Window

And when using multiple choice poll questions, either single or multiple answer, you can also choose to show the poll results by pushing that element to the audience.  Results will display the same way the question did and will be displayed as a percentage of people who choose each answer.  

Simu-Live Tips

You can still use poll questions during your pre-recorded Simu-live/Sim-2-Live events or On-Demand Recordings.

The setup for the poll questions is the same, but you'll want to keep the following in mind:

  • When using poll questions during the recorded portion of your event, all poll questions and poll results that will be shared with the audience need audio recorded to them (can be recorded as silence).
    • The amount of audio you need to record depends on how long you want the element to display to the audience.
    • If showing the poll in the Slide area, we recommend at least 30 seconds of audio, so the audience will have enough time to respond
    • If pushing the poll in a popup window, less recording time is required since the poll will remain visible until the attendee responds or the question times out
    • If you don't record audio, the poll question/result will only be displayed for 10 seconds
  • For standard Simu-lives and On-Demand Recordings - we recommend hiding the poll results in the Storyboard, so they don't show to the audience, since your presenters will not be able to speak to the results in real-time.  
  • For Sim-2-Live events - we recommend moving the poll results to the end of your Storyboard, so your presenters can address any questions or concerns.

articles.name: Adding YouTube Videos to Your Storyboard
articles.title: Adding YouTube Videos to Your Storyboard
articles.body:
The URL element can be leveraged to play YouTube videos during the webinar. This page reviews the steps for setting up the URL element to play YouTube videos in a webinar.

 

Things to Know

  • When using YouTube Videos set to show in the slide area, audience members viewing on mobile devices will NOT see that video play out within the Slides engagement tool. Instead, they will see the URL link to click on to open in a new tab. Users on desktop will see the video as expected.
  • By default, Google Chrome will automatically mute the video within the Slide Area.  Attendees using Chrome will need to unmute the video using the speaker icon at the bottom left corner of the Slide View Window. 

 

Setup

Login to Elite Studio as a Producer.  

Open the Setup tool and click on the URL option.  

Paste the YouTube video URL into the text box, give the video a title, and set the Display to URL in Slide Area.  

The display should always be set to URL in Slide Area so the video will play correctly for the audience.

Note: If you are creating a pre-recorded (On Demand or Simu-live) webcast and would like to incorporate a link to a YouTube video, you will need to add silence to the media file at the same location and duration as the YouTube video. If you are recording by phone, you can simply record silence and if you are uploading a media file make sure to include the appropriate length of silence to allow the video to play to it's completion so you don't create overlapping audio.

 

Presenter View

YouTube videos will play in the Live View window in Elite Studio when the URL element is pushed.  The video will continue to play until the next Storyboard element is pushed to the audience.  Any advertisements incorporated in the YouTube video will play to the audience.  

 

Audience View

YouTube videos will play for the audience in the Slides tool on the Audience Console. 

For those watching the webcast on-demand, the video will not pause, fast forward, or rewind if the Media Player is paused during the on demand playback.

articles.name: Breakout Rooms Moderator Experience
articles.title: Breakout Rooms Moderator Experience
articles.body:
Learn how to engage in an ON24 Breakout Room as a Moderator.

 

 

Things to Know

  • Participants who join a Breakout Room set up in a webinar or Forums event via Elite Studio, or those who enter the correct moderator access code when joining, are elevated to Moderator status and have additional capabilities (when Moderator is turned on during tool setup).
  • Moderators MUST be logged in via desktop or laptop - mobile devices do not support the Moderator role.
  • A dial-in option is not available when joining a Breakout Room (joining from Elite Studio or from the audience console).
  • Someone from the presenting team must stay connected to Elite Studio while attendees enter the breakout rooms.
  • When an attendee joins the Breakout Room in a Forums event, their webcam and mic will be disconnected, and they will be prompted to set up and connect with the camera and mic within the Breakout Room. The attendee camera and mic should default to the settings used in the Forums event. When attendees rejoin the Forums event, the camera and mic will automatically reconnect with the settings used in the Forum event.
    • If more than 50 participants are in a Forums event, attendees may lose their ability to join on video if they return from a Breakout Room. The first 50 attendees to rejoin the Forums event will be prompted to connect their webcams.

 

System Requirements

As a general best practice, close all unnecessary applications and clear your cache before logging into each event.  This will improve browser performance while ensuring all recent uploads and changes are visible.

 

Moderator Menu

In addition to the functionality of a regular participant, there are additional options for Moderators that will display in the meeting header.

In the Participant List, all Moderators will have a badge displayed next to their name.

Click the gear icon to change your microphone or camera settings.

 

Participant List

From the Participant List, Moderators can take action on a participant.

Hovering over a name and selecting the three dots, or clicking anywhere on the attendee video tile, will allow Moderators to do the following:

  • Mute participant's mic
    • Moderators cannot unmute someone’s microphone
  • Turn Off Camera 
    • Moderators cannot turn on someone's camera
  • Lower Hand
  • Disable Mic
  • Allow Screen Share
  • Remove a participant from the meeting
    • Note: Removing a user from the meeting sends the user back to the main event. Removal does not prevent the user from joining the room again.
  • In addition to taking action on a specific participant, Moderators can mute the microphone or turn off the camera for all participants by hovering over the All Participants row.
Muting Camera and Microphone for Attendee

Moderators can disable/allow an attendee’s microphone.

When disabled, the attendee will not be able to unmute their microphone and they will see a message stating they cannot unmute it until permitted by the Moderator. 

When allowed, the attendee can unmute their microphone.

Note: When a Moderator changes an attendee’s microphone from disabled to allowed, the microphone will still be on mute. It is up to the attendee to unmute.

By default, all attendees’ microphones are in the allowed state at the start of a Breakout Room. If you would like all attendees’ microphones to be disabled at the start of the meeting automatically, you can configure that in the tool setup. For example, if a Moderator will begin with prepared remarks and wants to ensure there are no interruptions from attendees, set the microphones to be disabled.

Attendee Hand Raise

Participants can raise their hands by clicking the small hand button. To lower their hand, the participant clicks the same button again.

When a participant raises their hand, the border of that participant's panel will momentarily flash yellow, and a yellow hand icon will appear. Also, the raise-hand count will increase by one.

To lower a participant’s hand, moderators can click on the Participant List > Participant Name > Lower Hand.

Screen Share

To begin a screen share, click the screen share icon. Attendees will see the screen share in the main window. All meeting participants will display on the left.

Moderators who join the room through the Audience Console can grant attendees permission to share their screens, to encourage a more interactive Breakout Room.

All attendees initially have screen share disabled.

Attendees that have been given permission will see the screen share icon along the top of their screen.

Note: For iPads, Beta Screenshare is available for IOS. This feature will be enabled by request only. Please contact Support for details The ON24 Screenshare app will need to be downloaded from the app store. Screenshare is not available on Safari browsers v15 and older.

Pinning Video Stream

Pinning allows you to always display one or more video streams in the video grid, even if they are not speaking.

For example, if three people are pinned, their video will always display in the video grid and the other video slots will dynamically display based on voice activation.

To pin people, click the Pin icon next to their name in the Attendees list.

Spotlighting

Spotlighting allows you to only display those who are pinned.

For example, if three people are pinned and you enable Spotlighting, only those three people will display in the video grid.

While Spotlighting is enabled, you can add people to the video display by pinning them, or remove people from the video display by unpinning them.

To enable Spotlighting, click the Spotlighting icon in the header of the video window.

Maximize Screen

To maximize the size of the Breakout Room, click the small icon in the upper right corner of the screen. To return to the default size, click the X button or press the Escape key.

 

Recording the Breakout Room

If the room has been set to record, a three-dot menu will appear in the moderator menu to provide access to the room recording controls.  The recording of the event will be available on the Archive page.

 

Chat

Moderators can clear the chat and remove past chat messages for all participants in the room.

Note: This does not impact the logging of chat messages for reporting purposes. All chat messages will still be captured in reports.

 

End/Leave Breakout Room

Pressing End Breakout will end the Breakout Room for all participants in the room, returning everyone to the main event.

If the event has concluded when the Breakout Room ends, participants will see a 'This meeting has concluded' message instead.

Press Leave to leave the meeting.

articles.name: Connecting to Breakout Rooms as a Presenter
articles.title: Connecting to Breakout Rooms as a Presenter
articles.body:
Learn how to enter a Breakout Room as a presenter from Elite Studio.

 

Things to Know

  1. ON24 Breakouts was designed to facilitate a two-way conversation between the audience and a guest speaker, sales rep, product consultant, or other relevant subject matter expert during the live Webinar or Forums event. For this reason, we recommend you designate a separate Breakout moderator from the event Presenter/Producer team.
  2. Testing of Breakouts is not supported through the Preview URL or before the live event. We recommend creating a separate test event and rehearsing the Breakout functionality in a live event after the Start Webcast button is pressed.
  3. If joining a Breakout Room as a Moderator, you must join from a desktop or a laptop - joining via mobile device is only supported for those joining a Breakout Room as an attendee.

 

System Requirements

  • Initiating screen share is not supported on the Safari web browser. If you are a moderator who needs to screen share, use a different browser (Chrome or Edge). There is no Safari limitation for attendees viewing someone else’s screen share. It is only initiating a screen share on Safari that is not supported.
  • Firefox is not a supported browser for Breakout Rooms.
  • Presenters running Chrome Version 99.0.4844.74 on iOS/Mac (Big Sur 11.6 and other versions) may experience an inability to screen share in Breakout Rooms (see fix below).
  • If joining a Breakout Room as a Moderator, you must join from a desktop or a laptop - joining via a mobile device is only supported for those joining a Breakout Room as an attendee.

As a general best practice, close all unnecessary applications and clear your cache before logging into each event.  This will improve browser performance while ensuring all recent uploads and changes are visible.

 

Joining a Breakout from Elite Studio

When you're ready to join the Breakout Room, click on the Breakouts tool in the right panel toolbar.

All of the Breakout Rooms created for the event will show in the list - click Join next to the room you want to enter.

Note: If the room was set to only show at the end of the event, the Join button will be greyed out until the event is over.

When you join the room in Elite Studio, you will be seamlessly disconnected from the live event stream.

Update your camera, background, and/or microphone.

Click Join again to enter the room.

If the Breakout Room was configured to have a Moderator, those joining from Elite Studio will have the full functionality of a Moderator, including muting attendees and sharing a screen.

Note: No access code is required when joining through Elite Studio.

Presenters and Moderators will have badges displayed beside their names to indicate who is leading the Breakout Room session.

If no Moderator was configured, those joining from Elite Studio will have the same functionality as other attendees.

When you leave the Breakout Room, you'll be disconnected from the room and returned to Elite Studio.

Click OK to join the event again and wait for Elite Studio to reconnect.

 

Joining a Breakout Room from the Audience URL

Those that will be running Breakout Rooms but are not part of the event in Elite Studio can join from the Audience URL just like an attendee. From the Audience Console, click on the name of the Breakout Room you wish to join. Choose your microphone and camera (if desired) and decide if you want your name displayed. If joining as a Moderator, enter the access code.  Then click Join.

articles.name: Breakout Rooms Attendee Experience
articles.title: Breakout Rooms Attendee Experience
articles.body:
Learn how to engage in an ON24 Breakout Room as an Attendee.  Breakout Rooms can be used in both Webcast Elite and Forums.

 

Things to Know

  • If the Breakout Room is full, users trying to join the room will see the message: Sorry, the room is currently full.
  • For Forums Events: If you have more than 50 people in a Forum event and they join a Breakout Room, they may lose the ability to rejoin the Forum event on video. The first 50 participants who rejoin the Forum after exiting the Breakout Room will be asked to connect their webcam.
  • Breakout Rooms are only available for attendees in the live event

 

System Requirements

As a general best practice, close all unnecessary applications and clear your cache before logging into each event.  This will improve browser performance while ensuring all recent uploads and changes are visible.

 

Joining a Breakout Room

When an attendee clicks the Join button within the Breakout Room tool, they are led to a panel to specify various connection settings prior to entering the room.

Camera

Use the dropdown to choose your camera, or set it to None if you want the camera off.

Once your webcam is connected, click the person icon in the webcam area to choose a virtual background or to blur the background.

Note: The blur background feature is not available on Safari.
Mic

Choose your microphone source.

Click the microphone icon to toggle between being muted or unmuted upon entering the room.

Note: Moderators have full control over when Attendees can unmute and be heard in the Breakout Room. If a microphone is currently disabled, the message "You can not unmute until a moderator gives you permission" will show below the microphone icon.
Input Volume

After specifying a microphone source, adjust the input volume.
Status

If the status is Connected, you will be able to enter the room.

If the status is Disconnected, there is a problem with the connection to the room.
Troubleshooting Tips

Access a page of troubleshooting tips if the status is Disconnected.
Display My Name

When a room is configured to show Attendees’ names, attendees can toggle Display My Name off. If this is off, you will show as “Participant” followed by a number to other attendees.

Note: Moderators will still have access to real names.

Access Code

This is where the Attendee and Moderator access code is entered (if configured).

If neither Attendee access code nor Moderator access code has been configured, these fields will be hidden.
Join As

Choose to join the Breakout Room as an Attendee or Moderator. An access code is required for Moderators when this role is toggled on.
This Session May Be Recorded

This checkbox is presented to Attendees so that they can provide their consent to be recorded. See below for more details about providing consent to be recorded.

Cancel and Resume Event

 Click this button to return back to the main event.

Join

Click this button to enter the room.

 

Providing Consent to Be Recorded

When a room has been configured to be recordable, attendees need to provide their consent to be recorded.

Depending on how the room was configured, there are two ways consent may be provided:

Configuration 1

On the Join screen, users cannot join the room until they check the box to indicate consent.

Configuration 2

No checkbox will be presented when joining. When a recording is started, attendees are shown the following message

 

Engaging in the Breakout Room

When in a Breakout Room, all other console tools become hidden. Conceptually, you have left the main event and entered a separate room.

In the Breakout Room, participants are shown in a grid layout. People that are actively speaking will have a highlight around their box.

The grid layout automatically adjusts as more people join. The grid layout and chat tool are resizable.

Participants that are actively speaking appear in the main grid area. If a person in the bottom row starts speaking, they will be promoted to the main grid area and take the place of a person who has not spoken recently.

Presenters and Moderators will always have badges displayed beside their names to indicate who is leading the Breakout Room session.

To maximize the size of the Breakout Room, click the small icon in the upper right corner of the screen. To return to the default size, click the X button or press the Escape key.

When a screen is shared, attendees will see the screen share in the main grid area. Participants will be shown in the bottom row.

In the example, a Moderator is sharing their screen (showing a clock). There are nine participants in the room and only one has their webcam turned on.

If enabled, Moderators who have joined through the Audience Console can grant permission to attendees to share their screens.

All attendees have screen share initially disabled.

Attendees that have been given permission will see the screen share icon at the top of their screen.

Note: For iPads, Beta Screenshare is available for IOS. This feature will be enabled by request only. Please contact Support for details The ON24 Screenshare app will need to be downloaded from the app store. Screenshare is not available on Safari browsers v15 and older.

If the chat has been enabled, the chat component will be shown to the right of the grid.

 

Attendee Menu

  • Participant List: Click to see a list of the participants in the room
  • Raise/Lower Hand: An attendee can raise a hand in a Breakout Room session by clicking the small hand button in the Attendee menu. An attendee can lower their hand by clicking on the same hand button.
  • Settings: Click to change your webcam or microphone
  • Camera ON/OFF: Click the camera icon to toggle the camera on or off.
  • Microphone ON/OFF: Click the microphone icon to toggle between being muted or unmuted.
    • Note: Moderators have full control over when attendees can unmute and be heard in the Breakout Room - if a microphone is currently disabled, the message, "You can not unmute until a moderator permits you" will show below the microphone icon.
  •  Leave: Leave the room and return to the main event

Many of the same Engagement Tools added to the event console can optionally be included in the Breakout Room. The functionality of the tools will be the same as they are in the event.

 

The Forums Process

When an attendee leaves a Forums event to join a Breakout Room, they're automatically disconnected from the Forums event so they may connect in the Breakout Room. Upon returning to the Forums event, the camera/mic should automatically reconnect with the same settings that were set before joining the Breakout Room.

If you have more than 50 people in a Forums event and they join a Breakout Room, they may lose the ability to rejoin the Forums event on video. The first 50 participants who rejoin the Forums event after exiting the Breakout Room will be asked to connect their webcam.

articles.name: Using QR Codes to Check In for Hybrid Events
articles.title: Using QR Codes to Check In for Hybrid Events
articles.body:
Make it easy for your registrants to check in at an in-person event by providing a QR code in their email confirmations when they register for Hybrid events.  Badges can be printed at home or scanned from the email link.  Alternatively, if a registrant needs assistance, their QR code can also be found in the Webcast Intelligence report. This QR code can be scanned by your team at the venue to mark attendees as having attended a specific event, providing you with accurate in-person attendance data.

Note: Submit a support ticket to turn on the ability to scan badges at the venue.
 

Things to Know

  • The Badge Scanner for venue staff will list all recent events marked as Hybrid in Webcast Elite. 
  • QR Codes are unique to every registrant and event pairing. If a registrant registers for multiple events within the same Webcast Elite account, they will receive a unique QR code for each event.
    • That said, the QR codes are interoperable within the same account - if a user registers for Event 1 and receives a QR code, they can use that code to register for Event 2 (as long as Event 2 is in the same Webcast Elite account) on-site at the event.
    • QR codes are NOT interoperable across sub-accounts. A QR code will only work for events within the same account where the QR code was created from.
  • QR Codes do not expire.
  • In order for the event to display within the dropdown the below two items must be true:
    • Event start date/time must not be  greater than 12 hours in the past
    • Event start date/time must not be greater than 7 day into the future
  • Find more information on setting up email confirmations with the QR code in the Hybrid Mode article

 

Report Access for QR Codes

If a registrant needs help on-site locating their QR code,  you can access their QR code directly through the Audience tab in the Webcast Intelligence Report.

The Hybrid QR Code column appears before the registrant’s name. Click the QR code icon or link to open the registrant’s personalized QR code.

Note: The column is not included in Excel exports it is visible only in the platform.

 

Checking in Registrants

Provide your venue team the Badge Scanner URL.  The Badge Scanner URL will be found with the other event URLs for Hybrid events. 

Pull up the URL on a mobile device, select the event from the dropdown, then click Start Scanning.  The app must be allowed access to the device's camera.

At the event, registrants will show the QR code in their confirmation email to your venue team.

The QR code can be printed out or scanned from the email.  If a registrant is unable to locate their code, access their code from the Audience tab in the Webcast Intelligence Report.

Note: Use the email token during event setup to provide registrants with the QR code.

Using the device's rear camera, the scanner app will read the QR code and check the registrant in.

Notifications on the screen will let you know if a registrant is successfully checked in, if they have already checked in using this QR code, or if the code is invalid.

You can also see the event name and an attendee counter.

Click on the counter to pull up a list of those that have been checked in to the event using the current device.  Use the search bar to quickly find a registrant.

A full list of registrants who checked in with a QR code will be available in the Webcast Intelligence report.

To change the event you are scanning, click on the browser's back button to return to the dropdown screen.

articles.name: Broadcast Video Encoding Guide
articles.title: Broadcast Video Encoding Guide
articles.body:
The Broadcast Video option allows users to incorporate HD quality video into Live webcasts. The Broadcast Video option requires external encoding gear to encode the video file at your location before sending the encoded stream to the ON24 platform. The recommended encoders include Wirecast, OBS, VMix, and Elemental, running on machine platforms consistent with those supported by the software. Two such machines, each publishing separate streams, are recommended for redundancy during your webinar.

Note: The Broadcast Video option is available at an additional cost and will need to be enabled for your account. Contact your ON24 Account Executive for more information.

  • Overview
  • Step 1
  • Step 2
  • Step 3
  • Step 4
  • Best Practices and Notes

Overview - Streaming Signal Flow to ON24 Ingress Servers

At a venue or auditorium, this would be a common setup. Note that A/V switching and encoding happens onsite.

Step 1 - Create Your Event

First, create a new event by choosing Broadcast Video as the Event Type on the Overview tab.

Once you create a new event with the Broadcast Video present type, Webcast Elite will provide the URL and Stream ID.

The URL and Stream ID need to be inputted into your encoder. For your convenience, Elite offers a copy option for both. You can also download the XML file to open in your encoder.

  • Encoder A is required and must be used in order for the webinar archive to process correctly.
  • Encoder B is for the backup encoder, it's optional, but playlists will assume it is present.
    • As a best practice, it's essential to have a backup encoder set up to avoid a single-point-of-failure.

Notes:

  • A Broadcast Video stream must be active for rehearsals or testing features such as polls, highlighting/centering engagement tools, etc. Otherwise, these features will not display.
  • The URL and Stream ID are case-sensitive. Please input exactly what is provided. Even a space at the beginning or the end will cause it to fail.
  • While ON24 systems record the streams for on-demand use, we recommend your have your encoder save an insurance copy.



 

Sample Output Settings Using Wirecast

Step 2 - Set Up Your Encoders

The encoders will require an un-proxied outbound connection on TCP port 1935; no proxied transport is supported for the encoded stream transport to the ON24 Ingress server.

RTMPS ingress is supported if your encoder supports it (Elemental and OBS are known to support it). The inbound RTMP stream is received at Ingress servers, then routed to transcoders for HLS (desktop and mobile) streams. The output rates associated with the transcoders are configurable; the "normal" rate is 300k+2500k video for HLS.

The specs below assume that you will furnish inbound streams at a higher quality than the output speeds.

720p
1080p

Note: Make sure that account settings are configured for this option.

Sample Wirecast Encoder Settings

Step 3 - Previewing the Encoded Video

Previewing the encoded video is recommended before the live webcast to check that your encoder is working properly.

To preview your encoder, start the encoder then use the Preview URL in Elite to open the preview console.

It is possible to view the B side streams - to verify that they are active and routed correctly - by appending "&streamtest=y" without the quotation marks to the preview link. This will cause the preview console to display a console that lists the available playlist entries. The "Primary" entries are A side streams, backup entries are B side streams. HTML5 streams are labeled Live Streams, and Hive or Kollective streams are appropriately labeled.

HLS streams are those seen on the desktop. Metadata is displayed when the streams are actually playing - the view above is not playing a stream. The bit rate associated with the Audio and Video is reported; it changes as the adjustable bit rate functionality (ABR) causes video speeds to step up or down. 

Tip: The video media window in the audience console defaults to a 16:9 ratio. If you are using 4:3 video, be sure to go to the Console Builder and change the size of the video window to match the 16:9 ratio. 

Step 4 - Running the Live Webcast

When it comes time for the live webcast, the following timeline is required:

  1. Start your encoder before audience lobby open time.  
    • The audience will be able to see the event console 15 minutes before the event start time, so you should start the encoder one hour before this to allow time for problems to be resolved if any arise.
    • If the encoder is started after the audience console is launched, the audience will need to refresh their console (F5 for Windows / Command + R for Macs) for the encoded video stream to appear.
    • If you need assistance at the event start, contact the Event Emergency Line.
  2. Click the Start Live button in Elite Studio when ready to begin the webcast, and then click the End Webcast button when the webcast is over.
    • Clicking these buttons will enable the auto-archive process.
    • The system starts recording at Start Live and stops the recording at End Webcast.
  3. Start recording for a local archive.
    • We recommend creating an "insurance copy" of the encoded video as a backup.

Best Practices and Notes

The specs provided here are supported by ON24 processing systems, downstream transcoders for mobile devices, and HLS outputs, CDNs, etc.

If you are knowledgeable, feel free to modify them as you think appropriate. If you need advice as to whether a given change is likely to cause trouble, please submit a case to Platform Support asking whether it matters; we'll try to get you a prompt answer.

If you are new, you can safely use the suggestions, as is. Please inspect the downloadable Wirecast configurations, as version changes can cause nonsensical configurations.

Constant Bit Rate streams should always be used. In Wirecast, check the Strict Constant Bitrate box.

For Platform / technical support options, contact Support.

Note: Custom Lobby video is not supported for Broadcast video event types.

And see the ON24 Streaming Architecture article to find additional information for well-informed decision making.  You may need to understand the way streams are received and transcoded to transform them from the RTMP input to HLS outputs (desktop and mobile).

Quality and Bitrates

Bandwidth requirements are a function of several issues:

  • Motion on the video- If the speaker is standing still behind a podium, there is much less video load than if they are pacing on a stage. Action in the background (as opposed to curtains, walls, etc.) increases the video load.
  • The frame rate directly determines the need for bandwidth. Frame rates less than 24fps allow the eye to see "choppy video" where there is motion. 24fps or greater, and the eye can't see the changes between frames. A non-expert user should never configure for greater than 30 fps. Screen sharing (static images of the entire desktop) is usually done at 10-12 frames per second, trading smooth motion for high resolution of the relatively static screen image.
  • If you are very bandwidth-challenged, configure for 300kbps and 15 fps, and ask the speaker to minimize his movement.
  • ON24 HLS streams utilize 2-second segments, each of which requires a keyframe. Always configure the encoder to provide a "keyframe every" rate equal to 2 times the fps. So a keyframe every 30 frames for a 15 fps video, or every 60 frames for a 30fps video. For Elemental encoders, specify a GOP of 2 seconds.
  • In situations where a video clip (720 or 1280p) is used to show a demo of a computer application - where the content shows is a computer UI with lots of text needing high resolution for the audience to be able to see it - the following is normally a viable solution. Set the frame rate to 15, the bit rate to 2500, and the width/height to 1280/720. The audience will normally enlarge their media player to something close to full screen, so a slower Frame Rate with a larger image is a viable compromise for a good quality viewing experience.
  • Always use Constant Bit Rate (in Wirecast, check the "Strict Constant Bit Rate" box), never Variable Bit Rate encoding. VBR can generate very large spikes in bandwidth demand. For bandwidth challenged devices (mobile users are frequently bandwidth challenged), this can cause audio/video sync problems.
Get a Head Start 
Dealing with any issues that show upon starting the encoders takes time, so we recommend that you start early and test thoroughly.
Encoders may be up and running an hour before the lobby opens. This allows time for troubleshooting the setup, prior to the normal presenters "run through".
You should absolutely use the Preview URL and evaluate the audience facing outputs. The fact that everything in the room looks good does not mean that the encoder output will produce outputs through ON24 systems. Check and verify!
A and B Streams are Essential
The playlists generated by ON24 systems provide at two (2) CDNs with A and B streams for each. These playlists will be used whether or not you encode a B stream. In the "no B stream" case, if the user rolls from the initial A stream to a fallback stream, they'll roll through two absent B streams (~20-30 seconds each) before resuming play on an A side stream. Network congestion can cause the  A side to roll to B, as can an A side encoder failure. In both cases, the audience experience will be sub-optimal due to the absent B stream.
A Word About Encoder Setup Errors
The single most common error seen is scrambling the B side encoder setup. The A encoder setup will show up (or not!) in the audience console if there is an error. The B side streams are only seen if the A stream fails. It is a good practice to set up both, then stop the A side stream with both running, to verify that the B side shows up. The most common setup error is to use rtmp:/fmseosa.on24.com/liveeosa as the URL for both A and B. The B side is rtmp://fmseosb.on24.com/liveeosb. The stream name for the A stream and B stream are identical except for the final character. If you push two identical streams as A and B streams, the ingress server will refuse the second instance of the A name. But if you push the B stream to the A ingress server, by using rtmp//fmseosa.on24.com/liveeosa, the names are different and the ingress server will not complain, but there will be no B stream. B stream must go through B ingress; they are isolated to avoid a single point of failure. Activate the Preview interface and verify that both A and B streams are seen.
Un-interruptible Power
It is a good idea to connect at least one of your encoders to an uninterrupted power supply. Having a laptop eliminates this need, as it'll run through a power outage. If the room goes down, do you have "technical difficulties" slides to push to the audience? It is a good idea to provide such slides in the slide deck, as well for the encoders. If encoders are not operating, the slides will not flip, as the media streams carry the prompts for the slide flips.
Investigate Firewalls
Run tests before your Live events, to ensure that there isn’t a firewall, proxy server/content filter configuration in your location that would block your stream from being published.
RTMP over TCP 1935 must be allowed unproxied outbound to 199.83.45.71 and 199.83.45.72, and 199.83.44.226 and 199.83.44.227.
Avoid WiFi, 3G, LTE,  and other Wireless Networks 
Wireless and Mobile Networks can often promise fast connections but are also prone to drops and congestion. Do not run a live event from anything but a hard-wired connection. If you do not have an unproxied hard-wired point of access, try to set A and B on different wireless nets - one on a mobile hotspot, one on WiFi, for example. And one should very actively avoid using WiFi if it is not dedicated to your encoder. Imagine when the in-room audience arrives with their laptops, mobile devices, etc. saturating the WiFi net.
Constant Bit Rate vs. Variable Bit Rate 

In a CBR file, the output bit rate is generally the same - in a much tighter range of values. In a VBR file, the reported bit rate is averaged out over the file, so some parts can potentially have low data rates and some really high data rates.

What happens in a VBR file is that during encoding, the encoder looks at the video file and does determinations to render out each frame at the bit rate (data rate) it thinks is optimal. Unfortunately, this can fluctuate wildly. During areas of extreme motion, rapid content shifts/transitions, if there are certain patterns or color combinations on screen, the encoder will greatly increase the data rate, in order to improve the quality of the video. Because it's looking to average out the data rate over the whole file,  this can cause wild data rate fluctuations, which can in turn cause major buffering issues. A lot of VBR files will playback just fine, but it’s a gamble.

Important: Please note that sometimes a file that is actually CBR will register as a VBR file. We create CBR files in Adobe Media Encoder that sometimes read in the metadata as VBR files because there is a small variance in the encoding data rate throughout. If this is happening to the client, they can safely ignore that.

The most important thing is that there are no wild fluctuations in the bit rate of the file. The easiest way to see that is to use a VLC player to playback a file, then look at TOOLS > CODEC INFORMATION > Statistics tab. In the Input/Read Section you will see “Content Bitrate” and you will notice the numbers changing throughout while playing back the file. If that number goes above 2200 kb/s a lot, you are going to have issues.

Supported Streaming Bit Rates and Per Stream Dimensions

Please use stream dimensions from the table below. Under no circumstances should either height or width be an odd number - such a configuration has been known to cause problems, especially for mobile devices. Also, using Stereo audio is pointless, because the normal stream processing methods convert the audio to mono in the final step before distribution to the audience. Also, instances have been seen where stereo audio will generate exactly inverse Right and Left channels; when combined, these cancel each other out, yielding a silent audio stream.

Please note, the "Output Stream Dimensions" column refers to the encoded stream output, not to the room set up or input stream. All audio should be sampled at 44.1 or 48kHz. 

The default data rate for HLS streams (desktop and mobile) are 300/2500kbps video, plus 64k audio served as Adjustable Bit Rate (ABR) streams.  These are final delivery rates, not the bit rate at which streams are encoded.

articles.name: Onsite Producer/Encoder Tech Checklist for Broadcast Video
articles.title: Onsite Producer/Encoder Tech Checklist for Broadcast Video
articles.body:
Review the Onsite Event Producer or Encoder Tech checklist with timeline and steps to setup encoders for a live Broadcast Video event.

 

120 Minutes Before Live Show Starts:

  • Set up A&B encoder laptops and make sure the internet connection is working. (Venue should always be tested at least 1 week prior to the event day.)
  • If you cannot connect the encoder to the network/ or stream over port 1935: Run preliminary network tests and verify ports are open. Alert the local IT contact to assist.
  • Crew should provide HD-SDI program feed with embedded audio.  Distribute to encoders and verify output stream settings are entered correctly.
  • Start “A”&”B” encoders and monitor from the preview URL (“A” stream is seen on the Preview URL)
  • Check CDNs to verify streams are reaching both A & B servers (type in &streamtest=y at the end of the preview URL and refresh). See “Stream Test Tool” doc for more info.  A and B are referred to as Primary and Backup on the streamtest tool entries
  • Work with the on-site crew to conduct a sync / clap test (use closeup of someone on stage)

 

90 Minutes Before Live Show Starts:

After verifying A/B server feeds:

  • View the preview URL and double check A/V quality for:
  • Picture quality. Make sure it is bright (not washed out) and that the colors look accurate. Skin tones should look natural.
  • Make sure the audio levels are loud without being over modulated. You should be able to hear comfortably with the level control on your preview computer set at the halfway mark.
  • Open Presenter UI on separate laptop & Click through presentation to verify slides are flipping (if applicable).
  • Confirm with client that the PPT deck you have is the most up to date version

 

60 Minutes Before Live Show Starts:

  • One hour before show time, restart the encoders. They should now stay engaged through the end of the webcast. Please wait at least 30 seconds between stopping and restarting your encoders. This allows servers to process the disconnect before you try to reconnect.
  • Check A&B server throughput (stream test tool). If you see RTMP streams, but no MPD streams, restart the encoder - again, be careful to leave it down for 30-60 seconds before restarting.
  • Do a final clap test to verify sync.  Have presenter speak into their mic.  Music is not an accurate measure to determine if the audio is at the right level.
  • Have an off-site location confirm streams look good to them, including Mobile. Mobile streams are now active whenever the encoders are active.

 

15 Minutes Before Live Show Starts:

  • Note: The lobby opens 15 minutes before the show starts. Online attendees will see and hear whatever is being sent over the encoders.
  • At this time, make sure you send only the show logo/music or lobby video loop
  • Make sure the 1st slide appears in the slide window

 

5 Minutes Before Live Show Starts – Live Webcast:

*Begin Local Backup Recording

  • Select “Start live” in Presenter UI
  • Push 1st Slide
  • Take A/V Camera Feed when program is ready to begin
  • Follow along with the slides and push polls, poll results, and survey if applicable

 

Immediately Following the Live Webcast:

  • Hit “Stop Live” in Presenter UI. Verify ON24 archive can be cropped in the ON24 platform.
  • Stop local encoders and backup recording. Upload backup encoder files to FTP or Dropbox and send a note to relevant parties alerting them to where the file is located.

articles.name: Take Action Tool
articles.title: Take Action Tool
articles.body:
The Take Action engagement tool allows you to invite your viewers to continue their content journey with you.  For example, your live event viewers can choose to register for upcoming events automatically, avoiding the registration page, or you can send them directly to your ON24 Engagement Hub to view more content. You can choose to direct them to external content (like surveys), or chat rooms with Bots, Sales, or Product staff to follow up.

Add the tools from the Tools Manager of Console Builder tab in your event.  Click the gear icon to open Configuration.

  • Things to Know
  • Display Options
  • Button Configuration
  • Styling Options
  • Library
  • Reporting

Things to Know

  • The size of the tool on the Console will be based on how it was set up - attendees will not be able to resize the tool when they view it.
  • Any Take Action tool set to display post-live will not display during live events, but you can use multiple instances of the tool if you want some to be available during the webinar.
  • The Take Action tool and the Take Action by Segment tool are similar but separate tools
    • If you have Segment Builder and would like to direct certain segments to different destinations, see the article on using the Take Action by Segment tool here.  

Display Options

Take Action tools can be set to automatically display after a live event (including Simu-live). 

  • Check the Show automatically on webcast end box if you want the tool to automatically appear in the post-live console - the tool will not show in the Menu Dock
  • If the box is unchecked, the tool will be available to the audience during the event, in the Menu Dock.
    • Note: On-Demand viewers will only be able to see the tool if this box is UNchecked.  

If one or more of the Take Action engagement tools is set to Show automatically on webcast end, then the audience console will continue to stay open after the event ends to display the tool.  The post-live audience console will remain open as long as your audience is interacting with it.

 

Pro Tips 

  • The viewing minutes and interactions will be counted in the live event report
  • If a redirect is set up for the live event, this will happen after the post-live console closes
  • If you are using both Survey and Take Action tools, the Survey will show as an overlay on top of the Take Action tools at the end of the event.  Once the Survey has been completed or dismissed, the Take Action tools will be revealed.   

Button Configuration

Select an Action.  Choosing the Custom option will allow users to specify a specific destination that will be presented to ALL attendees.

Select a Type.  Choose from Classic, Button Only, or Text + Button. 

Note: The Button Only option is only available when using Top Navigation. Set your Menu Type to Top Navigation first before setting the Take Action tool to Button Only.

If you choose AI Recommended, the ON24 recommendation engine will use AI to analyze the engagement data for each attendee including other webinars they've viewed, what resources they've downloaded, etc., to come up with a list of topics each attendee has shown an interest in.

Note: Changes to the AI Recommended configuration should be done at least 2 hours prior to the start time of the webinar to ensure that the recommendation engine has time to analyze the appropriate data and come up with relevant recommendations.  Last-minute changes may not be reflected in the tool.

Use the Fixed Destination dropdown to choose one of the following:

 

AI Recommended Content   

The Artificial Intelligence (AI) recommended content option allows the Take Action tool to provide personalized webinar recommendations to each attendee.

The ON24 recommendation engine analyzes the engagement data for each attendee (including other events they've viewed, what resources they've downloaded, etc.) to come up with a list of topics each attendee has shown an interest in.  The Take Action tool will then populate with one event from your account that will be relevant to each attendee.  It is a personalized recommendation, so Attendee 1 could be recommended Webinar X, while Attendee 2 could be recommended Webinar Y.

By presenting each attendee with a webinar that more closely matches their interests, attendees are more likely to click on and consume another piece of your content and continue their journey towards becoming a buyer.

 

How does it work? 

The recommendation engine has built-in logic to avoid poor recommendations:

  • It will not recommend a webinar that the attendee has already registered for or viewed.   
  • Only webinars that have more than 50 registrants (for future webinars) or 50 attendees (for past webinars) will be recommended. This removes the possibility of recommending test webinars or niche webinars.
  • The recommendation engine will factor in the age of the webinar.  More recent webinars will get a relevance boost, compared to older webinars.  

AI Recommended Content Configuration 

Choose AI Recommended Content under Action.

Under Filter by Time, specify whether you want the recommendation engine to recommend all events, future webinars only, or past webinars only.

Filter by Tags allows you to control which events are recommended to attendees.  Using tags limits the set of webinars that the recommendation engine can recommend to attendees.

The tags you can select from will be based on the tags added to your webinars on the Overview page. 

You'll also need to choose a Logical Operator for Tags:

  • AND - recommended event must have all the specified tags
  • OR - recommended event must have at least 1 of the specified tags

You are required to specify a Fallback Option.  There will be scenarios where the recommendation engine is not able to come up with a good recommendation for an attendee.

This could happen if none of the events being considered match the attendee’s interests.  It could also happen if there is a relevant recommendation, but the attendee has already registered for or viewed it.  Defining a fallback option ensures that even in those scenarios, the Take Action tool will have something to display.

Set the fallback option to be a Webcast, Engagement Hub, or Target.  Those options will correctly populate the token.  If you choose URL or Email, the token will be rendered to attendees as blank.

Attendees will be seamlessly registered for the recommended webinar after they click on the button in the Take Action tool.

Within the Text field add the #DYNAMICCONTENTITLE# token.   This token represents the title of the event the recommendation engine returns. 

When an attendee sees the Take Action tool, the token will populate the title of the recommended webinar.  The image, text, background color, button text, and color can all be changed to meet branding requirements. 

 

Styling

Pro Tip: The size of the Take Action engagement tool should be proportional to the console design. Testing through the Preview URL is recommended to confirm the size and layout works for the console design.

 

Background Color

Choose a background color using the color picker or a hex code.

 

Image

Upload an image to add visual appeal.  The image can be displayed as a circle or square and will be center justified. 

  • Minimum size is 120 120 px - anything smaller may appear pixelated
  • Maximum size is 4096 x 4096 px
  • Supported image file formats are JPEG, PNG, and GIF

 

Text

Use the rich text editor to choose the color, font, size, and alignment of the text

The character limit is 800

If using the AI Recommended action, a Tokens drop-down will also be available to add the dynamic content token to the text field

 

Button

Use the rich text editor to set the color, font, and size of the button label

The button will be center justified and localization logic does not apply

Note: Button text is required for the button and tool to work.

Library

A library of previously created tools can be created for easy reuse in other events.   The library is held at the account level, so all events can access the same libraries.  

To access the library, choose Open Library from the gear icon of the tool, then choose the version of the tool you want to use.

To add a version of the tool to the library, click the gear icon and choose Save to Library > Save as New.  Name the preset and click Save.  The name of the preset can be different than the name in the Attributes window

To update an existing tool in the Library with the changes you made, choose Save to Library > Update in Library.

To rename or delete a tool from the library, select Open Library and hover over any tool you would like to rename or delete.  Use the pencil icon to rename and the trash icon to delete

Note: Deleting a tool from the library only deletes it from the library - it does not delete the tool from any webinars using it. To delete existing tools from a webinar, navigate to that event’s audience console, and delete the tool.

Reporting

The Call to Action tab provides a graphical representation of the total amount of clicks for all of the Take Action tools used.  Clicking the pie chart will load a detailed report that displays each attendee’s name, company, the number of times they click on the CTA engagement tool, and an email address.  

Important: When Take Action Tools are set to show during live webinars, attendees who click on a Take Action tool must leave the audience console open for two minutes after clicking for the analytics to record the action. If an attendee leaves the webinar before the required two-minute period, or if the event ends before the end of the two-minute period, the click will not be recorded in reporting.  If the Take Action Tool is set to show at the end of the webinar, the click is recorded immediately.

articles.name: Streaming a Zoom Meeting into a Live Webcast using Broadcast Video
articles.title: Streaming a Zoom Meeting into a Live Webcast using Broadcast Video
articles.body:
